exercise_name: 3 Easy Workout Tips for Better Results

videos.description:
3 Easy Workout Tips for Better Results!

1 - During Chest Press, turn your dumbbells to a diagonal and think “elbows down”, rather than “elbows out”. You’ll get more range of motion and strain your shoulders less, which reduces injury risk!

2 - During Bent Over Rows, try to think about pulling with your elbows rather than pulling with your hands. It sounds like it wouldn’t do much, but doing this will help you better use your back muscles rather than favoring the weaker arm muscles!

3 - When training triceps, think “elbows in” to better challenge the muscle and get more out of each rep. Only let your elbows go out of it hurts your elbows to keep the weights in!

Hope these help! Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#fitnesstips #fitnessmotivation #workouttips #fatloss #orangetheoryfitness #homeworkout

exercise_name: 3 TIPS I USED FOR my PR 5:24 2000m row
coaching_cues: Focus on the lean forward, bending your legs as much as you can
The first 500m should feel SUSPICIOUSLY EASY… Re-assess your pacing at the 1000m mark and THEN BRING MORE if you conserved too much

videos.description:
3 TIPS I USED FOR my PR 5:24 2000m row!

1 - Remember that this is a LONG ROW no matter how fast or “slow” you are. This row is going to take 5 and a half minutes MINIMUM… for most, 7-9 minutes. Think about when you start the row really hard and fast… can you keep that for 7-9 minutes? OF COURSE YOU CAN’T SO DON’T DO THAT!

2 - Row long. You need as much LENGTH in each stroke as possible to keep you efficient for 7-9 minutes. Focus on the lean forward, bending your legs as much as you can. YES, YOUR HEELS CAN LIFT AND PROBABLY SHOULD! Heels planted is ideal for short intervals or if you have a large midsection that would prevent you from keeping your lean forward if you bent your legs more.

3 - Start off more conservative than you think you should. You can ALWAYS BRING MORE AT THE END but you CANNOT get back burning out the start! So don’t f*** yourself over by going crazy at the start because your adrenaline is high!

The first 500m should feel SUSPICIOUSLY EASY… Re-assess your pacing at the 1000m mark and THEN BRING MORE if you conserved too much. Most likely, you’ll have gone just hard enough to keep your pushing the same intensity for the rest of the row.

If you are immature and go really hard at the start, you’re gonna be f***ed for that final 500 meters - and that’s your own doing! I’M WARNING YOU NOW!!

BONUS TIP: THIS ROW IS GONNA BE HARD!! It’s going to be hard no matter what!! The sooner you accept that, the better mindset you’ll be able to cultivate to get after it and crush this row!! 

Good luck, get after it! NOW, DO IT!

Love always, 
Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rower #rowingmachine #erg #rowingrelated #orangetheoryfitness #2000m #tallguys

3 TIPS TO RUN FASTER:

Tip 1 - Focus less on tryin to take “bigger strides” and focus MORE on trying to “pick up your feet faster and higher”.

This idea of “pulling your feet” while falling forward from your hips is what helps you run fast without over-striding. You’ll be able to land your feet more under your center of mass, helping you keep up with the fast speed and feel more confident with it!

Tip 2 - Use your arms more! It might look goofy to “pump your arms”, but when your arms move, your legs move too! Bend your arms to 90 degrees and PUMP those arms to help you attack those fast speeds!

Tip 3 - Psych yourself up. Seriously. This is NEEDED to break through plateaus and actually run your fastest ever! What are you going to do? Fly off the treadmill? REALLY. YOU’RE GONNA BE THAT PERSON??

No.

You’re not going to fly off - because you’re going to attack your speed with control, focus, and you’re gonna pick up those damn feet and pump those arms. You fall off the treadmill when you get lazy. Don’t get lazy. Psych up and GO!!

You go this… NOW, DO IT!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#run #runner #running #runningworkout #treadmill #treadmillworkout #speed #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: 3 Tips You NEED for Better Split Squats
coaching_cues: Wherever your foot goes, that’s how far out it should be
If it’s not touching and you can’t get down there, you need to stop using weights and mastery the range with your bodyweight first

videos.description:
3 Tips You NEED for Better Split Squats!

Tip 1 is to simplify your set up! Simply sit on your bench and extend your leg. Wherever your foot goes, that’s how far out it should be! Now, there is no more guessing if you are standing too close or too far.

Tip 2 is to NOT USE WEIGHTS... at least at first! That’s because MOST people who use weights right away don’t achieve the full range of motion! You want your knee to touch the ground at the bottom of each rep. If it’s not touching and you can’t get down there, you need to stop using weights and mastery the range with your bodyweight first! You have your whole life to use heavy dumbbells... so, do it RIGHT first!

Tip 3 is to think about “dropping the back knee down” in order to get the full range! If you only focus on “bending the front leg”, it’s going to feel awkward and you literally can’t get as down low as you need to!

I hope these 3 tips can help you out and make this exercise a little less daunting! Give them a try and let me know how it goes!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#splitsquat #legday #quads #gluteworkout #glutesworkout #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #tallfitness #veganfitness

coaching_cues: Everyone wants to un-alive at that point, so take a moment to know that it will come, and that should help you mentally push through

videos.description:
500m Row SUUUUCCCCKKKSSS!!

It’s a long sprint. And it’s painful! 😂 BUT you can still have a good strategy and crush it!!

First, don’t go CRAZY at the start! A couple hard strokes, then SETTLE to a pace goal! Look at your old time, and set a goal for 0:01 faster this time. Hold that number!!

Don’t keep your rate too slow… you want 30 SPM or more here!!

How do you keep the high rate with power? Focus on the LEAN & SWING! This happens when you try to keep your heels and while really trying to lean and reach to the front each stroke! Use your abs to help you lean!

Get ready to hit THE WALL at 180m to go! Cuz it’s coming!!! You gotta push through. Everyone wants to un-alive at that point, so take a moment to know that it will come, and that should help you mentally push through!

Now, DO IT!!!!!! 🤘

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #rowingworkout #rowingispassion #500m #orangetheoryfitness #hyrox #crossfit

exercise_name: 9 Out of 10 People Do Step-Ups Wrong
coaching_cues: You should be switching at the bottom to get the most out of the exercise - not at the top

videos.description:
9 Out of 10 People Do Step-Ups Wrong!

And that’s because 9 out of 10 people aren’t thinking about WHEN they are switching their feet!

And most end up switching where it feels “natural”, which is on TOP of the bench! But this isn’t ideal..

And that’s because when you switch your feet at the top, you’re working one leg on the way up, and the opposite leg on the way down - robbing your working leg of a full concentric and eccentric contraction! You don’t get a true full rep!!

You should be switching at the bottom to get the most out of the exercise - not at the top.

And an easy way to remember to do this is to simply “toe tap” your non-working leg on top of the bench rather than fully step it up. This will remind you to keep the SAME LEG working all the way up and all the way down!

Give this a shot! You got this!! Now, DO IT!
 Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#fitnesstips #workouttips #stepup #legday #legworkout #legsworkout #legs #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit

coaching_cues: And when it comes to building muscle, getting stronger, and staying injury free, we want to maximize a SAFE FULL-RANGE

videos.description:
99% make this TRX Mistake...

And that’s trying to start push-ups / chest press at “THE TOP” of the movement!

It seems like the intuitive thing to do, but the tricky thing with TRX is that they are so unstable, it’s hard to gauge exactly how challenging it’s going to be!

And when it comes to building muscle, getting stronger, and staying injury free, we want to maximize a SAFE FULL-RANGE!

And MOST people don’t even come close to the full range of motion on the TRX chest press...

If you instead start the movement “at the bottom”, but walking forward, getting the handles situated under your shoulders, THEN stepping back to load it, and THEN pressing to the top - you’ll be able to safely get the full range while feeling confidently challenged!

It seems strange, I know... but you gotta give it a try if you haven’t before!

You’re going to feel so much more confident in your body, and with every rep, when you START AT THE BOTTOM!

Give it a shot! Now, do it!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#trx #trxtraining #trxworkout #pushups #chestday #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #fitnesstips

exercise_name: A CLASSIC example of “good form” that’s actually BAD
coaching_cues: Just because the return sequence of rowing is “arms-core-legs” DOES NOT MEAN YOU NEED TO BRING YOUR ARMS STRAIGHT OUT
Instead of “straight out”, you should be RELAXING your arms “down and out”
This keeps the proper sequencing (arms first) while keeping your shoulder engagement to a minimum

videos.description:
A CLASSIC example of “good form” that’s actually BAD!

Just because the return sequence of rowing is “arms-core-legs” DOES NOT MEAN YOU NEED TO BRING YOUR ARMS STRAIGHT OUT!!

Moving your arms straight out from your body involves your shoulders wayyyy too much and makes your shoulders and arms the first thing to get tired when you row - which we want to avoid!

Instead of “straight out”, you should be RELAXING your arms “down and out”. This keeps the proper sequencing (arms first) while keeping your shoulder engagement to a minimum!

Watch the pros row… they don’t ever carry their arms straight out (unless it’s a max effort short sprint. That’s a different story)! 

So, be like the rowing pros and practice RELAXING your arms down and out rather than bringing them straight out! 👍 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rowing #rowform #rowingform #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #rower #rowingrelated

exercise_name: A lot of people can’t “feel it in their legs” when they row…
coaching_cues: You’ll know this is you if you also find your torso simply “collapsing down” as you drive your legs
This should keep your torso in the forward position WITHOUT collapsing down, and you’ll REALLY start to feel your quads work

videos.description:
A lot of people can’t “feel it in their legs” when they row…

And this is because you’re doing “legs first”, but you’re pushing your legs down without any action happening at the HANDLE!

When the seat moves, but the handle doesn’t move, your legs aren’t doing ANYTHING! You’ll know this is you if you also find your torso simply “collapsing down” as you drive your legs!

The trick is to remember, “every inch the seat moves, the handle moves”! This ensures your legs are properly being used to power the start of each rowing stroke!

This should keep your torso in the forward position WITHOUT collapsing down, and you’ll REALLY start to feel your quads work!

This is all you have to do to feel rowing in your legs more! Don’t forget it!! 🔥

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rower #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #tallfitness #veganfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: After 12 years as a personal trainer…

videos.description:
After 12 years as a personal trainer…

I can confidently say that I still despise Lateral Lunges!

Not because they’re bad for you… they’re actually one of the BEST leg exercises in my opinion!

But because of how HARD they are!! Simply such an uncomfortable and challenging exercise.. but I’ve got some tips for you!

First, lighten the load. There is no need to load up super heavy on this exercise unless you have MASTERED the movement and have the mobility and strength to support big dumbbells.

Try 1 weight held goblet-style rather than 2 weights at your sides to reduce the load and help you keep more upright - rather than collapsing down!

Unlike a squat, where the focus is sitting your hips “back and down”, on the Lateral Lunges, focus more on simply “bending the knee”! This is a MUCH better way to think about the movement and will help you get a bigger stretch on your inner thighs while helping you get deeper in the lunge!

This move is TRULY one of the toughest legs exercises out there! But you’ve got this! Give it your best shot… now, do it! 💪

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#lunge #lunges #legs #legworkout #legsworkout #legday #otf #hellweek2025 #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness

videos.description:
Alternating Full Step Up to Knee Raise

I love that this movement takes out the confusion of which leg stays on the bench on the way down. We want to make sure members drive through the top leg and control the descent. As they get into rhythm, it is easy to start pushing off the bottom foot. It is very hard to see visually, so we just want cue “are you driving through the top leg”? “Push the top foot through the bench, stretching the leg long”. Another great one I heard (I believe from @will.liptak for a step down but regardless still works) is only place your heel on the ground, even just the cue of lifting your toes will help take drive off the bottom foot.

coaching_cues: You do not want to “stack your shoulders” or “stack your hips over your knees”

videos.description:
Are you awful at pushups? It’s time to try this…

Bench Pushups are the secret sauce ingredient to get your STRONGER at your pushups to eventually finally do one on the ground without your knees.
 Regressing to the bench isn’t something to be embarrassed about - in fact quite the opposite! It shows that you don’t have an inflated ego getting in the way of you actually making progress!

With bench pushups, the SET UP can be TRICKY. You do not want to “stack your shoulders” or “stack your hips over your knees”. This will lead to reps that simply aren’t effective for your strength and improvement!

Instead, try to remember to have your shoulders “SLIGHTLY BEHIND” the hands and your knees “slightly behind” the hips. This ensures a strong plank position!

And as you do your reps, make sure you do NOT go “straight down”. Aim yourself “down and forward” to keep your shoulders healthy and maximally work your chest muscles!

As you get better at these, you can start doing bench pushups without your knees, then graduate to the ground on your knees, and with enough time and patience (a reminder that you have decades of life left), you’ll eventually knock out your first pushup without your knees!

Now, do it!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #pushupchallenge #calisthenics #bodyweightworkout #orangetheoryfitness #homeworkout

exercise_name: As we age, we slow down…

videos.description:
As we age, we slow down…

Or at least MOST do! But keeping up with power and athleticism is what helps keep us feeling young no matter our age! 💪 

The dumbbell Snatch is a GREAT way to work on this! But the problem is that it’s such an intimidating exercise for a lot of folks!

And because of this, we end up opting for weights that are too light that won’t get us results!

To get comfortable with a HEAVY weight going up and over our heads, try the “jump and zip” method!

The idea is to use the explosive power of our legs (which are much stronger than arms) to JUMP the weight up, and using that momentum, to “zip” the weight up like a big jacket! The “zip” keeps the weight close to your center of mass to keep you strong and stable!

The jump and zip is a fantastic cue to help you conquer this tough exercise! And your body will thank you for helping it stay young because of it! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#snatch #power #age #aginginreverse #aging #athlete #athleticsm #orangetheoryfitness #homeworkout

exercise_name: Bear Plank Hold Same core muscles as the high plank, but a shorter lever

videos.description: Bear Plank Hold Same core muscles as the high plank, but a shorter lever. This shorter lever gives us the chance to really focus on the upper body and working protraction. Universally with planks members can choose how much energy they exert. There is low threshold strategies where members are using as little muscle force then they absolute need. Just the opposite would be a high threshold strategy where members feel like they are going to pass out if they hold that force for more than a minute. They can make it as easy or tough as they want.  #bearplank #bearplankhold #otf #coach #orangetheory

coaching_cues: Make sure members establish a wide base, the kneel mat can help

videos.description:
Bear Plank Switch Alternating Low Row

This movement is more about core and stabilizing the bear plank than it is about lat/pulling strength. Make sure members establish a wide base, the kneel mat can help. The further the weight moves off center with the row, the more challenging it will be. 
We can always use a high plank switch alternating low row for an option as it provides a much wider/stable base. 

#bearplankswitchalternatinglowrow #bearplanklowrow #bearlowrow #orangetheory #otfcoach

exercise_name: Behind every class is a coach growing right alongside their members

videos.description: Behind every class is a coach growing right alongside their members. 🧡🫶🏼

Bench Alternating Chest Press

Just make sure they know there will always be one dumbbell up as they alternate. If members do the opposite and have one in the down or stretch position it is going to be a ton more fatiguing. When one arm is straight they are getting some recovery, if they hold in the down position both arms are working the entire time.

videos.description: Bench Tap Squat Possible limited range of motion. Consistency in range of motion.  #otf #otfcoach #coach #benchtapsquat #rangeofmotion #squatdepth #consistencyinrange

Bent Over Rows are TRICKY!

If the weights are stuck at your sides, you’re not getting the most out of your reps…

But if you bring the weights out in front of you, you feel it more in your lower back!

So what’s the fix?

Instead of holding the weights vertically, TURN THEM horizontal when they are out in front! This reduces the distance the weights are from you, putting less strain on your back!

As you do each rep, try to twist the weight to maximize range of motion!

If you’ve been struggling to go heavy on this exercise, this might just be the tip you need! Give it a try and let me know how it goes! 💪

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘

exercise_name: Burn more calories on the rower by SLOWING DOWN
coaching_cues: To make rowing burn a lot of calories and actually feel like a good workout, you need to take strokes HARDER, and NOT row “faster”
Rowing machines generate resistance based on how much EFFORT goes into the drive of each stroke
Start rowing SLOW if you ACTUALLY want to start getting in a good workout on the rower

videos.description:
Burn more calories on the rower by SLOWING DOWN!

For those that aren’t experienced with using a rowing machine properly, they think that faster = more calories.

The “speed” of your rowing, aka the “stroke rate” is simply a measure of the rhythm of how many strokes you’re taking in… NOT HOW HARD YOU ARE TAKING STROKES!

To make rowing burn a lot of calories and actually feel like a good workout, you need to take strokes HARDER, and NOT row “faster”!

Rowing machines generate resistance based on how much EFFORT goes into the drive of each stroke. You could have a stroke rate of 30 strokes per minute, but if you’re not putting EFFORT into each stroke, you’re doing a whole lot of NOTHING! Hence the 700 calories per hour that happens when I just bounce up and down with no actual effort.

By slowing your stroke rate / rhythm down, you can actually get a better workout on the rower because you can focus on your form, but more importantly, putting more EFFORT into each stroke! That’s why you can see that at 20 strokes per minute, I can burn 2 times as many calories because I’m actually TRYING HARD each stroke - even though the rate is lower!

Can you generate even more by rowing hard AND using a high stroke rate? Absolutely! But you can’t row well at a fast stroke rate until you learn how to row well at a low stroke rate, first!

Start rowing SLOW if you ACTUALLY want to start getting in a good workout on the rower! 💪 Questions? Ask them!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rower #rowingmachine #row #rowingrelated #erg @concept2inc #concept2 #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: We cue a flat foot land in the burpee but if they do not want to elevate using a bench, what options can we give them?

videos.description:
I seriously wouldn’t even BOTHER doing Burpees if you’re not going to take the time to GO WIDE with your feet.

Simply going up and down WITHOUT thinking about your feet placement brings more risk than rewards to this exercise - and I’d just pass it up.

When your feet aren’t wide, you’re simply forced to round your back as you go down, which is just asking for an accidental strain. (P.S. rounding your spine isn’t inherently BAD, but it is when you’re not intentionally trying to train spine rounding). It’s unathletic and isn’t going to make you better at anything. Plus, it’ll make you SLOWER, meaning you won’t be able to get in as many reps.

If you take the 0.1 seconds it takes to go WIDE with your feet, the risk of accidental injury goes down to practically ZERO! Wide feet allow you to squat down each rep - and that’s infinitely better than just rounding down each rep.

Think “Wide-Together-Wide” each rep! Your feet CAN come together but do it as you jump to the plank - NOT when you jump back up!

NOW DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#burpee #burpees #bosu #bosuballworkout #cardio #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: Try to keep your feet WIDE

videos.description:
Burpees are no fun.. but burpees with a @bosu_fitness ??

Super tough!! How do you make these more manageable? It’s all about the feet! 🦶

That’s right! Your STANCE is the biggest determining factor to make these either manageable or straight up AWFUL!

Try to keep your feet WIDE! This allows you to squat in and out of your reps, which is a much stronger and more athletic position than just folding down like you would if your feet were close together!

Plus, when your feet are close and you have to curl down each rep, you’re way more prone to injuring your lower back! But when your feet are wide, you can keep a neutral spine when you squat! 💪 This helps keep you injury-free!

You can continue to despise burpees or you can learn to feel confident with them… but it really does come down to the feet!! Hope this help! Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.

exercise_name: Burpees “chest to floor”… to flop or not to flop
coaching_cues: If you want to use burpees to build STRENGTH and ATHLETICISM, you’ll need to slow things down a bit and take out the flop

videos.description:
Burpees “chest to floor”… to flop or not to flop?

Most would say flopping in and out of the pushup is ineffective… but honestly, CONTEXT matters!

If the goal is to get in reps quickly for competition, then flopping is 1000% going to be the fastest and most efficient way to get your reps done! But let’s be real: it’s not going to make you STRONGER or really help you make any gains outside of raising your heart rate.

If you want to use burpees to build STRENGTH and ATHLETICISM, you’ll need to slow things down a bit and take out the flop.

To make your burpees more effective, start by taking your feet wide! This makes it more effective to squat down and prevents back rounding and folding over each rep.

Then, for the pushup portion, try “knees on the ground” and aim down and slightly forward each rep to help you build actual strength in your pushups!

Which version is right for you? That’s for you to decide what’s important! Context matters!

Now do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#burpee #burpees #burpeechallenge #bodyweightworkout #calisthenics #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: You WILL build strength by controlling the way down

videos.description:
Can’t Do a Pull-Up? Here’s the formula…

1️⃣ Start off with Bar Hangs! Sounds too easy, I know, but THIS is the foundation of building pull-up strength! Literally just hanging!

2️⃣ After some weeks of practice, you move on to Scapular Retractions! This is where you start to really use your “pull-up muscles”, focusing on pulling your shoulder blades down, holding, and building strength in that contracted position!

3️⃣ Then, you move into Eccentric Pull-ups! This means using a chair/bench/something to get yourself to the top of the pull-up, holding your scapular retraction, then SLOWLY lowering down! You WILL build strength by controlling the way down! (Aka the eccentric).

4️⃣ Then, it’s time for assisted pull-ups! Use a bench/chair/something to put your leg on, then use as little/much effort in the leg as you need to to “get up” for the pull-up, then, release the leg and work on your eccentric on the way down!

5️⃣ After a few months of all of this work, you’ll be ready to start cranking out your first pull-up! This strength takes A LOT of time and patience, so don’t try to rush the process!

And of course, there are other ways to work towards pull-ups, but I like this strategy the most! What do you think!? Can you do a pull-up??

⭐️ Be sure to follow for more daily fitness tips like this!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#pullup #pullups #pullupchallenge #calisthenics #plantpower

exercise_name: Can’t do pushups without your knees still

videos.description:
Can’t do pushups without your knees still?

SO MANY find that knee pushups are no problem but can’t get anywhere close to doing a single pushup without the knees!

So how do you bridge the gap and FINALLY get your first full pushup?

The trick is to use the “in-between” pushup! This method involves using your knees ONLY on the way up.

But on the way DOWN, you go “no knees” and slowly lower yourself down to a 3-4 second count. This builds eccentric strength that will make your pushups stronger over time and will prove to make your pushups much more challenging than knee pushups alone!

Start doing sets of these “in-between” pushups like you would train your knee pushups and notice how much stronger you get after just a few weeks!

Soon enough, you’ll bridge the gap and have the strength to do pushups without your knees! Now, do it!!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #pushupchallenge #calisthenics #homeworkout #pushuptips #bodyweightworkout

coaching_cues: Bottom of a chest press, lats engaged, shoulder blades packed, and neck long
And usually, it leads to reduced range of motion and a lack of body control throughout

videos.description: Reciprocating Chest Press, Seated Overhead Triceps Extension, and Triceps Push Ups Timed Block
Tomorrow we see a close grip chest press via cluster set, but all set up cues apply when doing any heavy chest press. Let’s take advantage of that stretch reflex and load members up appropriately.
We ask for a slight tuck of the elbows or a 45° angle in chest presses and push ups… but do you know why?

Stabilizing the 11 & 1 Clock Press Important to get a good stable base in the bottom of a TRX Clock Press before even attempting to move the arms overhead. Bottom of a chest press, lats engaged, shoulder blades packed, and neck long.  #otf #coach #otfcoach #11&1clockpress #strapclockpress #trxclockpress #trx11and1clockpress #strap11and1clockpress #clockpress

Reciprocating Ipsilateral Bicycle Press This is one we have to be super clear in the demo so members aren’t staring at the screen trying to figure out which leg is bent/extended. It might even be helpful to use the rehearse method: “so is it same said straight or opposite side straight?”  It is also helpful to start off with both the arms and the legs bent, then chowing the same side to straighten up. Last starting the reciprocating process.  If the need we, we can always plant the feet and have them focus on the reciprocating of the chest press solo. #otf #coach #otfcoach #reciprocatingipsilateralbicyclepress #reciprocating

Floor Bridge Hold with Single Arm Close Grip Chest Press The technique may take a little to get comfortable with. There are a few advantages of working single arm at a time: time to focus on symmetrical strength, challenge core balance, use two hands to position and support dumbbell… We are also close to the ground, so a great opportunity for member to try something different/heavier. If members feel unstable we can always do this as a single arm floor press.  #bridgeholdwithsinglearmclosegripchestpress #floorbridgehold #floorbridgeholdwithsinglearmclosegripchestpress #singlearmfloorpress #otf

Chest Press Set Up for a Drop Set/Reverse Drop Set We want to minimize the transition time when we switch weights with both drop sets and reverse drop sets. Having two sets of dumbbells by your feet can be cumbersome and possibly even dangerous. If space allows, it is helpful to set up on both sides of the bench to maximize space and efficiency in dumbbell weight changes when working heavy.  #dropsets #reversedropsets #reversedropsetup #chestpress #otfcoach

The BIGGEST mistake people make on the TRX is trying to start the exercise from the “top”.

You really have no idea what the challenge is going to look like when you set up this way! And usually, it leads to reduced range of motion and a lack of body control throughout.

The incredible “trick” is to start the exercise at the BOTTOM of the range of motion, in the stretched position. For the TRX Chest Press, like in this video example, it allows you to feel the fully stretched position at the bottom of each rep and allows you to set your feet either further towards or further away from the anchor point. Once you’ve found your footing, you can push to the top!

And all of this lets you find the perfect amount of challenge for each rep while feeling confident and in control with full range of motion EVERY TIME!

Seriously… it’s time you tried starting at the bottom for TRX workouts! It makes a big difference in the BEST way!

Now, do it!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#trx #trxworkout #trxtraining #chestpress #pushup #calisthenics #bodyweightworkout #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: Concentration Curls are done wrong ~75% of the time

videos.description:
Concentration Curls are done wrong ~75% of the time!

When we isolate one arm at a time, with our elbow into the side of our leg as we curl up, the IMMENSE challenge of truly isolating the biceps TENDS to lead to MOST people curling up the wrist as they curl the weight up.

Curling your wrist is VERY BAD with this exercise and can lead to a Golfer’s Elbow injury over time if you do it repeatedly (this injury will keep you in pain for 6 months - 1 year)!

You HAVE to make sure you DO NOT curl your wrists as you curl the weight up. So, try to think “knuckles down” as you curl to keep your wrist relaxed and keep the action just in your biceps!

Hope that makes sense and you don’t WRITE IT OFF AS SOMETHING YOU DON’T DO!

After coaching 1000s of individuals through this movement over the years, MOST PEOPLE CURL THE WRISTS UNINTENTIONALLY! So, be intentional, and RELAX the wrist!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#bicep #biceps #bicepsworkout #armday #flex #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: But we want to actively avoid this because it puts tension into the forearms unintentionally
We want to keep tension throughout the movement and KEEP it challenging at the top

videos.description:
Rows are honestly one of the best exercises to use the @trxtraining for… but SOOOO many make this subtle, but impactful mistake!

And that mistake is curling in the wrists as you pull in.

It feels natural to do when you’re first learning how to challenge yourself with the movement - plus the handles rotate! But we want to actively avoid this because it puts tension into the forearms unintentionally. 

If you make a fist while relaxing your wrists, you’ll find your knuckles naturally rest BACK behind the level of your wrists!

When we row on the TRX, think: “knuckles back” to help keep your forearms out of the equation - that way you can keep all the action on the target back muscles instead! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#trx #trxtraining #trxworkout #back #backday #backworkout #orangetheoryfitness #fitnesstips #workouttips

Elbows “in front” as you do biceps curls will definitely make the start of the movement tough! But the problem is, it makes it easy to chat and lose tension at the top!

We want to keep tension throughout the movement and KEEP it challenging at the top! We do this by simply keeping our elbows pinned down at our sides!

Subconsciously you will WANT to lift your elbows as you curl to the top, but FIGHT the urge and keep those elbows down to truly feel biceps curls work like never before! 💪 Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#bicep #biceps #arms #armworkout #armday #flex #orangetheoryfitness #orangetheory

videos.description: Encouraging Heavy Recoveries and Load Considerations in a Heavy Two Handed Single Dumbbell Deadlift

Stepping too far back on Split Stance Deadlifts is the biggest issue I see when it comes to feeling comfortable and confident with this exercise!

Instead of taking a big step back, try to step just slightly behind the heel of the leg that’s in front. This allows you to stay more upright, while still keeping the focus on one leg at a time!

You’ll feel less strain in your lower back this way & feel more confident in the movement!

A small change that makes a big difference! Give it a shot! Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rdl #deadlit #deadlifts #rdls #legday #glutes #orangetheoryfitness

What you’re doing with your weight training probably isn’t helping you see long lasting results…

If you’re just focusing on “completing your reps”, you’re probably missing out on one of the most important aspects of weight training: the loaded stretch on your muscles in the most lengthened positions of the exercise!

Basically, the more STRETCH you can get on the muscles each rep, the better results you’ll see! 

It’s not about just “doing your reps”! A philosophy like that will have you most likely doing your reps more rushed and with less focus on a full range of motion. 

Sometimes, our ego gets in the way too. We end up going heavier to show off, but again, cut the most important part of the lift short by not focusing on a big stretch each rep!!

If you’ve been in a plateau, it might be time to start focusing less on your reps and more about the loaded stretch you can get each rep! You might even need LESS WEIGHT!!

Not only is this stretch philosophy great for muscle gains, but it helps us build flexibility and joint strength, too!! Nothing but wins!! But you still gotta do it!!

Now, do it!!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#chestpress #deadlift #shoulderpress #workoutinspiraton #workoutmotivation #fitnesstips #homeworkout #orangetheoryfitness

If you’re “feeling it in your low back” during deadlifts, I’ve got 2 things to keep in mind to help you feel it less in your back and more in your glutes and hamstrings!

Tip 1 is to make sure you’re keeping the weights as CLOSE TO YOU as you can! Think of it as “shaving your legs” with the dumbbells! Every inch away from your legs the weights go, the more you’re going to feel it in your lower back!

Tip 2 is to BEND YOUR KNEES MORE! Especially if you’re not super flexible, you NEED to bend your knees more or else you’re going to round your lower back because you’re just not flexible enough to keep your back straight. When you bend your knees more, it gives you room to scoot your booty back more so you can keep a neutral spine!

If you ARE really flexible though, keep just a little knee bend and think “butt back” to keep you from rounding!

These two tips should help you feel deadlifts a lot less in your lower back and more where you SHOULD be feeling them work!

Give these tips a shot and let me know if you found it helpful! :)

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#deadlift #deadlifts #deadlifting #rdl #legday #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #tallfitness
Sumo stance deadlift: Short people problems… ok, maybe just large dumbbell/strong people problems.  #otf #orangetheory #coach #fitness #deadlift #dumbbell
Deadlifts - Keep the weights closest to the center of mass to put less pressure in the lower back.  #otf #otfcoach #coach #deadlift #orangetheory #orangetheorycoach #centerofmass

Alternating Step Out Deadlift Remind members we are looking for a symmetrical stance and a very small step out. All other things deadlift apply: keep a flat back, soft bend on the knees, keep the dumbbells close.  #alternatingstepoutdeadlift #stepoutdeadlift #deadlift #symmetry #posteriorchain

Alternating Single Leg Deadlift We want a soft knee bend and the hips to stay squared forward. One of the faults that we see often is members initiating the hinge, then lifting the leg as almost like an after thought. We want them to work the balance from the beginning, so have them lift the foot up or back before the torso hinges forward.  #alternatingsinglelegdeadlift #singlelegdeadlift #deadlift #balance #posteriorchain

If you’re really feeling your lower back during deadlifts, here’s two things you need to fix!

First, don’t let the weights go out in front of you! Even just a couple inches out in front of your body can cause an exponential amount of strain to your lower back!

We want to feel this exercise in our hamstrings and glutes. Yes, your back muscles will ALSO be active, but you don’t want your lower back to feel like the limiting factor!

Try your best to keep the weights as CLOSE AS POSSIBLE to your legs/shins. The closer, the better! It might be the simple change you need to relieve your back discomfort!

Second, you need to bend your knees a bit more! If your legs are too straight, you’re more inclined to “reach” down each rep rather than HINGE back!

Remember to think “butt back” with each rep and make sure you aren’t REACHING down! Just stick your butt back and keep the weights close... feel the stretch, and squeeze your but forward and you’re good to go!

Hope that helps and makes sense! Give it a shot and let me know how it goes!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#deadlift #deadlifts #rdl #legday #legworkout #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit

coaching_cues: Ideal we would be using weight that is tough to shoulder press so we NEED the momentum and drive from our hips

videos.description:
Dip in Push Press

It is NOT a squat to shoulder press (meaning a superficial squat, then pause). The whole point of involving the hips is either more shoulder explosive power or to work from core to smaller extremities ie our strong hips/legs into our shoulders aka a transfer of power. 

If we do this with weight that we can normally shoulder press, goal would be to move it faster. Ideal we would be using weight that is tough to shoulder press so we NEED the momentum and drive from our hips. 

The depth of the dip is only as shallow as we need. The heavier the weight or the more the fatigue, the deeper the leg drive. The dip is a very vertical movement to stack the weight correctly and channel momentum vertical.

#dipinapushpress #otfpushpress #pushpressdip #orangetheory #otfcoach

exercise_name: Do you put your “hands under” yourself when you do Leg Raises

videos.description:
Do you put your “hands under” yourself when you do Leg Raises?

A lot of people do this because leg raises bother their lower back! Instead of this, try to LIFT your head, neck, and shoulders off of the ground and “crunch up” before you start your reps!

This will active your entire midsection AND eliminate any discomfort you might feel in your lower back from the exercise!

It’s a good way to also train your neck muscles a bit, too! If you feel your neck tense up, that means it’s working!! The neck has muscles just like the rest of your body and this is an easy way to lightly train it while doing core work, too!

If you haven’t tried this before, give it a shot! It’s my go-to for leg raises EVERY TIME now!

Is this the ONLY way to modify the exercise? Not at all! But I find that it’s the simplest way to change it up, “feel it more”, and keep you safe!

What do you think about this modification?
 Love always,
 Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#core #coreworkout #corestrength #abs #absworkout #summerworkout #orangetheoryfitness #tallfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: Do you REALLY wanna get GOOD at pushups
coaching_cues: Because your butt is going to want to “table top”, and w don’t want that
Instead, try to “squeeze forward” from the glutes while aiming “down and forward” to touch down

videos.description:
Do you REALLY wanna get GOOD at pushups?

It’s never going to happen with doing reps without your knees…

There is NOTHING TO BE ASHAMED ABOUT when it comes to “knee pushups”. They are simply a necessary step towards building strength to EVENTUALLY get pushups without your knees!

But when you go to your knees, ⚠️ WATCH OUT! Because your butt is going to want to “table top”, and w don’t want that!

Instead, try to “squeeze forward” from the glutes while aiming “down and forward” to touch down! 👍 This will also prevent your elbows from flaring out- which will help prevent shoulder pain while maximizing the use of your chest muscles! 💪

With enough time, range of motion, and INTENTIONAL effort on your knees, you’ll build up the strength and confidence to do pushups without your knees!

The bench is the BEST place to start, too! 🙌 NOW, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #pushupseveryday #pushupchallenge #chest #chestday #calisthenics #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: Don’t let the reach get members too anterior when they frame the knee with weights

videos.description: Don’t let the reach get members too anterior when they frame the knee with weights. Reaching a dumbbell out will allow a better counterbalance to sit back for a greater range of motion.

DON’T USE THE ROWER! IT’S BAD FOR YOUR BACK!!

How many times have you hear that before?

The thing is.. a lot of lower back injuries DO happen on the rower. But the good news is that those injuries are TOTALLY preventable!

In fact most rowing back pain and injuries occurs because of how you SIT on the seat! If you sit on the middle of your butt, when you reach forward to the front of the stroke, your lower back unintentionally rounds, and that’s usually what causes a back strain!

Instead, try to “sit on your junk” 🥜! A funny phrase I like to use to describe the action of tilting forward on your hips, trying to sit more on your hamstrings than your a$$, to help you untuck your lower back and keep it in a neutral spine position at the front of each stroke!

And what a difference it makes… give it a shot and let me know if this helps your rowing! Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #rowingworkout #crossfit #rowingrelated #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: Dynamic Half Kneeling Low to High Chop (aka Lift)

videos.description: Dynamic Half Kneeling Low to High Chop (aka Lift)

coaching_cues: Compress your legs as much as you can while maintaining 11 o clock
Drive your legs down while HOLDING your 11 o clock position (don’t lean as you push your legs)
Your elbows are both OUT and BACK with wrists flat
The handle is pulled right below the chest (below the bra strap)
Your shoulders are relaxed and eyes and head are forward

videos.description:
EASY ROW FORM CHECKLIST:

At the END (aka “finish”) of the rowing stroke, make sure:

1. Your elbows are both OUT and BACK with wrists flat.

2. The handle is pulled right below the chest (below the bra strap).

3. Your shoulders are relaxed and eyes and head are forward.

4. Your body is leaned back at a 1 o clock angle.

On the RETURN of the stroke to come up for the next stroke, make sure:

1. You RELAX your arms down and out over your knees (don’t punch out!)

2. As you bend your knees, lean forward to 11 o clock

3. Compress your legs as much as you can while maintaining 11 o clock.

4. Extend your arms fully straight and lift your hands ever-so-slightly to eliminate slack when you take the stroke.

As you TAKE the stroke:

1. Drive your legs down while HOLDING your 11 o clock position (don’t lean as you push your legs).

2. SWING back as your legs near full straightening.

3. Pull the arms in at the very end using the momentum generated before!

There ya go! Do you hit all of these check boxes?? Hope it helps you with your rowing!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowinglife #rowingmachine #rowingrelated #erg #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: FITNESS “TIP OF THE DAYS” ARE BACK

videos.description:
FITNESS “TIP OF THE DAYS” ARE BACK! 🔥

✅ Two tips to conquer split squats:

👣 Tip 1: Sit, extend, place — instead of trying to randomly set up your stance (and probably falling over in the process), just take a seat on the bench…extend your leg out, and THAT is where your front foot goes!

Bonus: try putting the TOP of your foot on the bench rather than having your toes on the bench! 👍 This will help you get lower each rep!

🦶 Tip 2: Kick your front foot to the side slightly! Rather than standing with your feet in line like a tightrope, stepping to the side slightly makes it so much more stable and will help you feel more confident in your balance!

📰 NEWSFLASH: split squats will always be hard… just not impossible if you use these tips!!

👇 Let me know if this was helpful and tag a friend who HATES split squats! 😂 Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#legday #legworkout #legsworkout #quads #glutes #orangetheoryfitness #gymtips #workouttips #strength

exercise_name: Floor Single Arm Close Grip Chest Press
coaching_cues: Shorter range of motion should allow us to use more weight
To get the most out of it, we want to place the elbow down and pause to remove the strength reflex

videos.description:
Floor Single Arm Close Grip Chest Press

Floor presses are generally used to build lock out strength particularly at the triceps. Shorter range of motion should allow us to use more weight. To get the most out of it, we want to place the elbow down and pause to remove the strength reflex. 
Working a single arm, allows us to use both arms to get the weight set up and situated. Teach members this process early on, so even if they don’t need it now, they know and understand it if they want to challenge themselves to something obnoxiously heavy but safely.

coaching_cues: Walk them in as much as you can, and if you want to feel it even more, turn your feet out slightly

😉

These tips should help you feel bridges more in your glutes than your hamstrings

videos.description:
Trying to build your 🍑 but not feeling the Hip Thrusts / Hip Bridges / Glute Bridges?

A lot of folks feel this exercise more in the hamstrings (backs of the thighs) than in the glutes. This happens when your feet are far out in front of you! The farther out, the more hamstrings!

That means in order to feel it more in your glutes, you simply have to walk your feet closer to you! Walk them in as much as you can, and if you want to feel it even more, turn your feet out slightly! 😉

These tips should help you feel bridges more in your glutes than your hamstrings! Give it a try and let me know if you found this advice helpful!

I post daily fitness tips for all sorts of exercises, so be sure to follow for more daily workout tips like this! 💪

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘

coaching_cues: But there’s something I think you should try that you probably aren’t doing

videos.description:
Aiming to touch elbows to just below your knees is a good landmark to aim for when doing goblet squats!

Many people struggle to get anywhere close to this because of the old-school cueing of “butt back and down”… “Weight in your heels”… etc!

Following this old advice will cut your depth short and shift load more into your lower back more than needed!

Instead, try to think “butt DOWN”, and “knees forward”! This helps you keep a taller torso with a more neutral spine position!

You’ll unlock more range and feel the exercise in your legs more because of it!

Cant get low without your heels lifting? Try adding a 1-2 sec pause at the bottom of the squat just before your heels start to lift. You’ll build ankle mobility and over time, you’ll get down no problem!

Try this!! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#squat #squats #legs #legday #legsworkout #legworkout #homeworkout #glutes #gluteworkouts #orangetheoryfitness

A common complaint with group fitness like Orangetheory is that there aren’t enough heavy weights to go around.

It’s a valid complaint. But there’s something I think you should try that you probably aren’t doing.
 And that’s adding an active, intentional, flexed PAUSE in the stretched position of each of your reps. An example is a 1-2 second active pause at the bottom of a Goblet Squat.

Instead of just rushing your reps up and down and complaining that the weight isn’t heavy enough… pause at the bottom. Don’t get loose and floppy down there, but actively contract hard and eliminate the up-and-down momentum.

This will make every rep feel that much heavier, will be a bit more forgiving on your joints, AND most importantly will help you increase your mobility - which has it’s own benefits of helping you feel better, move better, and stay injury-free more often!

Mobility is often rushed or overlooked in the group fitness environment.. this is your chance to make your weights feel heavier and get MORE benefit out of each rep!

Let’s just say this will do A LOT more for you than just complaining! ;) NOW, DO IT!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#squat #squats #legday #weights #realitycheck #fitnessproblems #orangetheory #groupfitness #otf #orangetheoryfitness #mayhem

exercise_name: Got a big belly in the way
coaching_cues: ❌ These are all things we want to AVOID

videos.description:
Got a big belly in the way? 🤰 🫃

Most that have a larger midsection opt to rest their arms on their knees while barely being able to lean forward at the front of the stroke - and compensate with just bending the legs as much as possible.

❌ These are all things we want to AVOID!

Instead…

✅ Loosen the bootstraps and angle your feet/legs/knees out! This give you room to lean forward.

✅ Grip narrowly on the handle. This allows you to fully straighten your arms and get a strong connection at the front of the stroke!

✅ Focus on more lean and LESS leg bend! The power in rowing comes from the hip swing, but you need lean to make that happen!!

Hope this helps!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingispassion #rowingmachine #rowingworkout #hyrox #hyroxtraining #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #erg

Hate burpees? Here’s why…

It’s PROBABLY because you’re keeping your feet too close together!

Literally as simple as that!

If your feet are close together, it’s gonna be a BAD TIME! It’s a further distance to travel, it makes you round your back unintentionally, and it’s just not athletic!

BUT if you can do your burpees with feet WIDE, you fix all of those problems!

And it truly changes the game for burpees - making them much more efficient to do and helping you feel less demoralized by them! 💪

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘

exercise_name: Have you been told you have “tight hip flexors”
coaching_cues: To make sure the effort stays in your glutes and doesn’t become a back arch, focus on “squeezing your abs in” as you squeeze your butt up
That should do the trick

videos.description:
Have you been told you have “tight hip flexors”?

With all the sitting we do, it’s probably true!

This can make doing hip bridges or glute bridges a bit problematic- because tight hip flexors will prevent you from FULLY extending your hips to the top!

And if you want glute gains or b00ty gains, you’ve gotta really get that butt up! That means extending MORE than you think!

So, I’d encourage you to think of your hip bridges more like a “hip bridge+”, where you add just an extra inch of lift more than you think, to bust through those tight hip flexors!

To make sure the effort stays in your glutes and doesn’t become a back arch, focus on “squeezing your abs in” as you squeeze your butt up! That should do the trick!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#glute #glutes #glutebridge #bootybuilding #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: HELL WEEK DAY 1 - THE HIGH JUMP - COACH TIP:
coaching_cues: Try to step back, THEN jump up high each rep

videos.description:
HELL WEEK DAY 1 - THE HIGH JUMP - COACH TIP:

If you want to not just “survive” Hell Week but actually get some great results & training from your workouts, you gotta bring some INTENTION to a couple moves today!

For the Lateral Hop Overs, it’s all about trying to step your foot BEHIND the foot that’s on the bench! A common mistake is to keep the feet more in line with each other, which puts you on your toes and doesn’t make you very powerful! Try to step back, THEN jump up high each rep! 💪

Next, for your All Out rows, it’s NOT about trying to row fast.. it’s about JUMPING BACK with power, but taking your time on the way back up! This will help you conserve your energy to make it through the rest of the workout! 👍

Hope these tips can help you have an epic first day of Hell Week!  You got this! Now, DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#hellweek #hellweek2024 #hellweekchallenge #otf #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #fitnesstips

exercise_name: HELL WEEK DAY 2 TIP OF THE DAY
coaching_cues: Focus on moving “down and forward” each rep to protect your shoulders and keep more action in the chest

💪

Third, to control your rowing, try to think about “rowing through peanut butter”
But it make a BIG difference in your ability to row hard while controlling stroke rate down

videos.description:
HELL WEEK DAY 2 TIP OF THE DAY!

This workout is all about managing your tempo/speed! This can be really tricky with certain exercises you’ll see - so here are some tips!

First, on a squat hold lateral walk, focus more on “bending your knees” rather than trying to sit your hips back and down! Because you’re holding a weight, sitting back is gonna tilt you over more making it much harder!

Second, for pushups, don’t wreck your shoulders by going straight down and up! Focus on moving “down and forward” each rep to protect your shoulders and keep more action in the chest! 💪

Third, to control your rowing, try to think about “rowing through peanut butter”. Weird, I know. But it make a BIG difference in your ability to row hard while controlling stroke rate down! 🚣

Now, DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rower #pushups #pushup #squats #hellweek #hellweek2024 #otf #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness

HELL WEEK DAY 3 TIP OF THE DAY:

“As Fast As You Can” is a true agility & speed-focused workout!

You’ll have a run/row to do and a lot of familiar power exercises on the floor - but the “coach reactive drills” are what are going to take this workout to a new level!

To TRULY get the most out of these agility exercises, try to think about “stepping on a thumbtack” or even… a LEGO!! Something that makes you go YOUCH!!

With this simple mindset shift from just “doing the exercise” to “stepping on a Lego”, you get so much more out of each rep from these main movements:
- Plank Pops
- Speed Skaters
- Twist Hops
- Fast Feet

If you want results, don’t just “do them”, freakin’ DO THEM with that thumbtack mindset shift! 💪 Have a great workout!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#agility #hellweek #hellweek2024 #hellweekday3 #orangetheory #otf #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: Here’s EXACTLY how to PR your 200m row
coaching_cues: This is the ONLY TIME you should be consciously changing your rowing form from the usual “long and strong” form
Don’t do this if you want to have a slow 200m sprint 🤷‍♂️ The choice is yours

videos.description:
Here’s EXACTLY how to PR your 200m row!

This is the ONLY TIME you should be consciously changing your rowing form from the usual “long and strong” form!

A 200m row is so short that you are better off taking more strokes that are shorter and quicker rather than longer, stronger strokes. There’s just not enough time and we need big power FAST!

How do you do this? You keep your heels down and LEAN A LOT!

If you do not lean forward enough, your heels will lift, but they will NEVER lift if you lean forward enough! From there, it’s the same stroke sequence…

Push, swing, pull… big leg push, big swing and lay back! Then a big lean forward at the front again and repeat!!

Do this FAST by pretending you’re stepping on a thumbtack (or lego) at the front of each stroke!

Do this and you’ll PR. Don’t do this if you want to have a slow 200m sprint 🤷‍♂️ The choice is yours.

Good luck and let me know how you do!!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #tallfitness

coaching_cues: Keep in mind, that this strategy ONLY WORKS for rows that are ~30 seconds or so
Because I’m bending my legs only a little bit, I need to make sure I’m getting the most out of them by trying to press them flat every stroke

videos.description:
Here’s how I rowed a 24.5 sec 200m row:

I focused on 3 primary things in order to produce max power. Keep in mind, that this strategy ONLY WORKS for rows that are ~30 seconds or so. Anything longer than that and this type of rowing falls apart - so keep that in mind!!

First, I focus on a high stroke rate. In this video, my stroke rate is 60+. That is only sustainable for a few seconds, hence why I only go that high for a row like this. A row like 200m is too short to focus on long, strong strokes. Save those for 2000m rows!

Second, I focus on getting max power through the “lean and swing”. This means getting an exaggerated lean forward each stroke by only bending my legs just shy of 90 degree angles! This allows me to keep my heels down and lets me lean forward a lot - and then swing back with max power, too! This also helps to keep the stroke rate super high like I talked about earlier.

Third, I make sure to focus on a full leg extension each stroke. Because I’m bending my legs only a little bit, I need to make sure I’m getting the most out of them by trying to press them flat every stroke. Sometimes when trying to take fast strokes, we forget to actually straighten the legs and flex the quads each stroke!!

BONUS: sit heavy in the seat and make sure all of your effort is horizontal with no upwards pulling. If you pull up, even a little, you’ll fall off the seat (ouch).

That’s how I rowed a super fast 200m! How’s your 200m? Let me know down below! NOW, DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowingmachine #rowingrelated #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #200m

exercise_name: Hip Bridges on the ground vs
coaching_cues: Essentially, squeeze up a bit more than you THINK you should in order to work through your tight hip flexors

videos.description:
Hip Bridges on the ground vs. on a bench!

Which do YOU like best?

✅ From a physics standpoint, bridges with your shoulders on the bench would create the most perpendicular resistance against gravity compared to doing the bridge flat on the ground!

✅ Either way, think of the bridge as a “Bridge+”. Essentially, squeeze up a bit more than you THINK you should in order to work through your tight hip flexors. Just that bit extra on top of what you THINK was high enough gets you a crazy intense contraction you’ll feel right away!

👍 Just make sure you keep your midsection braced tightly to avoid excess lower back arching!

Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#hipbridge #glute #glutes #glutebridge #fitnesstips

coaching_cues: It should feel EXTRA HEAVY in your hand/arms… but that’s because all you have to do is HOLD and let your backside do the work for you

videos.description:
The Hip Hinge Swing, done with a kettlebell or a dumbbell… is NOT an exercise that uses your arms at all outside of simply holding the weight!

Subconsciously, because we have to hold such a heavy weight to ACTUALLY challenge our glutes and hamstrings, we opt for a weight that ONLY feels heavy in the hand/arm. The problem is our arms are WAY WEAKER than our glutes and hamstrings.

That’s why most people grab a 15 pound weight… or a 20… or maybe 25lbs… but that is NOT ENOUGH!! With weights that light, you’re not going to be feeling your glutes and hamstrings work at all!

I honestly think that MOST people can manage at least a 30 lb weight! It should feel EXTRA HEAVY in your hand/arms… but that’s because all you have to do is HOLD and let your backside do the work for you!

But you gotta get over this mental hurdle! It’s holding you back from making those juicy bootay gains!!

Promise me you’ll go heavier than you normally do on this exercise today, okay?? You have to believe in yourself! You’re much more capable than you give yourself credit for!

Now, do it!!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#swings #kbswing #glutes #hamstrings #gluteworkout #power #athletic #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness
Skier swing: Members tend to understand hip power in the skier swing more than a hip hinge swing. Here are two faults to keep an eye out for! #otf #orangetheory #skierswings #coach #faults

Hollow Hold

Stemming from a gymnastics hollow body hold, this isometric movement is all about the core. Squeezing the abs to form a C shaped figure. Biggest faults we see is arching in the lower back and more so rounding of the neck/c-spine. Have members concentrate on closing the space between the lower back and the floor as well as leaving the neck open where we can fit an orange under the chin. 

#hollowhold #otfhollowhold #hollowbodyhold #gymnastics #otfcoach

coaching_cues: The triceps extension we want to only move from the elbow up

videos.description:
Hollow Hold Triceps Extension

The hollow hold stems from a gymnastic shape called the hollow body position. We are seeking a core challenged position with a neutral or slight posterior tilted pelvis/lower back. The triceps extension we want to only move from the elbow up. We can cue elbow highest to the ceiling, elbow stay stacked over the arm pits, the elbow is a hinge… members often go too heavy and limit total range of motion. 

#hollowholdtricepsextension #hollowbody #tricepsextension #orangetheory #otfcoach

How does run form change?

Whether it’s flat road or 15% incline… running mechanics are running mechanics!!

It’s the same formula. Practice pulling your foot up under you (not a knee drive, not a butt kick), switch the feet while relaxing how you land, and then falling forward with gravity from your hips!

The incline will make things more challenging, but it’s still the same formula! And this is actually best PRACTICED at incline as it forces you to make less mistakes!

Don’t be afraid to slow down - because running is a FORM not a speed! So just like with your pushups, give the FORM some practice! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #running #runner #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #runninglife #treadmill #treadmillworkout

exercise_name: How I rowed a 1:06 500 meter and howo row YOUR best 500 meter:
coaching_cues: If you have a goal set, you need to get RIGHT TO THAT SPLIT TIME and hold it
Third, you have to understand that no matter how fit you are, you will WANT TO BE NOT-ALIVE when you hit the last 150 meters or so
But you MUST keep going and not give into the fatigue
KEEP LEANING AND SWINGING

videos.description:
How I rowed a 1:06 500 meter and howo row YOUR best 500 meter:

First, make sure you’re actually watching your split/500m when you row. This number will literally tell you, at your current intensity, how long your 500m will take. If you have a goal set, you need to get RIGHT TO THAT SPLIT TIME and hold it! If you don’t pay attention to it, you’re going into the unknown and are most likely just going to crash and burn on the row.

Second, it’s super important to  focus on quick, powerful strokes… because this thing is just a “sorta long sprint”. That means keeping your HEELS DOWN on the foot plates by bending your legs only to about 90 degrees and focusing mainly on getting a BIG LEAN FORWARD at the front of each stroke! If you don’t lean, you won’t be able to keep your heels down, so lean MORE than you think!

Third, you have to understand that no matter how fit you are, you will WANT TO BE NOT-ALIVE when you hit the last 150 meters or so. Physiologically, it’s your body’s break point. But you MUST keep going and not give into the fatigue. To keep your split as fast as possible, keep focusing on your BIG SWING forward and backwards. Don’t fall into the trap of “getting vertical” with your torso. That will be the end of you and you will NOT PR. KEEP LEANING AND SWINGING!!

Lastly, make sure you row THROUGH the finish line. Don’t stop with 5 meters left… you will regret it. You gotta take an EXTRA stroke more than you think to finish with your truly fastest time.
 Good luck! Let me know how your 500 meter row goes down below!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘 
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #500m #benchmark

exercise_name: How much should you bend your knees during a dumbbell deadlift
coaching_cues: So, if you’re not very flexible, you need to bend your knees MORE in order to achieve that

videos.description:
How much should you bend your knees during a dumbbell deadlift?

The problem a lot of folks find is that the cue “a slight knee bend” ends up forcing a rounded spine position simply because of a lack of flexible hamstrings.

If you’re not very flexible, a “slight knee bend” isn’t enough to help you fully hinge over with a full range of motion!

The goal is to get your torso parallel to the ground while keeping a neutral spine position. So, if you’re not very flexible, you need to bend your knees MORE in order to achieve that! The amount of bend will simply vary from person to person - because we all have different levels of flexibility!

The main takeaway is to ALWAYS think “butt back” in order to keep the neutral spine position! Never “reach down”! And when you find the knee bend that works for you and your flexibility, you’ll feel more confident and get better results from this exercise!

Give those tips a try! Hope it helps! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#deadlift #deadlifts #legs #legday #glutes #glutesworkout #legworkout #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: How the World Record Rowers SPRINT
coaching_cues: They lean forward a lot at the front of the stroke
They keep their heels down
They REACH the handle up and into the machine
They full extend their legs and lean back
They pull the handle in right below the chest, not the stomach, to get more pull distance each stroke

videos.description:
How the World Record Rowers SPRINT!

These 3 record holders do the exact same things in order to sprint with as much power as possible.

1. They lean forward a lot at the front of the stroke. Getting your torso forward is what allows you to get POWER in the swing back.

2. They keep their heels down. This allows them to take strokes quickly and keep stroke rate high.

3. They REACH the handle up and into the machine. They don’t try to keep “chest tall” or perfect posture.

4. They full extend their legs and lean back. This makes sure you’re using all your leg and hip swing power.

5. They pull the handle in right below the chest, not the stomach, to get more pull distance each stroke.

6. They keep an ever-so-slight rounding forward of the shoulders in order to help keep the stroke rate as high as possible!

If you’ve never believed me, maybe you can believe them! This is how you SPRINT ON THE ROWER!!

Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rowing #rower #rowingmachine #erg #rowingrelated #sprint #worldrecord

exercise_name: How to correct curling/swinging in the clean

videos.description: How to correct curling/swinging in the clean.  #otf #clean #coach #corrections

How to make Burpees EASY:

Bring your feet WIDE! This shortens the range of motion while allowing you to squat in and out of the burpee position!

This is INFINITELY easier to do than defaulting to “feet close” which prevents you from squatting down, forcing a longer range of motion to get to the ground in a very unathletic way!

If you really hate burpees, it’s probably because your feet are still too close together and you’re not squatting down!

If you want MORE effective reps that aren’t so daunting and exhausting in all the bad ways, just spread your stance out a bit and watch the magic happen!

Simple as that! NOW DO IT!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#burpee #burpees #burpeechallenge #cardio #calisthenics #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: How to NOT DIE During Inclines & Hills…
coaching_cues:
How to NOT DIE During Inclines & Hills…

You want to AVOID “stepping out” or stepping “forward”
If you want to run up inclines with less pain and less effort, practice “pose” running
Don’t knee drive your leg and don’t try to kick your glutes… just simply pull your foot up

videos.description:
How to NOT DIE During Inclines & Hills…

You want to AVOID “stepping out” or stepping “forward”. This is UNFORTUNATELY how most people run by default due to shoes that have big squishy heels on them. The simple fact is that running this way makes hills WAY HARDER and it’s way more impactful on your joints!!

If you want to run up inclines with less pain and less effort, practice “pose” running!

First, find your pose by pulling your foot up and under you. Don’t knee drive your leg and don’t try to kick your glutes… just simply pull your foot up.

Then, practice picking the other foot up. Notice how the first foot NATURALLY falls down and you don’t have to think about it…. Now, practice switching back and forth. This is running in place!!

Now, all you have to do, is allow gravity to help propel you forward by “falling” forward. By falling forward WHILE you switch your feet, you’re running, and especially when it comes to running hills and inclines, you’re going to be way more efficient!

This stuff take practice AS ANY EXERCISE SKILL SHOULD… but if you don’t actively work on this, you’ll never get better and inclines will ALWAYS SUCK.

My advice? Slow your speeds down to practice this form! You’ve got your whole life to go fast… so don’t let bad form prevent you from reaching your fullest potential!

Love always,

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#run #running #runner #hellweek #hellweek2024 #orangetheoryfitness #treadmill #treadmillworkout

exercise_name: How to row your BEST 1000-meter row:

videos.description:
How to row your BEST 1000-meter row:

Having a goal will be a HUGE help if you’ve already done a 500m or a 2000m row before.

Basing a goal pace off of your 500m, aim for 3-9 split seconds slower than your 500m finish time. It’ll be closer to 7-9 seconds faster if you’re a dude, and closer to 3-5 seconds if you’re a gal!

Basing it off of your 2000m can be even more accurate. Take your 2000m row split/500m average and aim for 4-8 split seconds faster.

If you’re not sure how to calculate your 500m split average, google search “erg split calculator” and plug in the numbers!

If you don’t have anything to base your row off of, you just gotta keep in mind:

✅ Long strokes
✅ 26 strokes per minute or more
✅ Be conservative at the start
✅ Sprint the last 180m with all you’ve got!

Hopefully this helps! Good thing is… EVERYONE gets a personal best the first time 😉

How’d you do?? 👇
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingrelated #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #orangetheory #otf #orangetheoryfitness #rowingmachineworkout

exercise_name: How to Run Your FASTEST MILE ✅

videos.description:
How to Run Your FASTEST MILE ✅

INTERVALS of going up and down with your speed is great for training to get faster, but are NOT great for a race day strategy to PR on the mile.

You will be much more EFFICIENT and better off trying to pick one pace, and HOLD IT throughout the duration of the mile!

When you pick your pace, be sure to make small 0.1 mph adjustments up or down throughout to keep you in the fight whether you’re feeling great or not to hot!

Save your intervals for your TRAINING and hold one steady pace for your mile!

Give it your best and let me know how it goes!!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #runner #running #mile #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: How to Snatch heavy dumbbells INSTANTLY

videos.description:
How to Snatch heavy dumbbells INSTANTLY!

The reason why you can’t get the heavy weights overhead is because you’re trying to lift the weight up with your arm too much!!

The Snatch is NOT about using your arm to lift the weight overhead. The arm is just the GUIDE.

Instead, think of the Snatch as a JUMP and ZIP! Literally squat down, hinge back slightly, and JUMP up by driving your quads!

Then, use the MOMENTUM of the jump to “zip” the weight up! That means keep it close to you, like you’re zipping up your jacket. Don’t bring the weight far out in front of you!! Just ZIP IT like an oversized coat!

Once you practice and master the “jump and zip”, you’ll be bringing weights overhead that you never thought were possible!

Hope the “jump and zip” makes sense and helps you out! Let me know if you found it helpful!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#powerlifting #weightlifting #snatch #snatches #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit #veganfitness #tallfitness #tallguys

coaching_cues: While YES you want to think “butt back” like you’re loading up for a jump, you DO NOT want to “hump forward”
Keep this in mind for your swings and you’ll be feeling a lot stronger and confident with every rep

videos.description:
HUMPING IS GREAT.. but not what you wanna do when doing a Hip Hinge Swing!

While YES you want to think “butt back” like you’re loading up for a jump, you DO NOT want to “hump forward”! This is because you’ll most likely overextend your back and increase risk of injury!

To do this PROPERLY, after you “butt back” it, think about pressing your feet DOWN into the ground HARD! This is exactly what will swing the weight forward just enough for your to maximally work your glutes without over-extending!

Keep this in mind for your swings and you’ll be feeling a lot stronger and confident with every rep! Now, do it! 💪

exercise_name: I get SO MUCH RETALIATION on this topic…
coaching_cues: Your arms and shoulders should relax DOWN on the return if you want to row as efficiently as possible

videos.description:
I get SO MUCH RETALIATION on this topic…

Here’s proof from the killer crew at @georgetown_lightweight_rowing … that’s right, ACTUAL ROWING ATHLETES!

Your arms and shoulders should relax DOWN on the return if you want to row as efficiently as possible. If you carry your arms straight out, you’re going to tire out your shoulders way before your legs and hips and your form and performance will suffer from the discomfort.

Relaxing your arms down also forces you to pass your knees before your knees bend, which is PROPER form sequencing! Just don’t forget to lift your hands back up at the catch of the next stroke! 👍 🚣‍♂️

Now do it!… like them!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowingworkout #crew #rowingrelated #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: I just KNOW you’re getting WRECKED by inclines when running…
coaching_cues:
I just KNOW you’re getting WRECKED by inclines when running…

And it’s because you need to work on your running form
You need to get your feet to land underneath you
“Pulling” your feet up does NOT mean you try to drive your knee up or kick your butt

videos.description:
I just KNOW you’re getting WRECKED by inclines when running…

And it’s because you need to work on your running form!

The thing with inclines, is that they REALLY bring your running form errors to light! 

The biggest running form mistake? Trying to “push off” of the back leg or “step forward” with each stride. Both of these things will make you SLOWER in any sort of endurance focused run! That’s because whether you step forward OR push off, you’re going to be getting your leg way out in front of you. And you ONLY move FORWARD when your legs get under, then behind you!

When your leg is in front, you can’t move forward. You need to get your feet to land underneath you! This will come from practicing the Pose Method: pull, switch, and fall!

“Pulling” your feet up does NOT mean you try to drive your knee up or kick your butt. JUST. PULL. Pull your foot up under you!

Practice doing this while switching back forth between feet as quickly, and with as little effort as you can. When you combine this with a “fall forward”, you’re running!

When you fall forward, pretend you’re being pulled up with a string attached to your head! This will prevent you from hinging forward from your hips and will allow gravity to move you forward as pull and switch your feet!

This stuff takes A TON OF PRACTICE. And you have to UN-CONDITION YOURSELF from the crappy form you’ve developed ever since you started playing around in your Nikes/Sketchers/Vans/Converse etc since you were 5 years old. Those shoes that provide cushion and support CONDITIONED you to BAD FORM!

It’s time to undo it all by dropping your ego, slowing your speeds down, and practicing!

This will help you prevent injuries and work through inclines with more confidence! Hope this helps!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #runner #running #runningtips #treadmill #treadmillworkout #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: I know we talk about it in the demos but let’s be overly clear when we talk abou

videos.description: I know we talk about it in the demos but let’s be overly clear when we talk about the two different setup positions.

I see those sneaky hands…

Hands under your booty during Leg Raises? We subconsciously do this because we don’t have the strength to keep a neutral spine as we lower our legs down, which makes our back arch excessively and creates low back pain!

One solution is to reduce the range of motion until you cant keep your spine neutral - SURE!

BUT another option is to CRUNCH UP your head, beck and shoulders, and THEN do the leg raises! This gets more of your midsection involved and virtually eliminates any back pain!

Just try to think about your hips “scooping” as you do your reps rather than just “lifting your legs”!

Give this variation a try if you haven’t already and thank me later!!

NOW DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#core #coreworkout #abs #abworkout #absworkout
