exercise_name: I will take this to my grave…

videos.description:
I will take this to my grave…

 A LOT of shoulder pressing is BS! Elbows flared out, bringing the weights down to… the ears? The forehead? It seems like when people try to record how heavy they lift, they just randomly determine how far to go down… it’s nonsense.

It’s ego lifting.. it’s leading to unnecessary injuries, and it’s not a reliable way to track reps or progress effectively.

How do we fix the situation? SIMPLY bring your elbow slightly in front of you! It doesn’t take much - but going from “flared out” to slightly in front INSTANTLY reduces injury risk to your shoulders, and allows you to hit an ACTUAL full range, which is TOUCHING the weight to your shoulder!

When you do those two things, you make the shoulder press SAFE and way more EFFECTIVE!

Enough with the nonsense!!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#shoulders #shoulderworkout #weightraining #mobility #orangetheoryfitness #bodybuilding

exercise_name: If it HURTS your back doing deadlifts…
coaching_cues: Keep those weights as close as you can by “shaving” with them to keep you at minimal risk for a lower back strain

videos.description:
If it HURTS your back doing deadlifts…

“Shaving your legs” 🪒 is one of the BEST cues to help you reduce the back strain your feel during deadlifts!

Because every inch away from our body the weights are when we do the deadlift, the more strain we feel on our lower back and the less we feel it where we SHOULD - the hamstrings and glutes!

Keep those weights as close as you can by “shaving” with them to keep you at minimal risk for a lower back strain!

Oh, and don’t forget to think “butt back” to help you hinge over. Never “reach down” with the deadlift!

Butt back…
Shave the legs…
Don’t reach…

That’s all you need for some effective RDL / dumbbell deadlift action! Hope this helps! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#deadlift #deadlifts #rdls #legday #legworkout #legs #legsworkout #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: If it hurts your knees to lunge…

videos.description:
If it hurts your knees to lunge…

First, try to step back FURTHER than you think.

The more you step back, the less shearing force you’ll feel on your knee joint!

If that doesn’t help, ditch the weights.

Go bodyweight.. and if that still hurts? Use a chair or @trxtraining to help you to reduce your body weight!

From there, you find your pain-free level of lunge ability.

Then, it’s just having the patience to be consistent and SLOWLY build yourself up in intensity! To body weight… light weights… heavier… etc!

It all starts with the big step back! Give it a try!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#lunge #lunges #leg #legs #legday #legworkout #legsworkout #quads #kneepain

exercise_name: If you CAN’T do a full push up, but knee pushups are too easy…
coaching_cues: Keeping GOOD form is key as you bridge the gap

videos.description:
If you CAN’T do a full push up, but knee pushups are too easy…

You gotta start taking bench pushups SERIOUSLY. This is THE WAY to close the gap!

It’s all about bench height… the higher the bench, the easier the push-up gets & the lower it is to the ground, the harder the push-up gets!

Keeping GOOD form is key as you bridge the gap! That starts with ✅ screwing your hands into the bench to create stability tension in you shoulder joint!

Then, make sure each rep you aim “down and slightly forward” to help guide your elbows back, rather than glare them out!

Start with the high bench, and once you’re repping 6+ reps confidently, lower that bench and keep practicing!

Don’t forget that all of this takes TIME and PATIENCE! But this is the way!! 💪 Give it a shot! Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #pushupchallenge #chest #chestday #plantpower #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: If you have BACK PAIN from rowing…

videos.description:
If you have BACK PAIN from rowing…

🤔 It might be from how you’re sitting!

When we sit where it’s most “comfortable” on the rower, we tend to end up with your pelvis/hips tucked under us.

❌ This exaggerates rounding in the lower back, which can increase your risk of a back strain!

✅ The fix is to “untuck” your hips by trying to sit more of your hamstrings on the seat, rather than your glutes.

This can be a little bit more uncomfortable initially, BUT it helps keep your spine in a more neutral position!

🔥 This helps you get MORE length each stroke all while protecting your lower back more!

If you’re dealing with rowing back pain, take a moment to see how you’re sitting and try this adjustment to see if it helps! 👍

Join the Just Row community with hundreds of following along workouts & coaching by me! 💪

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #rowingispassion #rowingworkout #hyrox #crossfit #orangetheory #rowingrelated

exercise_name: If you’re bad at pushups and want to get better, start at the BOTTOM
coaching_cues: If you’re bad at pushups and want to get better, start at the BOTTOM

videos.description:
If you’re bad at pushups and want to get better, start at the BOTTOM!

Most of us default to starting pushups at the “top” of the movement. This can work for some, but for a lot of folks who can’t crank out a bunch of pushups (or even 1 full pushup without knee assistance), starting at the top of the movement might not be the best idea.

If you start by lying on the floor, you actually give yourself a fighting chance to have great form and a full range of motion each rep!

The key thing here is make sure that you place your hands LOWER than the line of your shoulders! If your hands are too high, you’re going increase your chance of a STRAIN IN YOUR SHOULDERS and probably develop shoulder issues over time…

Proper pushupss require your elbows to guide “back” rather than “out”! That’s why starting on the floor is so great… you can place your hands BELOW the line of the shoulders, which naturally puts your elbows in the correct place.

From there, use your knees to help you out if you need to. You can even regress to a bench, with the same type of start, and build up to your knees over time.

If you’re working on pushups without your knees, this is still a GREAT place to start, because again, it emphasizes PROPER form, and prevents you from doing “ego reps”.

Start your pushups from the bottom rather than the top to help get your pushups to the next level!

Love always,

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #pushday #chest #chestday #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #homeworkout

exercise_name: If you’re sitting on YOUR NICE CUSHY BUM, you’re messing up
coaching_cues: Sure, it isn’t as comfortable, but it’s a SAFER and STRONGER position and a NON-NEGOTIABLE if you want to row well and pain-free
Keep this in mind for your most optimal rowing

videos.description:
If you’re sitting on YOUR NICE CUSHY BUM, you’re messing up!!

When you row, if you sit where it’s the most comfy, you’re most likely tucking your pelvis underneath you, which is going to round your lower back as you row.
 When your lower back is rounded, you’re just ASKING for a low back strain when using the rowing machine. AND it will prevent you from getting maximum length in your stroke, and the most power out of your body SWING after you push your legs.

And ALL YOU HAVE TO DO TO FIX THIS, is take 2 SECONDS to try to “untuck your lower back” by sitting more on your HAMSTRINGS than your glutes!

Sure, it isn’t as comfortable, but it’s a SAFER and STRONGER position and a NON-NEGOTIABLE if you want to row well and pain-free!

Next time you sit down to row, YOU BETTER NOT “JUST SIT”! Take those couple seconds to sit more on your hamstrings and untuck your lower back, and you’re going to be ready to crush ANY ROW that comes your way!!

Keep this in mind for your most optimal rowing! NOW DO IT!  Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rower #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #rowingworkout #tallfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: In Launch, we talk about preseving the stimulus of the movement

videos.description: In Launch, we talk about preseving the stimulus of the movement.  When we break down the Arnold press, what makes it unique is the rotational aspect. If members are limited to no overhead movements, we can preserve the stimulus by using a front raise to a lateral raise.  We still target the front delt to start, use a rotational aspect to shift it to the medial delt all the while keeping the members safe without going overhead.  #otf #orangetheory #coach #options #shoulders

exercise_name: In rowing you want to use your LEGS first
coaching_cues: In rowing you want to use your LEGS first
Keep this in mind to make sure your rowing stays effective, strong, and efficient

videos.description:
In rowing you want to use your LEGS first!

But it doesn’t work if you just “press your legs down”.

If the seat moves back, but the handle doesn’t move and your body collapses forward, you’re doing what we call in the rowing world as “shooting the slide”, which results in ZERO leg power!!

In order to TRULY use your legs first, you have to make sure that “every inch the seat moves, the handle moves”! This means your torso does NOT collapse down, and you feel strong tension in your legs as you PUSH back each stroke!

Keep this in mind to make sure your rowing stays effective, strong, and efficient! NOW DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rowinglife #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #hyrox #orangetheoryfitness #rowingrelated

exercise_name: Incline Chest Press to Alternating Incline Chest Press

videos.description: Incline Chest Press to Alternating Incline Chest Press Superset
Incline Chest Press to Alternating Incline Chest Press #otf #coach #otfcoach #inclinechestpress #alternatingchestpress #chest #superset

Incline Chest Press vs. “Normal” Chest Press 💪

With Incline, you’ll feel WEAKER than you would if the bench was flat! That’s because the incline uses mostly your upper chest and shoulders compared to the flat bench which uses more of your chest (bigger and stronger than shoulders)!

❌With EITHER variation, don’t make the mistake of flaring your elbows out to the sides!

✅ Turn your dumbbells diagonally and allow your elbows to drop DOWN, rather than out, to get a strong and safe range of motion every rep!

🤔 Which do you prefer? Incline or Flat? Let me know!

Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.

exercise_name: Ipsilateral Single Arm Split Stance Deadlift
coaching_cues: Keep the knee over the ankle with a mostly vertical shin; keep an eye out for a straight leg with a negative shin angle
Keep the hips square and the dumbbell close

videos.description:
Ipsilateral Single Arm Split Stance Deadlift

In any b-stance/split stance deadlift we want to keep majority of weight in the front leg so it helps to take a shallow step back with the toe planted. If members step back too far, most end up putting too much weight in the back leg. Keep the knee over the ankle with a mostly vertical shin; keep an eye out for a straight leg with a negative shin angle. Keep the hips square and the dumbbell close.

Remind members, this is ipsilateral. Usually I cue dumbbell fills the empty space but this one “which ever hand has the dumbbell, the same leg is forward”.

#ipsilateralsinglearmsplitstancedeadlift #ipsilateralbstancedeadlift #bstance #deadlifts #otfcoach

coaching_cues: The problem is, you don’t want to overdo too much too quickly
Try my 1, 2, bounce technique to help you control the movement and make it more manageable than hopping as fast as possible

videos.description:
Is age just a number?

While certain life circumstances can slow us down as we age, there’s plenty we can do to feel young!

Working on your explosive athleticism can help keep your body feeling young! The problem is, you don’t want to overdo too much too quickly!

The Bench Hop Over is a super accessible way to train your athleticism! You can try to hop over the bench right away, but if that seems like too much or is too intimidating, you can scoot down to the edge of the bench and practice without fear of tripping!

Try my 1, 2, bounce technique to help you control the movement and make it more manageable than hopping as fast as possible!

Moral of the story: scaling back athletic movements is the FIRST step to building your athleticism even as you age! 💪 You’re never too old!

Love always,

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#stayyoung #orangetheoryfitness #athlete #calisthenics #bodyweightworkout #homeworkout #workoutinspiration #workoutmotivation

exercise_name: It’s easy for coaches to overcomplicate things…

videos.description:
It’s easy for coaches to overcomplicate things…

For the deadlift especially, there are a lot of cues you COULD give… but in a group class setting, there’s no time to get into the details! Here’s what I recommend:

✅ Shoulders down

✅ Abs tight

✅ Slight knee bend

✅ BUTT BACK!

The “butt back” is the biggest takeaway from this! It’s a great cue to help folks who are untrained or unfamiliar! Sometimes when time is short, this is the only cue you’ve got in a mass setting! Give it a try! Now, do it! 💪

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#deadlift #deadlifts #fitnesstips #workouttips #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: It’s official… training your biceps on an incline bench is BETTER

videos.description:
It’s official… training your biceps on an incline bench is BETTER!

You get more range, more stretch at the bottom, and you’re more stable!

But you’re only gonna make the BEST gains if you don’t let your natural tendencies take over…

And that means making sure your elbows do NOT sneak forward!! They’ll move forward if you aren’t thinking in order to help you “rest” at the top of each rep!

BUT if you actively think about keeping your elbows “slightly back”, this won’t be an issue! And you’ll feel this exercise work from top to bottom, and where it matters most!

Seems like a tip you wouldn’t think you’d need, but I’ve watched 1000s do this wrong time and time again!! So don’t be like so many others and use that brain power to keep those elbows BACK!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#biceps #bicep #armworkout #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #tallfitness #veganfitness #strengthtraining

exercise_name: Keeping squats as safe and effective as possible starts with the FEET
coaching_cues: Keeping squats as safe and effective as possible starts with the FEET
It’s all about grounding down with a stable base to align your knees, thighs, and fully engage your hips to keep things strong and smooth

videos.description:
Keeping squats as safe and effective as possible starts with the FEET!

And it’s not just about “having the right stance”! It’s all about grounding down with a stable base to align your knees, thighs, and fully engage your hips to keep things strong and smooth!

If we don’t do this, we open up the possibility for injuries to the knees or hips!!

✅ That’s why “screwing in your feet” is my favorite cue for this! It does the job of creating a strong arch, which helps to allow your knees to track over and in line with your feet, while taking out slack from the hip joint to keep your glutes doing their job!

Take the 2 seconds that’s it takes to “screw your feet” into the ground - and you’ll start having the BEST squats of your life! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#squat #squats #squatchallenge #legday #legworkout #legsworkout #legs #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: We want to really watch this position from the front view

videos.description: Bent Arm Lateral Raise We see lateral raises often so this bent arm position provides some variance. It also reduces the stress on the wrist and less variability with the position of the elbow.  We want to really watch this position from the front view. To maximize the effectiveness, we want the hands directly in front of the elbows. Often members are going to bring the hands inward. This reduces the overall load brining the weight in from the end of the lever. While we can always use it as an option, to get the most out of this hands would be in directly in front of the elbows.  #bentarmlateralraise #lateralraise #shoulders #delts #deltoids

exercise_name: Low Bench Alternating Step Down Reverse Lunge to Hammer Curl
coaching_cues: Make sure members are getting deeper into the lunge
Weight choice will be limited by the strength of the hammer curl so should be on the lighter side for a deeper lunge

videos.description:
Low Bench Alternating Step Down Reverse Lunge to Hammer Curl

The low bench is used to create more range of motion. Make sure members are getting deeper into the lunge. If they are just doing a superficial step back, they are better off doing a flat ground lunge. Weight choice will be limited by the strength of the hammer curl so should be on the lighter side for a deeper lunge. 

#lowbenchalternatingstepdownreverselungetohammercurl #lowbench #deficitreverselunge #hammercurl #otfcoach

Low Bench Box Jump

This is going to be more comfortable for some members than a flat ground jump squat with the reduction of landing impact. It’s not necessarily wrong, but it is safest to have them land full foot on the bench and step down. 
It will be intimidating for some, so have some prep to build them up as well as some options to keep them working on power output but being safe and successful! 

#otflowbenchboxjump #boxjump #plyometrics #orangetheory #otfcoach

videos.description:
Low Side Plank

Members usually find out quickly they have one side stronger or more coordinated than the other. Keep a sharp eye on the shoulders to make sure they are stacked and the torso is perpendicular to the ground. Having them drive the arm straight up will help a ton if you don’t do too much kinesthetic cueing. It helps to use a dumbbell or the bench for some gentle support. 

#otflowsideplank #lowsideplank #sideplank #plankvariation #ptfcoach

videos.description: Low Side Plank Rotation Great core movement combining rotation and anti-rotation. Everything from the waist down we squeeze to keep as steady as possible. The rotation comes from the upper body squeezing the obliques to rotate through the upper back/thoracic spine.  Very often members don’t even realize their hips are moving. We have to think of ways to steady the hips. This can be some kinesthetic cueing by holding their hips in place, placing your leg as a block, placing something on their hips to balance… We can always have them do some reps in the kickstand/kneeling position before moving to the feet.  #lowsideplankrotation #lowplankrotation #sideplanktwist #sideplank #antirotation

coaching_cues: Keep an eye out
We want to build them to be more resilient and handle the stress of anti-rotation and keep them pain free
Which basically means that we don’t want to cross back too far to the side
This should be PAIN-FREE, but still challenging

videos.description:
Is it SAFE for your knee to move side to side when doing Cross-Back or Curtsy Lunges?

The short answer is YES! That’s because the knee does more than just extend and bend the leg. The ligaments within the knee structure allow for SLIGHT rotation of the knee joint!

Key word being SLIGHT! Which basically means that we don’t want to cross back too far to the side!

A good cue to remember is to step “JUST PASS” the midline of the front foot when you cross over - that’s it!

This allows you to keep good knee alignment and build strength in the quads, glutes, and connective tissue that supports that slight rotation!

If this is PAINFUL, you’re doing too much! Either stepping too far to the side or not regressing enough.

Don’t just default to using dumbbells. Try body weight only! Still feeling pain? Regress to holding onto chair or TRX straps to offset some of your weight into your arms. This should be PAIN-FREE, but still challenging!

You’re not too weak to do this! You just need to stop letting your ego get in the way and realize that you can regress while STILL having time to build up strength… life is long and you’ve got time!

But we gotta do it RIGHT and not get hurt!! Now, do it!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#lunge #lunges #legday #kneepain #knees #legworkout #legday #orangetheoryfitness
The Daily Beat talks about it but members often short the range of motion with the low bench. Keep an eye out. #otf #orangetheory #coach #lunges #lowbench #rom

videos.description: Quite often members don’t have the mobility to straighten the leg in the single leg squat aka the pistol position. They are also often surprise on their ability to achieve success with this option.

Make sure we have options to have our members safe but also progressing to control in a bigger range of motion while staying explosive.

exercise_name: My BIGGEST realization as a trainer for over 10 years…
coaching_cues: They need to be INSANELY TOUGH in the last few reps of your set

videos.description:
My BIGGEST realization as a trainer for over 10 years…

Most people simply aren’t lifting heavy enough to see the results they want 🤷‍♂️

The weights you lift can’t just be “a little uncomfortable. They need to be INSANELY TOUGH in the last few reps of your set. And for many, it’s simply too intimidating to “go heavy” to get the real results out of weight training…

How do you overcome this intimidation? It starts with SUPER LOW-RISK exercises like Bench Rows! An exercise like this is perfect to get out of your head and into the mindset of: HEAVY!

Why? Because if it’s too heavy, you can simply drop it down. No risk. Nothing!

We can work on building confidence for other exercises later… but THIS ONE is where it all starts! So, stop making it “kind of uncomfortable” and LETS GET HEAVY!!

Now, DO IT!!! 25 pounds+ for the weight. Try it.

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#fitness #fitnesstips #fitnessmotivation #heavy #workout #workoutmotivation #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: My knee pain went away when I started squatting like this…
coaching_cues: But once you realize that you can “screw your feet into the ground” to better activate the hip joint to stabilize the knees and ankles - WOWZA
And when you turn your feet out, like myself and many others did/do, you CANT create that screwing tension to activate your hips
Of course we all have different hip anatomy, so you might need to stand wider or more narrow, but the concept is the same

videos.description:
My knee pain went away when I started squatting like this…

That is, with “toes forward”! What a game-changer this is when you actually know how to apply it correctly!

“Toes forward” didn’t work well for me in the past because I would just point forward and hope for the best. But once you realize that you can “screw your feet into the ground” to better activate the hip joint to stabilize the knees and ankles - WOWZA!!

This “screwing” concept is the real magic! And when you turn your feet out, like myself and many others did/do, you CANT create that screwing tension to activate your hips! And if you can’t get your hips involved, you’re not going to stabilize those knees which can lead to pain!

Of course we all have different hip anatomy, so you might need to stand wider or more narrow, but the concept is the same!

If squats have been giving you trouble, try this technique and notice what a difference it makes! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#squat #squats #squatform #squatting #legday #legs #legworkout #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: Never let your knees go over your toes when you squat…
coaching_cues: The key is to regress squats as FAR BACK AS YOU NEED TO in order to squat knees over toes pain-free
We just need to drop our ego, and regress in order to BUILD down the line

videos.description:
Never let your knees go over your toes when you squat…

How many times have you heard that??

If you’re trying to keep your knees from going over your toes to “protect” your knees, you’re probably doing more long-term harm because you’re essentially training your body to not let it be OKAY for your knees to go over your toes?

But should it be okay? YES! Because of natural motions like jumping & going down stairs, our bodies were DESIGNED to have our knees go over our toes!

The key is to regress squats as FAR BACK AS YOU NEED TO in order to squat knees over toes pain-free.

That means no weights, and not even bodyweight if that hurts… use assistance to shift some of your weight into your upper body, such as TRX to offset up to 99.99% of your bodyweight.

There IS a pain-free level you can start with… and then you have your entire life to build up and get strong! We just need to drop our ego, and regress in order to BUILD down the line!

Let’s get back to training our body to have increased ABILITY, rather than try to limit it - especially as we get older!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#squat #squats #squatting #kneesovertoes #legday #orangetheoryfitness #tallfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: Not enough people realize how MASSIVE of a mistake this is…
coaching_cues: Then, drive back with your hands lifted high and notice INSTANTLY how much stronger and connected your stroke feels

videos.description:
Not enough people realize how MASSIVE of a mistake this is…

On the @concept2inc (and pretty much every rowing machine, water rower etc)- you’ve gotta understand that the chain CONNECTS during the stroke sequence at a SLIGHT DIAGONAL angle!

It is not “straight in and out of the machine”. Seriously… go film yourself rowing and you will see EVERY TIME the chain snaps up slightly.

Every inch of the drive you take before the “snap up” is WASTED because you have little to no connection when the chain isn’t slightly diagonally pulled.

While this example in the video might be exaggerated, carrying your hands low is taking most of the efficiency out of your rowing stroke.

❌ The answer is NOT to “scoop” the handle to lift the hands! This is unsustainable at high stroke rates.

✅ Instead, try this drill. Pause at the knees with the handle low to your thighs, then slowly “trickle” aka lift your hands up up up… it’s a SUBTLE movement. Then, drive back with your hands lifted high and notice INSTANTLY how much stronger and connected your stroke feels.

Massive difference. That handle path takes some practice to nail down, but once you get it, you’ll feel connected from start to finish every stroke! 💪

Happy rowing! 🚣‍♂️

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #rowingworkout #rowingworld #hyrox #crossfit #rowingispassion #rowingrelated

exercise_name: One of the hardest parts about Split Squats is NOT losing your balance

videos.description:
One of the hardest parts about Split Squats is NOT losing your balance! It can be super frustrating…

Here’s an easy trick to help with balance:

Think about standing like you’re on “railroad tracks” rather than on a “tightrope”. If your feet are in a line, your balance is going to suffer, but if you simply step your foot slightly more out to the side to better distribute your center of mass!

It won’t negatively affect the muscles worked and will actually HELP you get better stabilization from the front working leg - allowing you to lift heavier and feel more confident each rep!

Give this tip a shot for your next set of Split Squats and notice how much better they feel! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#legs #legday #legworkout #legsworkout #squat #squats #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: This row will take awhile, so you need to TRUST the pacing goal

videos.description:
PACING STRATEGY: 1000m Row 🔥

Aim for 3-6 seconds SLOWER on your split/500m compared to your best 500m row time.

For example, if you row a 1:25 500m, you’d aim for a 1:28-1:31 on your 1000m row. The more experienced you are, the closer to ~3 spit seconds faster you can get!

The trick is to get there RIGHT AWAY! Don’t let adrenaline cause you to go faster. This row will take awhile, so you need to TRUST the pacing goal!

Keep the stroke rate high (28+) with lower effort strokes to best use your cardio and not gas out your muscles when trying to hit your split/500m goal right away!

Then, just hold. If you felt you were too conservative, go extra hard for the last 200m.

But you can’t get back burning out early!!!!! Now, DO IT!

What’s your best 1000m time? 🤨
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowinglife #rowingispassion #erg #crossfit #hyrox #orangetheoryfitness #benchmark #1000m

coaching_cues: “Pull and roll”… that should help to remind you to keep those damn legs bent as you pull yourself forward
Squat down, handles at your sides
Pull forward and try to ROLL as you pull

videos.description:
The HARDEST TRX exercise gets A LOT EASIER when you keep in mind my 4 step process for Pull-ups!

1. Squat down, handles at your sides
2. Lean back, keeping your legs bent from the squat
3. Pull forward and try to ROLL as you pull
4. Stand up and press through your arms

Most people screw up the 3rd part by straightening the legs before standing. STOP DOING THAT!! It’s literally making it impossible for you to do this exercise correctly.

“Pull and roll”… that should help to remind you to keep those damn legs bent as you pull yourself forward!!

Do you struggle with this exercise? Do you like it?? Let me know your thoughts!

Hope this post could help you out! 💪

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#trx #trxtraining #trxworkout #trxtrainer #pullups #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #tallfitness

coaching_cues: BONUS TIP: Try your best to get as much range (under control) as possible

videos.description:
Pullovers feeling weird? Here’s the fix…

When doing the pullover, a lot of folks experience shoulder or arm discomfort when doing the lift.

The fix for this is to “turn your elbows in slightly”! Instead of letting your arms do whatever when you hold the weight, simply try to “turn” your elbows like you’re trying to touch them together!

This sets your arms and shoulders into a more stable position and will definitely make the lift feel more stable - and make YOU feel more confident in your reps!

BONUS TIP: Try your best to get as much range (under control) as possible. This is a GREAT exercise for helping you open up a tight chest and counteract the “shoulders rounded forward” position that we spend most of our life in! Don’t rush this one!

Love always,

Coach Austin
.
.
.

coaching_cues: Important that we try to maintain a flat back in this explosive movement
Keep practicing consistently - there’s nothing you can’t accomplish
Remember to squeeze your butt forward, brace your abs, and aim diagonally down to the bench TOUCHING your chest
Keep your knees off of the ground for the eccentric (lowering phase), which is where your body is strongest
Make sure you go slow on the way down to get the most out of this variation
Knees on the ground

videos.description:
Don’t fall into the trap of defaulting to “no knees needed” pushups if you can’t get the full range!!

You’re letting your precious ego get in the way from you actually getting RESULTS!

Instead, keep in mind these regressions:

1. Knees on the ground. Remember to squeeze your butt forward, brace your abs, and aim diagonally down to the bench TOUCHING your chest!

2. No-Knee Eccentrics! These are the bridge between knees and “no knee” pushups! Keep your knees off of the ground for the eccentric (lowering phase), which is where your body is strongest. TOUCH your chest, then place your knees down to push yourself back to the top! Make sure you go slow on the way down to get the most out of this variation!

You have your ENTIRE LIFE to get “good” at pushups! But you’ll never get there if all you do is half-range BS pushups!! So, dial back, and the let the gains begin! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #pushupseveryday #pushupchallenge #chestday #chest #ego #plantpower #orangetheoryfitness

I’d NEVER recommend Wall Pushups for anyone who wants to actually get good at pushups!

First off, the wall prevents you from moving through a proper pushing arc with your arms. When your hands are on the wall, your only option is to go “straight into the wall”, which causes your elbows to flare out and simply doesn’t help you get strong in the motion you’ll need to get strong in to do pushups on the floor.

Speaking of “get strong”, the wall also presents so little challenge, even for the most untrained simply because gravity goes straight down as you push your body back horizontally. There’s simply no resistance to get strong from. And if you tried to step back to create challenge, it only makes the form worse!

Starting on a high, elevated bench with your knees on the ground is the BEST place to start getting good at pushups! This setup helps you perform the proper “arcing” motion we want with pushups and provides enough resistance to stimulate strength gains while still being scalable to all fitness levels. 

Too tough? Elevate your hands to a higher bench surface. Too easy? Progress this by lowering the height of the bench/ledge/surface.

Eventually, you’ll be able to progress to flat ground with your knees! Then, the half knees on, half knees off variation, and eventually full pushups without your knees!

There’s a process to get your first pushup - and it takes time, but it definitely doesn’t take Wall Pushups!!
 Now, do it!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #pushupchallenge #bodyweightworkout #calisthenics #homeworkout #orangetheoryfitness

Reality check: You’re not getting better at pushups because you’re TOO SCARED to regress…

Getting good at pushups comes from MASTERING every single regression.

That is going to take a hit to your ego… because the fitness world has deemed pushups to be this thing that everyone should just be able to do… that’s HORRENDOUSLY false, by the way.

Start by mastering Bench Pushups. And by mastery, we’re talking FULL RANGE touching your chest to the bench EVERY REP. When you can string together consistent sets of 10 reps at least, you’re ready for Level 2.

Level 2 is the knee pushup. Knees on the ground, and aiming diagonally down and forward, TOUCHING YOUR CHEST, then pushing up and back. One again, work your way up to nailing 3+ sets of 10 reps with this level before moving on.

Level 3 is the HYBRID pushup, which I like to think of as “knees on, knees off”. You start by going down SLOWLY without your knees on the ground, to a 3 second count. Touch your chest, then put your knees ON the ground, then push back up and repeat.

When you get good at those, eventually you’ll be able to TRULY knock out pushups without your knees, with QUALITY form and NO EGO!

This is what it takes. Good pushups are hard to do. And getting strong at pushups is a long and strenuous journey. But the good news is you have your whole life to GET GOOD. But it starts with doing things RIGHT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#pushups #pushup #pushupseveryday #push #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys

Bear Plank to Alternating Knee Tap to Push Up Make sure members start off with a wide knee/foot base that allows a more stable position to keep the hold steady when we go through the knee tap.  When it comes to the push up, members often crane their necks, shorting the range of motion. It will help to give them a higher point of focus to keep a neutral neck.  The kneel mat can help to make sure members return to a good bear position. We can also encourage a knee push up if needed for a better range of motion.  #otf #coach #otfcoach #bearplanktoalternatingkneetaptopushup #bearplankkneetappushup

Burpee (No Push Up aka Up Down) Encourage members to try to keep the heels planted when the hands find the ground to relieve pressure in the ankle/knee. Important that we try to maintain a flat back in this explosive movement. Generally can go feet inside hands or feet out but always helpful to land wide on the return.  #burpee #burpeenopushup #updown #otf #coach

Alternating Burpee to Bench Lateral Step Over No push up on the burpee; one burpee one full step over. So if we have the space, burpees are done on both sides of the bench. If you are even questioning safety or you think it will be close, have members only do burpees on one side of the bench (but have them turn opposite direction half way). #alternatingburpeetobenchlateralstepover #burpeestepover #burpee #otf #otfcoach

Side to Side Push Up Very similar to our step out motions we are really working a weight transfer from hand to hand. Normal push up ideal form, neutral spine (probably eye focus 1-2’ forward), chest touching/thighs do not, corkscrew the hands into the ground with a slight tuck of the elbows.  If we use knees as our option, make sure members use the kneel mat to reduce friction/burn on the knees. If members can’t do them from toes but knees are too easy, we can elevate them up to a bench.  #pushups #sidetosidepushups #otf #otfcoach #alternatingaidepushups

If you feel like you’re never getting better at pushups, you might wanna hear this…

Your pushups will NOT get better if you’re treating the pushup as a “down and up” motion. This strict “down and up” tends to put a lot of excess strain on the shoulders without optimizing the use of your chest muscles!

The key to getting stronger at pushups? Start moving diagonally with the motion. A ever-so-slight “down and forward” motion will take strain off of your shoulders and put most of the work on your chest - where it’s supposed to be!

This WILL make pushups objectively “feel harder”, but you’ll be getting more out of each rep in the best way possible. Don’t forget to regress with knees on the ground or the hybrid “knees off, knees on” method I show in the video!

Hopefully these tips can help you out on your pushup journey! Keep practicing consistently - there’s nothing you can’t accomplish! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #chest #chestday #chestworkout #calisthenics #calisthenicsworkout #bodyweighttraining #orangetheoryftiness

coaching_cues: Make sure knee tracks 2nd/3rd toe
You can always step around a less agressive angle to control knee tracking
Make sure to step back and out
Don’t plant the foot then squat
If the knee collapses inside of the big toe, we get excessive stress on the lateral stabilizers of the base leg knee

videos.description: Refining the transverse squat! We do a lot of saggital plane movements and even some frontal plane but not too much in the transverse plane.  1. Don’t plant the foot then squat. If you plant the foot then squat. The tranverse plane only worked to get the foot into place, then you are doing a regular squat. Think rotate and squat simultaneously.  2. If the knee collapses inside of the big toe, we get excessive stress on the lateral stabilizers of the base leg knee. Make sure knee tracks 2nd/3rd toe. You can always step around a less agressive angle to control knee tracking.  3. If the base foot moves, we are taking away from the tranverse plane and getting closer to a transverse lunge vs a squat. Glue that foot in place.  4. Stepping back in-line with the base leg puts excessive rotation at either the knee or hip. Make sure to step back and out. You can always use a bench as a marker to ensure you are stepping out and around.  Enjoy and keep those knees safe!  #otf #orangetheory #coach #tranversesquat #transverseplane #squat

exercise_name: Reverse Biceps Curls can be really uncomfortable…
coaching_cues: So, we don’t want to fight our anatomy by trying to hold the weights horizontally

videos.description:
Reverse Biceps Curls can be really uncomfortable…

They can feel awkward or painful on the wrists, but that’s usually the case if you’re trying to hold the dumbbells “horizontal”.

If you relax your arms at your sides, make fists, and then look down at your hands, you’ll notice that our body NATURALLY rests our wrists at a slight diagonal angle.

We do not have arms that sit nice, perfectly horizontal - because we’re not robots!
 So, we don’t want to fight our anatomy by trying to hold the weights horizontally. Instead, hold them at a slight angle, palms down, as you curl. You’ll instantly feel stronger and more comfortable with this exercise!

Now, do it!

Coach Austin
.
.

#biceps #bicep #armworkout #armday #armsworkout #orangetheoryfitness

videos.description: Front Loaded Contralateral Reverse Lunge to Single Arm Press with Upward Rotation

Lateral Bound to Reverse Lunge Plyometrics are generally categorized into either short response or long response. Lateral bounds are a long response where we take time to load, explode, and then absorb force.  To keep members successful start off with a small distance to get used of the balance it requires in the lateral movement. As they move through the timed section, ideally they would open up more range still controlling the balance.  With this as a primer, we want to move through a big range of motion in the lunge, opening up range at the hip and knee.  #otf #coach #otfcoach #lateralboundtoreverselunge #lateralbounds

Alternating Reverse Lunge Reverse lunges are often easier on the knees than forward lunges. We do want to keep an upright torso vs hinging. We should also be pretty analytical when it comes to options. Can we maintain the intended stimulus (heavy, endurance, TUT…)? Can we maintain the intended movement pattern target (squat, hinge, lunge…)? Can we maintain unilateral vs bilateral? All of this should be considered for the given option.  #alternatjngreverselunge #reverselunge #lunge #otf #otfcoach

Alternating Forward Lunge Even though the majority of lunge we see are reverse lunges. There is a good amount of value in a forward lunge with an upright torso. Not only is a bit more quad biased, it gives us an opportunity to strengthen the musculature around the knee. If there is discomfort at the knee, we can always have the try stepping out further, or even going to that reverse lunge.  #alternatingforwardlunge #lunges #quads #otf #otfcoach

Bent Over Rows are a staple for building strong back muscles - but REVERSE grip rows can offer MORE!

When your palms face away for a “reverse grip”, you’ll recruit your biceps to help with the lift - allowing you to lift more weight!

So make sure you go HEAVIER than you normally would if you want to get the most out of this exercise!

When you set up, you don’t want your lower back to round, so make sure you keep your feet close and think “butt back” with a slight knee bend to hinge you over safely!

Lastly, try to think “elbows back” when you lift the weights up! Simply “pulling up” with your hands isn’t going to recruit your muscles optimally for this style of row.

Go heavy, hinge properly, and think “elbows back” and you’re gonna rock this one! Now, do it!

Coach Austin

coaching_cues:
I just needed something click-baity;)

The mistake is leaning back as you drive your legs
You HAVE to settle to a goal pace if you want to successfully pull a new best on the rower
And for the 2000m, that pace should feel ALMOST TOO EASY for the first 500m
A lot of hate for the rower stems from the lack of having CONTROL and PATIENCE on the return (also known as ‘the recovery’) during each stroke
The “work” of each stroke is done on the drive back
But rushing up for the next stroke more often than not leads to a slower, less intense drive back
Start taking your time on the way back up, and drive back with as much force as you can, and you’ll start to enjoy rowing A LOT MORE
But that’s EXACTLY how much your legs should be bending to optimize power & stroke rate for this very niche sprint
If you want to level up your rowing power, you’ve gotta master the front end body position with the rowing stroke
Keep your heels down and only bend to ~90 degrees in your legs

videos.description:
A lot of hate for the rower stems from the lack of having CONTROL and PATIENCE on the return (also known as ‘the recovery’) during each stroke.

Don’t get it twisted: fast strokes are good for SHORT SPRINTS! But anything longer than that… you’re in for a bad time if you’re trying to “row fast”.

The “work” of each stroke is done on the drive back. And that’s where we want the HARD/FAST action to be. But the moment you hit the finish position, you’ve gotta practice turning your brain off and taking your time on the way back up.

Slowing down the return to a 2-3 second count really helps you to practice proper form sequencing and gives the monitor a chance to run out some extra meters every stroke!

But rushing up for the next stroke more often than not leads to a slower, less intense drive back.

Start taking your time on the way back up, and drive back with as much force as you can, and you’ll start to enjoy rowing A LOT MORE!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowingworkout #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness

Why listen to some rando (me) when you can watch the WORLD RECORD HOLDER row the 500 meter row!?

When we watch @bigphil90 , his performance gives us great insight into a handful of useful techniques to do for this sprint row! Keep in mind, these techniques are specific to the 500m, and wouldn’t be advised for longer rows or any rowing focused on basic, steady-state cardio health & longevity.

This is form and technique for PERFORMANCE.

1️⃣ A focus on high stroke rate. Phil is 6 foot 9 inches tall, yet still manages a 40+ stroke rate throughout, managing a 47 in the last few pulls! ✅ Keep the stroke rate high, because this is a sprint!

2️⃣ Focus more on a BIG & STRONG lean and swing, rather than using long strokes. Check out Phil’s leg bend… it’s about 90 degrees. But that’s EXACTLY how much your legs should be bending to optimize power & stroke rate for this very niche sprint.

Bending your legs more will prevent the average person from getting the proper 11 o’clock lean forward you want for this sprint! So, try to think “90 degree leg bend”, which for most (not all) will be a “heels down” approach.

3️⃣ Fightint until the end! The effort and intensity from the first stroke to last stroke was virtually the same! And that’s because there was a goal race pace to hold (which you need to set and stick to) and an understanding that the last 150m will be UNBEARABLY HARD and you will want to slow down.

Fight until the end, even when your body wants to give in! What a row… 👏 Good luck to everyone sprinting their own 500m! Let me know how it goes! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #hyrox #rowingrelated #rowingworkout #orangetheoryfitness

⚠️ If you want MORE meters for FREE when rowing, you gotta stop pulling each stroke.

Rowing is a pull, right? In most contexts, YES. But in the contexts of rowing on a ROWING MACHINE, the stroke is very much NOT a pull!

It’s a PUSH. Every stroke starts with a leg push. And while many have HEARD that before, most still struggle to execute the leg push properly. 

Most end up with a sort of “push legs AND pull arms at the same time”-hybrid type of movement! I personally was guilty of this for YEARS when I was rowing in college.

But a true rowing PUSH with the legs means that your torso stays LEANED forward for the first half of the rowing stroke. If you lean back AT ALL during the leg push, you miss out on the power of the hip swing.

But if you swing AFTER a leg push and THEN pull, that’s where the magic happens! I like to think of it as “earning the right to pull” each stroke. You only earn it if you push, then swing first!

I hope that makes sense… this takes some SERIOUS practice and intention. But it will pay off for you in the long run! Let me know your thoughts…

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rowing #rowinglife #rowingmachine #rower #erg #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit

If you want to level up your rowing power, you’ve gotta master the front end body position with the rowing stroke!

This position tends to be one of the most difficult to get right when looking at the entire rowing stroke - and even small mistakes can cost you A LOT of power!

This most important aspect of a good front catch position on the rower is to make sure your torso angles forward at a 1 o’clock position.
 A LOT of people make the mistake of bending the legs too much, with makes it nearly impossible to get the proper lean forward. This usually results in a more “vertical torso”. And if your torso is vertical, you won’t get much power in your layback with each stroke!

To help ensure you get the proper lean forward, I highly encourage you to bend your legs less. This gives you more room to lean in! Try thinking “heels down, lean more” to help with this!

The pro rowers are able to bend their legs all the way while still getting the proper lean, and you can do that too if you have the flexibility and awareness to do so.

But for most, just start with less leg bend to help with more lean!

From there, you know the drill: Push, swing, pull! 

Happy rowing! Hope this helps! NOW, DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#rowing #rower #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #power #orangetheoryfitness #cardio

The idea here is to manage having a larger midsection while still being able to achieve the same LEAN at the front of each stroke that you would get if you didn’t have a large midsection.

Most that are heavier tend to feel like they’re getting crushed when they try to lean in - so how do we fix it?

1. Loosen the foot straps. This allows you to turn your feet out, and therefore push your knees out instead of keeping them together. And no, this is not bad for your knees.

2. Stop bending your legs all the way up. Keep your heels down and only bend to ~90 degrees in your legs. This will give you room to lean without hurting your lower back.

And you’re good to go! Push, swing, pull. Same sequencing, same stroke technique for the most part! Hope this helps!

- Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rowingmachine #rower #rowing #erg #rowingrelated #rowingworkout #orangetheory #fatloss

If you have a large midsection (pregnant or otherwise), you won’t row optimally if you try to row with traditional rowing form!

You have to modify a couple things if you want to get the most out of every rowing stroke! 👍

First thing is to NOT strap in tight with your feet! Loosen up the foot straps instead and point your toes and knees out slightly!

‼️ PSA ‼️ Rowing with your knees angled out will NOT hurt your knees as long as your toes point out, too!

Second thing to do is to grip the handle with a NARROW grip, rather than as wide as possible!

A narrow grip will allow you to row between your legs, which lets you get the ideal lean forward we want each stroke!

With those two changes in mind, it’s the same PUSH - SWING - PULL sequence you’d normally do! The mechanics stay the same… the setup is what changes!

Hope that helps!!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #row #rower #rowingmachine #erg #rowingmachineworkout #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #veganfitness

The BIGGEST Rowing Complaint (besides “it sucks”) is not being able to keep your “heels down” when you row!

First, here’s some context: YOU ONLY KEEP YOUR HEELS DOWN IF YOU’RE SPRINTING!

If you’re doing anything else other than a sprint, your heels can lift, and they should be lifting as long as you can achieve the proper body lean at the front of the stroke!

If you can’t keep your heels down when you’re sprinting, it’s because you’re not leaning forward enough. SIMPLE AS THAT!

You need to emphasize MORE body lean in order for your heels to stay planted. If you lean more, your heels stay down. Nuff said.

And when your heels stay planted, you row higher watts and a better sprint because you can row a higher stroke rate. WOO!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #veganfitness

🗣️“Arms punch out” is a cue I hear that makes me want to PUNCH OUT ANY COACH WHO SAYS IT!!! 👊

Because it’s WRONG. Yes, your arms come out first on the return, but they should NOT be going straight out. This creates unnecessary tension in the shoulders and will make your arms tired before your legs, which we do NOT want if we want to row at our best!

Think about “relaxing down and out” with the arms instead! ✅ This will allow your arms to come out first in a more smooth way, and encourage you to follow the “arms, body, legs” sequence in a much more fluid motion!

Don’t punch your arms out on the return or I’m gonna come to your studio and fix it!!!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rowingmachine #rower #row #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #veganfitness

Everyone THINKS they know how to row because of their CrossFit or Orangetheory coach…

But training on the erg (aka the rowing machine) is A LOT different than rowing in a racing shell…

The dynamic path the handle should take while erging relaxes your shoulders, tapping your arms “down and out” followed by a slow lift up to the catch position, from where you then drive back and pull into the chest.

This is NOT what you see in a rowing boat. Most finish positions end with the oar handle close to the lap, and the angle of the handle path is a “high to low” path due to the angular motion of the rowing oar through water.

Why don’t the pros on the U23 German women’s team and GB men’s team (in these video example) row on the machines like they would on the water? Because they are training their FITNESS on the ergs and training their SKILLS on the water.

Why would you row like you’re on a boat if you’re not on a boat where the dynamics and environment are completely different? It’s all about context!

Also, stop punching your arms out when you row because that’s making you slower! 😃

Coach Austin 🤘 🚣‍♂️ 
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #ergo #rowingrelated #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #hyrox #hyroxtraining #rowingispassion

The @hyroxworld competitors are some high-caliber athletes, but so many who have DECENT row form are still making this massive mistake.

And this mistake goes far beyond Hyrox. I just needed something click-baity;)

The mistake is leaning back as you drive your legs. There are many degrees to this, and while im exaggerating a bit in this video, it isnt uncommon for it to be that extreme of an early lay back… but ANY degree of early lean back means you are getting NO POWER from the hip swing.

You gotta lay back only AFTER the legs are fully straightened to get the most out of the hip swing.

To practice this, try the Reverse Pick Drill. Classic drill for rowing athletes - essentially breaking the stroke down to “legs first”, then adding in the torso, then adding in the arms!

Even a few strokes at each point can help you FEEL the difference! Give it a shot!

Happy rowing! 🚣 🤙

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #hyrox #rowingmotivation #rowingrelated #rowingworkout #rowingform

For 99% of people, all you have to do to PR your 2000m row is to not start so fast.

TOO MANY let the adrenaline of the race get to them and end up burning out after the first 500m.

You HAVE to settle to a goal pace if you want to successfully pull a new best on the rower! And for the 2000m, that pace should feel ALMOST TOO EASY for the first 500m.

But then, if for SOME REASON you have extra energy in the last 500m, you can crank it up then! But you can’t get back burning out the first 500m…

To set your goal, go and search “erg split calculator”. Click on the link from @concept2inc and type in a finish time that is 1-2 seconds faster than your current record. All you have to do is SETTLE to that split and hold it after the first few strokes of the row.

Can YOU control your body and settle?

Or are you going to let another personal best slip away because you let adrenaline and ego get the best of you in the first 2 minutes?

The choice is yours.

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rowing #rower #rowingmachine #erg #2000m #2k #2km #crossfit #hyrox #orangetheory

A LOT OF YOU WILL NOT BELIEVE ME when I tell you the REAL reason you can’t feel it in your legs when rowing…

And that’s because it’s a concept that most people don’t even think about for a second! 

And that is the vertical HEIGHT of your handle when you row. 

The fact is, MOST PEOPLE carry the handle too low when they row. And that’s a BIG problem considering that on rowing machines, the handle and strap/chain grab tension out of the machine at a SLIGHTLY UPWARDS ANGLE!

Just watch carefully in the video… you can see how the handle has to “snap” into position each stroke, and that position is a slightly diagonal upwards. That means if your hands are lower than that, the moment you try to use your legs, the first portion of your leg drive is your handle trying to find connection! And that means you really aren’t using your legs effectively!

You simply just need to carry your hands higher. How much higher? You’ll have to play around with it to find the sweet spot… go too high and the handle will “snap” down the other way. Your handle is always trying to find the line of tension… Take some time to practice playing around with your handle height and see if you notice a difference when you carry your hands a little higher!

Love always,
 Coach Austin
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #rowingmachineworkout #rowingform #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit

coaching_cues: That means you gotta use your LEGS if you want to get the most out of the rowing stroke

videos.description:
Rowing cardio = PUSH, not pull…

When you lift weights and “row”, you’re using your back and arm muscles to PULL the weight- as you should!

But in the CARDIO version of rowing, you’re using your entire body! That means you gotta use your LEGS if you want to get the most out of the rowing stroke!

And that means you have to PUSH away using your legs BEFORE you pull your arms at the end!

The # 1 mistake people make is “pulling” the start of the stroke - and it’s totally destroying your rowing capability! Do your best to remember to PUSH instead of pull, to start every stroke! 👍

Love always,

Coach Austin
.

coaching_cues: Focus on making sure your torso leans forward at an 11 or 1 o’clock angle and your arms are as straight as possible

videos.description:
Rowing isn’t for short people.

It doesn’t mean you can’t do row well! It’s just that when measuring raw output on rowing machines, bigger & taller people will produce more power than shorter, lighter people of equal fitness & technique!

But most people don’t have great technique - so if you’re a shortie, you need to MASTER the technique and have BETTER technique than your tall counterparts!

You gotta row as LONG as you can even when you’re short. That means mainly focusing on the forward LEAN at the front of the stroke with super outstretched arms!

Focus on making sure your torso leans forward at an 11 or 1 o’clock angle and your arms are as straight as possible! Let your shoulder blades round forward to truly get your full reach!

The , as you push your legs and swing back, make sure you’re leaning back to a complimentary 11 or 1 angle!!

When this is your focus, you can out-row A LOT of taller folks!

Now, do it!!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowinglife #rowingworkout #orangetheoryfitness #veganfitness

exercise_name: Rowing isn’t what you think it is
coaching_cues: We do NOT want to think about the handle moving in a straight line in and out of the rower
We also do NOT want to “swirl” or “swoosh” the handle as if you’re taking your oars in and out of the water
On the return, you want to RELAX your arms and shoulders to allow the handle to tap down close to your thighs
On the drive, the handle drives back fairly straight, but in a slightly diagonal motion to maximize stroke distance

videos.description:
Rowing isn’t what you think it is.

The “way to row” is a lot more dynamic than it might seem!

We do NOT want to think about the handle moving in a straight line in and out of the rower. This makes the motion very robotic and can tire out muscles that aren’t helpful for efficient rowing.

We also do NOT want to “swirl” or “swoosh” the handle as if you’re taking your oars in and out of the water. This leads to too much motion and inefficiency and can lead to chaos at higher stroke rates!

Both are wrong! But the RIGHT way to row involves a combination of both.

On the return, you want to RELAX your arms and shoulders to allow the handle to tap down close to your thighs.

Once you pass the knees, the handle starts to track UP into a lifted position at the catch (front) position!

On the drive, the handle drives back fairly straight, but in a slightly diagonal motion to maximize stroke distance! And then, the process repeats!

Once you can start implementing this into your rowing, you’ll feel just how fluid, efficient, and strong your rowing can be! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowinglife #rowingmachineworkout #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: Rowing world champions do 1 thing you probably don’t do enough of…
coaching_cues: And neither should you
Keep leaning back each stroke, but don’t forget to lean forward too

videos.description:
Rowing world champions do 1 thing you probably don’t do enough of…

And that’s LEAN FORWARD!! Most of us know to lean back when we row, but we can get so focused on bending our legs to get up to the front of each stroke that we overcompress our legs and don’t get enough torso lean!

The torso lean forward aka shoulders in front of the hips is CRUCIAL for maximum efficiency and power in your rowing stroke. It allows you to push with your legs, and then SWING with your torso and hips! It’s that swing that generates a huge amount of power - and if you’re not leaning forward enough, you’re not getting any power in your swing!

This truly is the 1 thing you will see across all top-tier rowing athletes when they row.. they NEVER compromise the lean forward! And neither should you!

Keep leaning back each stroke, but don’t forget to lean forward too!

Shoutout to @eltgilmore for the great demo!

Now DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit #tallgirls

exercise_name: Rowing “fast” does NOT come from just taking more strokes at a quicker rate…
coaching_cues: Sometimes you don’t need to increase the stroke… you just need to increase your power-per-stroke

videos.description:
Rowing “fast” does NOT come from just taking more strokes at a quicker rate…

Your “boat speed” in rowing comes from the watts you generate - which is based on both stroke rate AND most importantly… your power per stroke!

In these two examples, you can see the drastic difference between just “taking strokes faster” and actually putting more POWER into each stroke!

Sometimes you don’t need to increase the stroke… you just need to increase your power-per-stroke! 💪 🚣‍♀️ 

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #rowinglife #rowingispassion #rowingrelated #crossfit #orangetheory #hyrox

Running speeds “trained” vs. “untrained”.

What are the biggest differences you see? 🤔

A few tips:
- Focus less on trying to “step forward”
- Focus more on trying to pull your feet of the ground quicker
- Pump your arms forward and back, not side to side
- Stand tall through the hips
- Try to “fall” forward as you pick your feet up

Happy running! 🏃 🏃‍♀️

exercise_name: Same speed, DRASTICALLY different running form

videos.description:
Same speed, DRASTICALLY different running form!

The drastic differences in form and effort between 130, 150, and 180 strides per minute really highlight how cadence impacts our running!

At 130, you basically have to jump each step, POUNDING down on your joints just to keep up…

At 150, things get a little better, but you still end up landing your heel way out in front of your body, creating a ton of unnecessary impact.

At 180, the quick cadence of the feet allows the feet to land much closer to your center of mass, allowing you to more effectively use your foot and calf musculature to cushion your landing, resulting in way less impact!

Does that mean 180 is the perfect cadence? Not necessarily, but it does highlight that a higher cadence in general can help reduce intense impact on your joints each stride!

Something to think about… 😉 NOW DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #running #runner #runners #runningmotivation #treadmill #treadmillworkout #cadence #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: SHOULD YOUR HEELS LIFT WHEN YOU ROW
coaching_cues:
SHOULD YOUR HEELS LIFT WHEN YOU ROW?
The Olympic rowers in this video will show you the truth…

The short answer is: yes for the most part
✅ You DO lift your heels if the row is longer than 1 minute and you want to be as efficient and long each stroke as possible

videos.description:
SHOULD YOUR HEELS LIFT WHEN YOU ROW?
The Olympic rowers in this video will show you the truth…

The short answer is: yes for the most part!

❌You DO NOT lift your heels if you’re sprinting for 1 minute or less!
❌ You DO NOT lift your heels if you have a large midsection or you’re pregnant!

✅ You DO lift your heels at all other times AS LONG as you can keep a forward body lean every stroke!

✅ You DO lift your heels if the row is longer than 1 minute and you want to be as efficient and long each stroke as possible!

Any questions? Let me know!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘

exercise_name: Shoulder blades back and down “in the back pockets”

videos.description: Shoulder blades back and down “in the back pockets”. Keep it slow and under control (unless you want to thicken traps and build a bigger, thicker neck) 💪

Shoulder pain during chest press? 🤕

Try this simple fix!

If chest pressing with dumbbells lights up your shoulders in all the wrong ways, it might be your grip that’s the problem… not saying it’s ALWAYS the problem… BUT for many, it is!

👉 Instead of holding the weights horizontally like a barbell, turn them diagonally. ✅

This lets your elbows drop naturally — instead of flaring out — and BINGO! 💥 Shoulder relief!

Try it on your next set and feel the difference!!

🎯 Small tweak, BIG payoff! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.

Back to Back Shoulder Exercises (1/2 Kneeling Shoulder Press to Stand and BOSU High Plank Alternating Leg Lift)  #otf #coach #otfcoach #shoulders #bosu #backtoback #halfkneelingshoulderpress #plank #plankvariation #core #abs

Alternating Neutral Grip Push Press The whole intention of the dip is to gain a little power but the shoulder is still working a good amount. The depth of the dip is dependent on how much help the shoulder needs. If the weight is easy to moderate, the dip is shallow. The more fatigue that sets into the shoulder, the deeper the dip should get. Members should choose a weight too heavy to do multiple reps of a shoulder press. A good option would just to do an alternating explosive shoulder press.  #alternatingneutralgrippushpress #dumbbellalternatingpushpress #pushpress #shoulders #otf

Biceps Curl to Shoulder Press Solely in the curl we really focus on the importance of elbow position as it changes the leverage/momentum arm. Since this is a combination movement, it’s ok if the elbow moves forward of the torso since we are transitioning into a shoulder press. I follow the VVA but it is helpful to do both palms facing forward and palms rotating inwards when it comes to curls. For the shoulder press, idea palms face forward with elbow to the side. For some elbow in front of torso will help, for other palms fully facing in on a neutral grip. Last, if member are tight through the shoulders or are having a hard time bracing the core, we can always offer a staggered stance to help. #bicepscurltoshoulderpress #curltopress #combinationmovement #otf #coach

Single Leg Shoulder Press This movement is going to be a ton more challenging for members than it seems, especially with cushioned running shoes with a high stack height. Might have to remind members: balance only gets better if we work on it. Focal points matter! If they use the toe down let’s be strategic on placement and remind them to only use it as much as they need. If we use lateral raises or scaption, just keep in mind center of mass is changing and balance may be more difficult.  #singlelegshoulderpress #balanceshoulderpress #balance #otf #otfcoach

Seated Reciprocating Shoulder Press Reciprocating is going to require coordination and stabilization. The seated posting is going to restrict any contribution from the legs as we fatigue. As it reciprocates, it may create some space in the AC joint for some, if there is still pinching we can try neutral grip or his go to a lateral raise. It’s important for us as coaches to demo heavy so they know it should be a struggle. It doesn’t have to be your true weight but it should be heavy. It is also important that we know how to explain how to get setup for heavy safely and teach them even if they don’t need it yet. #seatedreciprocatingshoulderpress #shoulderpress #seatedshoulderpress #orangetheory #otfcoach

coaching_cues: Make sure members don’t bring the leg too far back an they stay as steady as possible through the torso

Members can use the top hand for support but a bolster will help keep the torso upright. We can also use a kickstand position for many as well.  #lowsideplankpendulum #abs #core #sideplankpendulum #otf

exercise_name: Side plank positioning on the BOSU

videos.description: Side plank positions on the BOSU
Side plank positioning on the BOSU #otf #coach #orangetheory #orangetheorycoach #bosu #sideplank #hipdip #shoulderimpingement #activeshoulder

SIMPLE TIP: “Elbows back”

One of the BEST ways to get more out of your Hammer Curls is to be really mindful of your elbows throughout each rep!

Your brain is smart - and will do ANYTHING it can to get you to “rest” during reps of an exercise. And on the Hammer Curl, that rest break happens at the top of each rep when you subconsciously allow your elbows to shift in front of you!

DON’T DO THAT!!

You have to THINK ABOUT IT, and actively keep your elbows back behind you by an inch or two… This LITTLE CHANGE will make it so that you don’t cheat the top of the rep - which is where you want to feel it the most!!

Not much more to say than that… other than the fact that after coaching thousands of fitness classes with 10s of thousands of people at this point… MOST WILL STILL LET THE ELBOWS COME FORWARD. Are you gonna be like them??

Or are you going to do it BETTER?! If you’e cool, you’ll give this a shot! Hope this helps! Have fun!

Love always,
 Coach Austin
.
.
.
#arms #armworkout #bicep #biceps #orangetheoryfitness #otf #tallguys #trainingtall #veganfitness

Alternating Single Arm Snatch There are a lot of technical pieces to this movement, but the best thing we can do as coaches is to simplify it; jump and punch. We want to be clear that this is a hip power movement, not an arm movement. Jump the weight up and punch at the top. Arms do little to no work. We want the dumbbell to move in as straight of a line possible.  Most important is to drop the hips low before jumping the weight up!  #alternatingsinglearmsnatch #dumbellsnatch #singlesarmsnatch #otf #otfcoach

exercise_name: So you’re just gonna GO INTO THE UNKNOWN
coaching_cues:
And you want to make sure you don’t hurt yourself 👍

We keep this exercise SAFE and EFFECTIVE but walking forward and starting at THE BOTTOM
You’ll feel your abs engage, and you’re set to “roll” to the top

videos.description:
So you’re just gonna GO INTO THE UNKNOWN?!

You’re setting yourself up for a disaster if you start the TRX rollout at the TOP!

This is a TOUGH exercise! And you want to make sure you don’t hurt yourself 👍

We keep this exercise SAFE and EFFECTIVE but walking forward and starting at THE BOTTOM!!

Get your arms up, biceps to ears if you can, and then take a small step back! You’ll feel your abs engage, and you’re set to “roll” to the top!

And now you know EXACTLY where to go with each rep! Bingo! Just like that!

TRX exercises like this are always more effective when you start at the end of the range of motion, rather than the start! ✅ Give it a shot!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘

exercise_name: Speed skater lunges and speed skaters with power
coaching_cues: Intention is single leg force and lateral control and balance

videos.description: Speed skater lunges and speed skaters with power.

Speed skater lunges and speed skaters with power. Intention is single leg force and lateral control and balance. As coaches, when we give options individually we have to dog deeper on why are we prescribing the option. What can we do better to help them advance instead of generic options like less weight, limited range of motion 🙄 (those are obvious always). Let’s always think about what the intention of the exercise is and how can we give them small steps to be better everyday. #otf #orangetheory #fitness #coach #coaching #speedskater

coaching_cues: Hips/toes stay pointed forward, lateral power and control
Speed skaters w/power #otf #orangetheory #coach #orangetheorycoach #speedskater #lateralcontrol #force

videos.description:
Speed Skater with Power

A lot of similarities with a speed skater. Hips/toes stay pointed forward, lateral power and control. With power we are adding a little more force, which most likely show with a higher height. We are also reaching, if not touching the ground to load the hip and be more explosive. A great option would be a lateral lunge with reach to overhead to reduce impact. 

#speedskaterwithpower #speedskater #lateralpower #orangetheory #otfcoach

coaching_cues: Especially as we work a slow descent we might need to slightly tip the torso forward for better weight distribution
This option gives them the chance to focus weight in the front leg and a big range of motion while providing them a little more balance and control

videos.description: Front Loaded Contralateral Single Arm Split Squat (Slow) In all split squats we want the feet to be about shoulder width apart. We want majority of weight in the front leg, maybe 70-80% of weight. Especially as we work a slow descent we might need to slightly tip the torso forward for better weight distribution. When it comes to that deliberate slow count, often members push too much weight in the back leg. We need constant reminders to be heavy in the front leg.  It is much more challenging at tempo with up high and away from the hips, if members are having a hard time shifting weight forward, we can always have them use the option of weight down by their side. If they have balance issues, we can always move to ipsilateral loading as well.  #otf #coach #frontloadedcontralateralsinglearmsplitsquatslow #frontloadedcontralateralsinglearmsplitsquat #balance

Goblet Rear Foot Elevated Split Squat Option (Bulgarian Split Squat Option) Often members are hesitant on this movement due to mobility or balance issues. This option gives them the chance to focus weight in the front leg and a big range of motion while providing them a little more balance and control.  #gobletrearfootelevatedsplitsquat #rfess #bulgariansplitsquat #benchgobletrearfootelevatedsplitsquat #balance

exercise_name: Split Squats are so freaking HARD
coaching_cues: Sit on your bench, then fully extend a leg
Place your weight down around the foot, on the ground
Stand up and place your back foot on the bench (preferably top of the foot facing down on the bench)
Drop you back knee to the ground

Grab your weights and get reppin’!

Just my preference

videos.description:
Split Squats are so freaking HARD. I know you agree…

Here’s my 5 step guide to an easier setup and better execution of this brutal exercise:

1. Sit on your bench, then fully extend a leg. That’s how far out you wanna be.

2. Place your weight down around the foot, on the ground.

3. Stand up and place your back foot on the bench (preferably top of the foot facing down on the bench).

4. Drop you back knee to the ground.

5. Grab your weights and get reppin’!

Just my preference. What do you think? How do you set up?

Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#splitsquat #splitsquats #squats #legday #orangetheory #fitnesstips #workouttips #homeworkout #garagegym

exercise_name: Split Squats are so good for you but SO AWKWARD
coaching_cues: Try to think about dropping the back knee to lower you down RATHER than bending the front leg

videos.description:
Split Squats are so good for you but SO AWKWARD!

The trick to feeling less awkward?

Focus LESS on the front leg and MORE on the non-working leg!

Try to think about dropping the back knee to lower you down RATHER than bending the front leg!

This will make it much easier to get the full range that makes this exercise EXCELLENT! Just don’t forget to push through the front leg to get back up! 😉 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#legday #legsworkout #legs #legworkout #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: Split Squats will ALWAYS be HARD

videos.description:
Split Squats will ALWAYS be HARD! But try this…

To make this exercise a lot more manageable, try to put all of your focus into “dropping down the back knee” to help you get to proper depth! If you focus on bending your front leg (the working leg), you’ll be pushed forward and definitely won’t get as low as you should!

When you focus on “dropping the back knee”, THEN pushing the front leg, you’re getting full ROM and will feel a lot stronger with your reps!

✅ Don’t forget about all the variations to try, too! Bodyweight, 1 dumbbell on the shoulder, 1 dumbbell down below, 2 dumbbells, etc! There’s a lot of variety to discover and find out what works best for you!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#legday #legs #legworkout #legsfordays #legsworkout #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #tallfitness

exercise_name: SPRINTING on the rower all comes down to “leg bend”
coaching_cues: Instead of rowing long and efficiently, you need to row QUICK & FAST
Focus on a more intentional lean forward to help you from over-bending your legs, too

videos.description:
SPRINTING on the rower all comes down to “leg bend”!

Instead of rowing long and efficiently, you need to row QUICK & FAST! This happens when we keep the SAME legs-core-arms form but just bend our legs up ~halfway.

Just look at the world record sprint rows… they go at a crazy high stroke rate with a lot less leg bend than you’d have on any row longer than a couple hundred meters.

To help you practice this, make sure you lift your hands vertically a little higher than usual and make sure you drive horizontally while sitting heavy to prevent you from falling off of the rower! Focus on a more intentional lean forward to help you from over-bending your legs, too!

Combine this with a high rate and BIG intention, and you’ll crush your 200m or any other super short sprint row! 💪 Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingispassion #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #hyrox #orangetheoryfitness #orangetheory #rowingworkout #sprint

The old-school cue of “hips back first, then bend the knees” is often more limiting than it is helpful!

It stems from the incorrect premise that having your knees go over your toes is a bad thing. The truth is, it’s NOT!

Is your knees hurt when your knees go over them during a squat, it means the load is too heavy. Simple as that!

To help achieve a better range with your squats, try to think about your hips going back AND your knees bending at the SAME TIME! This specifically optimally utilizes your hips, quads, calves, and tibialis for the most effective and deepest squats!

And again, if you’re struggling with this, DIAL BACK the weight used! Don’t get stuck with shallow form for the rest of your life! Try it!!
.
.
.
#squat #squats #legs #legday #legworkout #orangetheoryfitness
