coaching_cues: And as they are coming up, that’s when you want to “get under them”

videos.description:
Still can’t figure out “cleans”? Try this…

The “Clean” is all about trying to “get under” the weight!

An example I like to use is when a moving box is slipping out of your hands when you carry it and you have to kind of “lift and get under” to regain your hand position.

The clean is kind of the same thing! Think about doing a mini 1/4 squat and jumping. It’s that “jump” that gives the weights momentum upwards. And as they are coming up, that’s when you want to “get under them”.

The key is to use weights that are TOO HEAVY to lift with just your arms. If they aren’t heavy enough, it’s going to feel awkward and you won’t really be getting anything out of the exercise.

This is YOUR TIME TO SHINE and grab weights that you’re not used to grabbing! And from there, just get under those weights like you would if it was a box slipping out of your hands! ;)

Love always,
 Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #otf #powerclean #tallguys #veganfitness #trainingtall

exercise_name: Stop going into the unknown with your TRX Chest Press
coaching_cues: Don’t just “see what happens” — take control from the start and build strength AND confidence on the TRX

videos.description:
Stop going into the unknown with your TRX Chest Press!

If you’re starting this move from the top, you’re guessing your way through the setup — and that’s how form breaks down. Instead, start from the bottom:

✅ Walk yourself forward
✅ Get the handles under your armpits
✅ Elbows out and back
✅ THEN step back and press

Now every rep has a roadmap. You’ll feel more stable, more confident, and get more out of every set. Don’t just “see what happens” — take control from the start and build strength AND confidence on the TRX!

NOW, DO IT! 🤘
.
.
.
#trx #trxsuspensiontraining #trxworkout #trxtraining #chest #chestpress #pushup #orangetheoryfitness

Stroke RATE 👀 vs. Stroke POWER 💥

In rowing, it’s important to understand that the stroke rate ALONE doesn’t determine the intensity or split/500m of your row!

You can row HARDER with more POWER at lower stroke rates to achieve the SAME kind of split/500m you’d see at high stroke rates!

The challenge in rowing is finding the right BALANCE between power and rate to most efficiently get you to the finish line as fast as possible! Something that is much easier said than done!

Use this as a reference to remind you that sometimes more stroke rates IS NOT something you need, that perhaps taking each stroke harder and with more power can get you to where you wanna be! 💪

This is also how you make ANY stroke rate, fast or slow, challenging! 🙌

Come row with me on Just Row! 🚣‍♂️

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #ergo #hyrox #hyroxtraining🏃🏻‍♂️ #rowingrelated #rowingworkout

coaching_cues: You want to AVOID having your thumbs face each other, because this causes your elbows to flare out - reducing ROM and increasing injury risk
Remember, you’ve got your whole life to get strong

videos.description:
Struggling with Pushups? Try this! 👇

Angling your hands slightly can work wonders to make you feel stronger and more confident with pushups!

Simply turning your hands out (thumbs more forward), you naturally align your elbows, arms, and shoulders into a stronger and safer position!

You want to AVOID having your thumbs face each other, because this causes your elbows to flare out - reducing ROM and increasing injury risk!

And of course don’t forget to scale down your pushups to your knees, or even hands on a bench, to help you work full ROM and build strength over time!

Remember, you’ve got your whole life to get strong! But that has to start by doing things RIGHT, first! 💪

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #pushupchallenge #chestday #calisthenics #homeworkout #orangetheoryfitness

Struggling with rowing form? Try this…

✨ NOT strapping your feet in! ✨

Sounds crazy, I know.. but hear me out!

Beginners rely on the footsteps way too much - which allows for compensations and poor form to sneak through, even when you’re TRYING to use good form!

✅ Straps are great for racing and hard efforts. But outside of that, you could do without them when you’re learning!

⚠️ BE CAREFUL when you try this and start slow… try this drill at your own risk! It will essentially ELIMINATE 90% of form problems if you can manage it!

Full video on YT, just search “Just Row”! 💪

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowingform #rowingworkout #erg #ergworkout #hyrox #rowinglife #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: The way we traditionally hold a weight for the Sumo Squat makes it challenging to get the most effective depth that we SHOULD be aiming for each rep

videos.description:
The way we traditionally hold a weight for the Sumo Squat makes it challenging to get the most effective depth that we SHOULD be aiming for each rep.  Literally the dumbbell is IN THE WAY!

That’s why I will always recommend holding it by the BAR instead of on the end. Holding it this way gives you more room to get lower - but of course don’t smash your nuts! Just simply tilt the side facing you down slightly to avoid hitting yourself on the way up each rep.

Plus, if you’ve got sweaty hands like me, this version of holding the dumbbell makes you feel a lot more confident in going heavy!

A simple change that makes a big difference! Give this one a shot and let me know if you found the advice helpful!

Now, do it!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#sumosquat #squat #squats #legday #legworkout #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: The #1 drill every rowing Olympian has ever done… and why you should do it too

✅ Try to keep a fluid sequence
✅ Think “push, swing, pull” on the drive

This drill is great for all fitness levels
Keep your feet WIDE

videos.description:
The #1 drill every rowing Olympian has ever done… and why you should do it too!

✨ The Pick Drill ✨

This drill at its core helps you develop the basic sequencing of a proper rowing stroke.

It starts with ~60 seconds of “arms only”. This means only extending your arms out and pulling them in. During this part you should:
✅ Hold your legs flat, quads flexed
✅ Hold your layback, abs braced
✅ Pull in below the chest

The second part is ~60 seconds of “arms + torso”… sometimes called “arms and core”. Same thing.
During this part you should:
✅ Keep your legs flat, quads flexed
✅ Focus on a big hip swing, then pull

Finally, finish the drill by adding in the legs for a full stroke…
At this point, your stroke should feel a lot more purposeful and more efficient!
✅ Try to keep a fluid sequence
✅ Think “push, swing, pull” on the drive

This drill is great for all fitness levels. If you struggle with good rowing form, give this a go as a warmup before your next rowing session!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingispassion #rowingmachine #erg #hyrox #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #rowinglife #rowingrelated

✅ The # 1 BURPEE TIP to feel more confident and stay safe!

Keep your feet WIDE! This is an instant game-changer and might actually make you start loving burpees more!!

When your feet are wide, you can SQUAT DOWN each rep rather than fold over like you would if your feet were close!

If your feet aren’t apart, you’ll simply increase your risk of hurting yourself through a lower back strain - especially if you don’t train spinal flexion on a regular basis!

So, avoid injury and feel more confident with burpees by simply taking your feet WIDE! Now, do it! 💪
.
.
.

exercise_name: The Arnold Press is not what you think
coaching_cues: You will feel more fluid, strong, and in control when you can make it one motion of a “twist and press” rather than piecing it out

videos.description:
The Arnold Press is not what you think...

It’s not a step-by-step “turn and press” motion!

This exercise was designed originally to simply get more stretch on the shoulders by bringing the weights in front of you and down more. Whether or not it ACTUALLY stretches the shoulders more is up for debate, but that’s besides the point.

The point is, this motion is simply ONE MOTION! “Twist and press” at once, then reset. That’s literally it!

You will feel more fluid, strong, and in control when you can make it one motion of a “twist and press” rather than piecing it out.

Be sure to keep your chest nice and proud as you bring the weights in front to get as much shoulder stretch as you can and stabilize the press overhead.. you don’t wanna slouch on this one!! And I’m telling you this as a fellow sloucher tall guy!!

Hope this can clear the air a bit and makes sense. What are your thoughts on this exercise?

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#shoulders #shoulderworkout #shoulder #arnold #arnoldpress #orangetheoryfitness #veganfitness #tallfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: The BEST cue to help you “feel it” on the rower

videos.description:
The BEST cue to help you “feel it” on the rower?

PUSH, then pull!

The rowing machine is NOT the same type of rowing as you’d use for weight training. The rowing machine is cardio that simulates the sport of rowing! 🚣‍♀️

And the main action is in the legs, not your back! PUSH your legs first, then swing back through the hips, and pull AFTER all of that!

Push first, then pull! Watch your rowing performance skyrocket! 🚀

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowingispassion #rowinglife #rowingrelated #rowingworkout #erg #crew #orangetheoryfitness #hyrox #crossfit

exercise_name: The Chest Press is inevitable on your fitness journey…
coaching_cues:
✅ Turn the dumbbells diagonally
✅ Think: “elbows down”

You don’t need to tuck them in or anything, but don’t let them flare out

videos.description:
The Chest Press is inevitable on your fitness journey…

And I know you’re trying to stay SAFE and EFFECTIVE with your workouts! So here’s my advice for the Chest Press…

Think about your elbows going “down” rather than going “out” when you do your reps. Don’t hold the weights like a barbell! There’s no need and it’s only going to increase your risk of injury while most likely reducing your range of motion!

✅ Turn the dumbbells diagonally
✅ Think: “elbows down”

You don’t need to tuck them in or anything, but don’t let them flare out!

This will set you up for a pain-free and effective experience for this super common gym exercise! 👍 Hope it can help you or someone else out!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#chestday #chestworkout #chestpress #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #tallfitness

exercise_name: The Deadlift Cue That Changes The Game

videos.description:
The Deadlift Cue That Changes The Game!!

“Butt Back”. That’s it!

While keeping the weights close, instead of thinking about “bending over” or “reaching down” or “hinging” or anything like that… simply think about your butt pushing BACK behind you!

THIS will hinge you over while keeping a strong neutral spine - helping you feel this exercise more where we want it (glutes and hamstrings) and less where we don’t (the lower back)!

Seriously, if you haven’t tried this cue out yet, you’re missing out!

What deadlift cues help you the most?? 🤔
.
.
.

exercise_name: The Double Crunch is a MUST for the abs
coaching_cues: The Double Crunch is a MUST for the abs
If you want to step it up, try it with a dumbbell, but no need to go super heavy

videos.description:
The Double Crunch is a MUST for the abs!

This one is a true “bang for your buck” exercise as it targets your upper, middle, and lower abs all at once!

You can do it anywhere, too!

Start without a weight - trust me, you don’t need it right away!! With hands behind your EARS (not your head), crunch your shoulders and upper back off of the ground as you “scoop in” your legs!

The idea is to make yourself like a “roly poly” (you know, the little bug?)! This might seem like “bad posture”, but rounding the spine like this is how you really work the abs!

If you want to step it up, try it with a dumbbell, but no need to go super heavy! 😉
.
.
.
#core #abs #absworkout #sixpack #sixpackworkout #orangetheoryfitness #veganfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: The dumbbell clean (technically a hang power clean)

videos.description: The dumbbell clean (technically a hang power clean).

The dumbbell clean (technically a hang power clean). Intention is to express power at the hip on an external load. Use full extention to maximize power output, then retract the hips to sink under the load at a point of it’s apex. Then using the legs to stand the load upright. While there is a shrug and an aggressive extension at the ankles, we generally don’t cue that much. When athletes think about those features it tends to slow the movement and make it choppy.  KISS principle. Stuck to simple cuing; “Jump the weight up and land under,” “jump under the weights,” “tall before you get small”… Be as creative as you want and find something that jives with your speech. #otf #orangetheory #cleans #power #weightlifting

exercise_name: The inability to STRAIGHTEN YOUR ARMS OVERHEAD is getting out of control
coaching_cues: The inability to STRAIGHTEN YOUR ARMS OVERHEAD is getting out of control

videos.description:
The inability to STRAIGHTEN YOUR ARMS OVERHEAD is getting out of control!!

Test it for yourself... can you truly LOCK OUT your elbows and with straight arms bring your biceps behind your ears without arching your back? 

You’ll probably surprise yourself and realize that you have to bend your elbows a little bit or arch your back to get there... NOT GOOD!

You ever see those grandmas/grandpas that have the permanently hunched posture?? That is what happens if we don’t train our strength and mobility for overhead reaching!

GOOD NEWS: The Pullover is a great exercise that addresses both strength and mobility of this motion!

When you do this exercise, keep your arms as straight as possible while going EXTRA SLOW as you stretch each rep.

When you feel the stretch, go for an inch more of stretch!! THAT is what’s going to push your strength and mobility to the next level! Of course make sure you’re using a weight that’s challenging, but not too crazy!! You don’t need a lot of weight to make this work!

Most people miss out on the benefits because of quick reps that don’t get the full stretch!

SLOW DOWN and GET THE STRETCH each rep, and you’ll be standing tall and proud all the way through old age!!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#flexibility #flexibilitytraining #mobility #mobilitytraining #pullover #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: The Only Deadlift Tip You Need

videos.description:
The Only Deadlift Tip You Need! “BUTT BACK”!

When doing dumbbell deadlifts, the cue “butt back” reigns as the single most effective cue for a proper “hinge” in my opinion! After trying to explain the deadlift a dozen different ways, after thousands of clients, “butt back” seems to be what sticks the most for folks!

This prevents you from “reaching” which can round your lower back unintentionally! “Butt back” keeps your spine neutral and helps you properly load your glutes and hamstrings!

If you’ve struggled with deadlifts in the past, this is the cue for you! Hope it helps and makes sense!!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#deadlift #deadlfits #rdl #orangetheoryfitness #plantpower #veganfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: The part people MISS OUT on with the Biceps Curl
coaching_cues: This means that when we do the Biceps Curl, we don’t just want to “curl up”
We want to be INTENTIONALLY trying to twist out as we curl up

videos.description:
The part people MISS OUT on with the Biceps Curl...

It’s the twist!! Your biceps don’t just flex and contract when you bend your arm (flex your arm), but they also contract MORE when you rotate your wrists outwards!

Don’t believe me? Look down at your arm right now and try it!

This means that when we do the Biceps Curl, we don’t just want to “curl up”!

We want to be INTENTIONALLY trying to twist out as we curl up... I like to think “pinkies to shoulders” to emphasize this!

You might not think that this does much, but WOW do you feel a big difference!

Next time you’re working on your biceps, think about “pinkies to shoulders” and really twist those things as you curl, and I promise your biceps will feel it more than ever!

Let me know what you think!!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#biceps #bicep #armworkout #bicepworkout #orangetheoryfitness #veganfitness #tallfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: The reason why you hate burpees…
coaching_cues: I’m telling ya… having your feet close together makes burpees 1000% harder than they need to be

videos.description:
The reason why you hate burpees…

Is because of your FEET! I’m telling ya… having your feet close together makes burpees 1000% harder than they need to be!

Feet close = rounding down, a longer way to go to get up and down, and doesn’t make you athletic!

Feet WIDE is the way to go! This puts you into an athletic position to squat down while being able to get down and up quicker with less distance to travel! It also protects your lower back, too!

Trust me, you’re going to feel WAY more confident in burpees and PROBABLY hate them a little less! 😉
.
.
.
#burpee #burpees #calisthenics #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #veganfitness

exercise_name: The Step DOWN might help your knees better than the traditional Step Up
coaching_cues: That’s because the focus isn’t on going up, it’s on lowering down with as much control as possible

videos.description:
The Step DOWN might help your knees better than the traditional Step Up!

That’s because the focus isn’t on going up, it’s on lowering down with as much control as possible!

It’s this focus on the eccentric (the lowering phase) which really helps your body learn how to properly use your glutes in conjunction with your quads! This helps to build strength and stabilization in the knee joint which can help you combat joint pain down the line!

This exercise is SLEPT ON!! Super helpful when you focus on lowering down to at least a 2 second count! Try not to just flop down and up off of the bench! Take your time and you’ll really reap the benefits from this one! 💪 NOW, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.

exercise_name: The Sumo Squat is NOT About Squatting WIDE
coaching_cues: Your stance SHOULD be a little bit wider than usual - but we just don’t want to overdo it

videos.description:
The Sumo Squat is NOT About Squatting WIDE!

If your focus is simply on taking a “wider stance” than a normal squat, you’re probably going too wide… and if you’re too wide, you’re not gonna be able to get as low as you SHOULD be getting!

The golden rule for Sumo Squats is to focus more on the leg and foot ANGLE! Make it an intention to turn your legs out a lot so that you can get those inner thighs workin’!

Your stance SHOULD be a little bit wider than usual - but we just don’t want to overdo it! Think “slightly wider than normal”!

With these tips in mind, you’re going to get lower and get more out of each rep in your workouts! Hope this helps! Happy Squatting!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#squat #squats #sumosquat #legday #legworkout #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: The TRICK to feeling balanced that no one talks about…
coaching_cues: You STILL need to practice and you WILL still fumble and fall sometimes - but you’ll FIGURE IT OUT if you stay persistent

videos.description:
The TRICK to feeling balanced that no one talks about…

With Single Leg Deadlifts, you can feel A LOT more confident with your balance when you STOP focusing on trying to hinge over so much and instead START focusing on “lifting the back leg more”!

This simple cue helps to create more stability between your upper and lower body. Without it, your back leg drags behind and makes balancing feel impossible!

🫵 DO NOT FORGET that balance is a skill set like anything else. You STILL need to practice and you WILL still fumble and fall sometimes - but you’ll FIGURE IT OUT if you stay persistent! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘

exercise_name: The TRX Single Leg Squat is SO TOUGH and most mess it up…
coaching_cues: This also means that you should use a “kickstand leg”

videos.description:
The TRX Single Leg Squat is SO TOUGH and most mess it up…

And by “mess it up”, I mean “leaning back as you squat down”!

It’s important to remember that a single leg squat is just like a normal squat - your torso leans forward slightly as you drop down!

This means that when holding TRX, make sure your arms stay bend and angle up as you squat down, rather than straighten as you fall back!

This also means that you should use a “kickstand leg”! This foot allows you to offset some of your bodyweight away from just the working leg - making it so that you can scale the exercise back as much as you need to in order to match your fitness level!

These tips hopefully will help you better conquer this exercise! Good luck! 👍

Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#trx #trxtraining #trxworkout #trxtrainer #squat #pistolsquat #legday #legworkout #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: The “Reverse Crunch” is more than it might seem…

videos.description:
The “Reverse Crunch” is more than it might seem…

For many, it doesn’t feel like it does anything as you curl your knees in and out…

BUT if you’re someone who sneakily puts their hands under their 🍑 during Leg Raises, this exercise is actually a great way to help you STOP doing that!

You put your hands under your 🍑 because you don’t have the strength in your abs to lift straight legs (sorry lol). But did you know that the more you bend your legs, the easier it is on your abs??

So, the Reverse Crunch is essentially the most modified way to do a Leg Raise! Use this exercise to experiment with your leg angle! 📐 Bend them less as you practice “scooping in your hips”, to find the limit where your abs are working hard and you don’t need your hands under your bootycheeks!

Don’t underestimate this one! And straighten your legs a bit on it for more challenge! 😉 If you still don’t feel it work, go for the Leg Raise! Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#abs #absworkout #abworkout #core #coreworkout #corestrength #sixpack #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: This drill will fix one of the BIGGEST mistakes in your rowing
coaching_cues: Careful when you do this, you’ll want to lean back
But if you can do Legs Only, and then transition to full strokes, you can achieve the power in the swing as you lean AFTER the leg drive

videos.description:
This drill will fix one of the BIGGEST mistakes in your rowing.

And that mistake is leaning back early. Or “opening up” early with the torso.

I have coached 1000s on rowing form personally, and time and time again, this early lean back is one of the biggest issues preventing folks from maximizing their rowing potential.

Doing the “Legs Only” drill is a great way to fix it. All you have to do is take a few strokes (usually 1 or 2 minutes worth between 16-20 s/m) with ONLY pushing your legs back.

Careful when you do this, you’ll want to lean back. But try not to! The lean in the rowing stroke happens AFTER the legs. Proper power and form comes from pushing the legs first, then right as the legs near full extension, you swing back with your torso and pull in the arms.

If you lean early, there is no power in the swing. But if you can do Legs Only, and then transition to full strokes, you can achieve the power in the swing as you lean AFTER the leg drive!

This takes TONS of practice, but why not give this drill a shot for a minute or two before your next workout to help you improve your form!

Questions? Let me know! And thanks @eltgilmore for the great demo. And thanks @keepitjorganic for the great shirt.

Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #rowingmachineworkout

exercise_name: This is PROBABLY why your shoulders hurt when you do the Chest Press…

videos.description:
This is PROBABLY why your shoulders hurt when you do the Chest Press…

Flaring your elbows out to the sides is the problem.
Holding the dumbbells horizontally like a barbell is the ROOT of the problem.

Holding your dumbbells diagonally and letting your elbows go down at a 45 degree angle, rather than out at a 90 degree angle is the solution.

You’ll also feel stronger and get a better range of motion. 
Simple as that.

Now, DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘

coaching_cues: If you’re bad at push-ups, you need to start “screwing” your hands into the ground

videos.description:
THIS IS THE ONE, FOLKS!! 🔥 The # 1 PUSH-UP TIP!

If you’re bad at push-ups, you need to start “screwing” your hands into the ground!

It’s like you’re trying to twist them out WITHOUT them actually moving! This creates stability at the shoulder joints and prevents your energy from leaking outside of what you need for pure pushing strength!

Without worrying about ANY other cue, try this method out and notice what a difference it makes for you! You might actually surprise yourself with how well you can do your pushups after you try this!! NOW, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.

exercise_name: This is the WORST cue I’ve heard from coaches…
coaching_cues: For most rowing efforts, you want to focus on relaxing your arms “down and out”

videos.description:
This is the WORST cue I’ve heard from coaches…

Listen, your arms punching out works in only one scenario on the rower: and that’s when you’re full blown SPRINTING!

For most rowing efforts, you want to focus on relaxing your arms “down and out”. Just watch the pros doing steady state… they all allow the arms to go down low to the thighs on the return.

This keeps your form in check and forces proper sequencing (arms, torso, legs) while allowing you to focus on putting your energy where it matters most: the drive! 💪

Don’t punch your arms out like a robot! 🤖

Austin 🤘
.
.
.

exercise_name: TOE STRIKING is just as bad as HEEL STRIKING
coaching_cues: When we run, we should NOT be trying to land on a specific part of our foot
The focus should instead be on seeing how fast you can pick your foot up after it lands

videos.description:
TOE STRIKING is just as bad as HEEL STRIKING!

The issue isn’t which part of your foot you’re landing on… the issue is TRYING to land somewhere on your foot!

When we run, we should NOT be trying to land on a specific part of our foot! It’s the WRONG focus!

The focus should instead be on seeing how fast you can pick your foot up after it lands. Because as soon as you lift your foot off of the ground, your other foot naturally falls to keep you from face-planting the ground!

When you can focus on “pull-pull-pull” rather than “land land land”, you’re able to naturally boost your cadence and improve your efficiency! It’s simply going to make your running FEEL BETTER!

Stop focusing on how you land and start focusing on how you PULL! ❤️ 🏃‍♂️
.
.
.
#run #running #runner #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #treadmill #orangetheoryfitness #tallfitness #tallguys

Tomorrow we are going to see the seated uppercut to stand. These same stance concepts apply. I recorded thia after the workout Jun7th, but it doesnt repeat 🤪 #otf #coach #seatedtostand #squatstance #goodbase #stance

Torso Rotations, Torso Twists, Russian Twists… whatever you wanna call them - STOP using your arms so much!

You’d think that having the weight held away from your body would demand more out of your core muscles, but we simply overcompensate and use our arms more instead! This takes away from the rotational core strength we’re trying to build!

The fix is easy! Instead of holding the weight away from you, STICK IT ON YOUR UPPER CHEST! Hold it on yourself tightly and even though it seems like it would make it easier, it makes it WAY MORE challenging on your core than you could have imagined!

Try “elbows out”, too! This gives you a “twist goal” to shoot for with each rep… slow and steady rotation to the side, aiming for “elbow between the legs” if you can!

Take it slow, stick it to your body… basically the opposite of everything you’re probably doing already! Give this a shot and feel the difference! 💪

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#core #coreworkout #absworkout #abs #abworkout #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #tallfitness

coaching_cues: Keep switching, and you’re now “running in place”
One of the biggest misconceptions about running is that you should be moving forward by pushing off of your foot each stride
This means simply pulling your foot up - not a knee drive or butt kick, just pull your foot up
Most times they are holding on for their safety… but they usually just need to slow their speed to something they can control

videos.description:
Whether it’s the Orange Everest Challenge or any other incline-focused treadmill routine, you gotta face reality…

Inclines are TOUGH! And if you struggle to keep any sort of jog or run at any incline over 10%, it’s usually because of your form! Inclines FORCE us to think about our form, because they prevent overstriding!

Over-striding happens when we’re thinking about “stepping forward” with each stride! This is WRONG!! And it will always slow you down…

On the treadmill especially, we can think of our running as “falling while pulling”. Essentially, all we have to do is get our feet off of the ground, fall forward from our hips, and let gravity do the rest!

Lifting off our feet gets us into our “pose position”. Find your “pose” by simply pulling your foot up. Not butt kicking, not knee driving, just pulling it up under you with minimal effort.

Now, simply switch and do the same thing on the alternate foot. Keep switching, and you’re now “running in place”. Now, fall forward from your hips (not your torso), and you’ll be running forward!

You’ll find that inclines become A LOT more manageable when you give this some practice - but it’s gonna take some work!! Do your best!!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #running #runner #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #treadmill #treadmillworkout #orangetheoryfitness

One of the biggest misconceptions about running is that you should be moving forward by pushing off of your foot each stride.

But if you do this, all it does is make you step further out in front - and you can’t move forward when your leg is out in front of you! Plus it causes you to strike hard on your heel and can increase your risk of developing joint issues in your running journey!

It’s NOT about trying to step forward or “push off”! Instead, focus on finding your runners pose!

This means simply pulling your foot up - not a knee drive or butt kick, just pull your foot up!

Then, pull the other foot up, and practice switching back and forth between your feet with as little effort as possible!

Now all you have to do is use gravity to help you move forward by “falling” forward from your hips while pulling and switching your feet!

Doing this will help you land with your feet under you more, which prevents over-striding that only hurts you and slows you down!

It takes some practice, but it’s worthwhile to practice and build up the technique! Soon you’ll be running pain free and as fast as ever! 💪

Now do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #runner #running #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #runninglife #treadmill #jogging #orangetheoryfitness

Holding on when on the treadmill changes you gait cycle by limiting your rotation. Members also tend to lean back and lean while holding on. Most times they are holding on for their safety… but they usually just need to slow their speed to something they can control.

⚠️ FACTS: If RUNNING HURTS your knees, hips, or ankles… it’s not you, it’s your stride!!

That heel-smashing, overreaching run form? Yeah, that’s why you’re hurting… it’s this idea of “stepping forward” that is pushing your running BACKWARDS in progress!

Instead of trying to “step forward” — start thinking:

PULL. SWITCH. FALL.
💥 Pull your foot up
🔁 Switch your feet
⬇️ Fall with gravity
That’s it. Your body already knows how to land — just get out of its way!

👟 Switching away from big squishy shoes and into more minimalist shoes or zero drop shoes like @altrarunning is a great way to naturally reinforce these mechanics, too! 💪

💡 Try this next time you run and FEEL the difference.
Say goodbye to joint pain - and MAYBE just MAYBE you’ll actually enjoying running and finally feel confident in your form! NOW, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.

exercise_name: Treadmill inclines are NOT hard once you’ve got the technique down
coaching_cues: Like flat road running, we want to do our best to not try to “step forward”
Keep these in mind and you’ll crush any incline that comes your way

videos.description:
Treadmill inclines are NOT hard once you’ve got the technique down!

Like flat road running, we want to do our best to not try to “step forward”. Especially up a hill, that’s a recipe for disaster.

✅ Instead, try to think about “pulling your feet up” while trying to “fall” into the hill!

✅ Pump your arms forward and back - not side to side!

✅ Keep tall by trying to think about the crown of your head being “pulled to the sky”

✅ Squeeze your butt forward to prevent binging over and causing back pain!

Keep these in mind and you’ll crush any incline that comes your way! Now, DO IT!
.
.
.
#treadmill #treadmillworkout #run #running #runner #runningworkout #jog #jogging #powerwalking #cardio #inclines

exercise_name: TRX Biceps Curls are a great alternative to weight training your biceps
coaching_cues: You do NOT want to “curl in the wrists” as you curl your arms
Keep these tips in mind and get after it

videos.description:
TRX Biceps Curls are a great alternative to weight training your biceps!

A couple things to keep in mind with this one:

First, make sure you curl to your HEAD rather than to your chest! ✅ Keep your elbows high to make this happen and REALLY feel it in your biceps! 💪

Second, make sure to RELAX the wrists!! You do NOT want to “curl in the wrists” as you curl your arms! If you do, you’ll end up using more of your forearms to curl up, which takes away tension from the biceps alone! ✅ Think: “knuckles down” to help with this!

Keep these tips in mind and get after it! 👍 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#trx #trxtraining #trxworkout #biceps #arms #armworkout #orangetheory

exercise_name: Using your arms to help you up on Sit-ups is NOT CHEATING
coaching_cues: This is where our muscles flex to control the “lengthening” of those muscles to lower you back down
But if you just GIVE UP because you don’t want to use your arms to get you up, you’ll never build the strength
Just control the way down, too

videos.description:
Using your arms to help you up on Sit-ups is NOT CHEATING!

In fact, it’s one of the best tools you can use to help you get stronger at sit-ups if you really struggle with them!

That’s because your arms help you out on the “concentric” phase of the sit-up (the actual sitting up part). THIS is where our muscle strength is at its weakest, and that’s why having the arms to help is a great option!

But that’s because the REAL STRENGTH is going to be gained on the eccentric part of the sit-up (lowering back down from the top). This is where our muscles flex to control the “lengthening” of those muscles to lower you back down. THIS is where our muscles are the STRONGEST, and as long as you lower down slowly and keep tension, you’re going to build a ton of abs strength that will EVENTUALLY make it so you don’t have to sue your arms to get up!

But if you just GIVE UP because you don’t want to use your arms to get you up, you’ll never build the strength! So, use those arms!! Just control the way down, too! 👍 Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#abs #absworkout #abworkout #core #coreworkout #sixpack #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: Want to run better? Improve your CADENCE

videos.description:
Want to run better? Improve your CADENCE!

The higher your cadence, the lighter impact you’ll have each step! This can help you prevent injuries, prevent over-striding, and improve your efficiency!

While there might not be a “perfect” cadence, aiming for as close to 180+ is a good place to start - even if you’re a taller human like myself!

You can practice this by running in place to a metronome! Literally just search “metronome” and you can use it right on the first page! Once you can pull and switch your feet without moving forward, try to do it at a slow speed!

It takes a ton of practice, but you e got your whole life to get fast! 😎 Give this a shot and let me know how it goes!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘

exercise_name: What does a wood chopper work

videos.description: What does a wood chopper work?
What does a wood chopper work? #otf #orangetheory #coach #core #woodchopper #momentum

What does “good running” even mean?

Well, it’s not “stepping forward”. If you’re trying to push off your back leg and stride forward, you’re simply “putting on the brakes” and slowing yourself down!

Landing your foot out in front of you in any way means that you aren’t moving forward!

Instead of thinking of running as “strides forward”, try to use the @posemethod !

Simply practice “pulling” your feet under you! Pull your foot, pull the other foot, back and forth switching with as little effort as possible!

Then, all you have to do is fall forward with gravity as you pull and switch! This definitely takes practice, but when you do this, you run with LESS effort and less “putting on the brakes”!

Whether it’s the treadmill, flat road, inclines… you gotta pull, switch, and fall to run efficiently!

Who taught you how to run, anyway?

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #runner #running #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #marathon #orangetheoryfitness

What’s a GOOD 1-Mile time? 🤔

This is a ridiculous question. Too many factors play into your mile - like age, height, weight, gender, experience, etc.

There is no “good” mile time objectively!

A GOOD mile time is subjective and specific to YOU. If you can improve your last mile you did, that’s a GOOD mile - wouldn’t you say???

Study your previous mile time. Set your speed to beat it by 1 second. Hold the speed from start to finish and give it all you’ve got in the last 0.1!

Now, go get your GOOD mile time!! 😉

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#mile #run #runner #running #runningform #runners #runningmotivation #orangetheoryfitness #themile

exercise_name: What’s THE BEST type of Pushup to help you get better
coaching_cues: If you TRULY want to get better at push-ups, you gotta start lifting your hands off of the ground
It’s a fool-proof way to ensure full range, which is going to be the biggest driver to help you get better over time
It actually takes a bit of a stretch to get there, so you need to be INTENTIONAL to do it

videos.description:
What’s THE BEST type of Pushup to help you get better?

It’s DEFINITELY the Hand-Release Push-up! Normal push-ups sometimes just won’t cut it.. especially when it cuts our range of motion short and puts our shoulders in a strenuous position!

If you TRULY want to get better at push-ups, you gotta start lifting your hands off of the ground! This means that you lower down as SLOWLY as you can, FULLY lay down, then lift your hands off of the ground, then set them back down before pushing back up!

It’s a fool-proof way to ensure full range, which is going to be the biggest driver to help you get better over time!

HOWEVER... you can’t just randomly lift and put your hands back down! You can actually put MORE strain on your shoulders if you aren’t putting your hands in the right spot!

Where’s the right spot? Right BELOW the line of your shoulders! It actually takes a bit of a stretch to get there, so you need to be INTENTIONAL to do it!

But when you do, you’ll REALLY get the most out of your pushups!

Give this a shot and watch your push up strength really go up over time!! 💪

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#pushup #pushups #chestday #chestworkout #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit

exercise_name: Which SHOES should YOU wear to run better
coaching_cues:
Which SHOES should YOU wear to run better?

It’s a hot topic

videos.description:
Which SHOES should YOU wear to run better?

It’s a hot topic! 🔥 But here’s my opinion!

Cushioned shoes are great because they provide, well, CUSHION to make running less impactful. ⚠️ The problem is having my a big cushion allows you to get away with BAD FORM and over-striding!

Minimalist / flat shoes have no support and force you to run more naturally (like you would if you were barefoot running). This helps you improve your run form by preventing over-striding!

⚠️ The problem with minimalist shoes is that you have to start off WAY SLOWER than you’re used to, because your useful running musculature in your calves and ankles need LOTS OF TIME to develop and get used to running properly, especially if you’ve been running in Brooks / Hokas / Nikes / Adidas / On Clouds etc your entire life!

✅ Practicing slower, shorter runs in minimal shoes can help you improve your form!

👉 Then you can put on your cushioned shoes for your longer/harder runs for that extra support while still running with good form!

👇What are your thoughts on running shoes? Let me know! Hope this was helpful!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#running #runner #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #runninglife #run #orangetheoryfitness

Why floor presses? Powerlifters use it to improve weak sticking points usually the lockout. It reduces range of motion so can go heavier and easier on the shoulder joint. Also, the pause takes away the stretch reflex.

exercise_name: Why MOST PEOPLE Can’t Do TRX Pull-ups…
coaching_cues: You MUST keep your legs bent as you pull forward

videos.description:
Why MOST PEOPLE Can’t Do TRX Pull-ups…

Yes, it’s a complicated exercise - but you are PLENTY strong enough to do it! It’s simply about technique!

And the biggest technique issue most face is straightening the legs as you pull forward. If your legs straighten, you GET STUCK!

You MUST keep your legs bent as you pull forward. And that’s literally the fix. Think of yourself like a roly-poly… ball yourself up as you pull forward… “pull and roll”!

Then just practice the 4 step sequence! Squat, lean, “pull & roll”, and stand!

This exercise does NOT have to be so difficult- you’re just making it hard on yourself when you don’t have to!

Hope this advice helps and you can feel more confident with your pull-ups! 💪

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#pullup #pullups #trx #trxworkout #trxtraining #orangetheoryfitness #orangetheory #tallfitness #veganfitness

exercise_name: Yooo WHY does everyone hate burpees
coaching_cues:
But MOST people make burpees soooo much harder than they need to be…

And it’s all because of your FEET

videos.description:
Yooo WHY does everyone hate burpees??

They aren’t THAT tough!! But MOST people make burpees soooo much harder than they need to be…

And it’s all because of your FEET!

That’s right! Your FEET are the problem! And your feet are most likely TOO CLOSE together!

When your feet are close, you have a lot further down to travel to get to the ground, and it’ll involve you rounding your back in an unathletic position!

If you SIMPLY just take your feet out WIDE, you reduce the distance to the ground, and you allow yourself to SQUAT down athletically, which protects your back, makes you more athletic and explosive with your reps!

Who would’ve thought your feet were the reason why burpees are so frustrating! Try wide feet and let me know if it changes your mind on the burpee! 😉

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#burpee #burpees #burpeechallenge #crossfit #otf #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys #veganfitness

exercise_name: You don’t HAVE to “stretch” to get flexible

videos.description:
You don’t HAVE to “stretch” to get flexible!

In fact, you can gain MOST of your flexibility through your STRENGTH work!

Getting more flexibility in your inner thighs can happen simply from being more intentional with SUMO SQUATS!

Instead of angling your feet out, it’s all about taking a wider stance! I know, this contradicts things I’ve said in the past - I’m not afraid to admit that! But you’ve GOTTA try this!!

The idea is to keep your feet pointing FORWARD rather than turned out while using the concept of “screwing your feet” into the ground to stabilize your knees and work on the inner thighs!

Take your time lowering down, and really try to find a slightly uncomfortable stretch at the bottom position. Bonus points for a little pause at the bottom!

THIS type of specifics stretch-based strength training is what’s going to help you get more flexible without sitting around to stretch all the time.

Give it a shot! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#stretch #stretching #flexibility #mobility #strength #squat #squats #legday #legsworkout #legworkout

You have your ENTIRE LIFE to “go fast”!

When I hear comments like “that hurts”, all it’s telling me is that you’re doing too much.

The running form I talk about in this video can be done starting at 0 MPH and I KNOW that everyone that has working legs can practice this slow, and steady, at 0 MPH. FROM THERE, you build up over time!

Just like learning any new skill, you have to REGRESS and DROP YOUR EGO so you can practice, learn, and actually allow your body to build up the ability to handle the effort and skill!

I promise… you HAVE TIME!! Take your time my friends!! That’s how we stay injury-free! 💪

exercise_name: You were taught how to row like a ROBOT

videos.description:
You were taught how to row like a ROBOT! 🤖 

It’s not about “punching out” the arms, then core, then legs. It’s so robotic, feels awful, and is not sustainable for any row longer than a couple minutes!

Instead of “arms” punching out, try think about “relaxing down and out”... the sequence is still arms first, but its not “ARMS”... do you see what I mean? Relax them down and out and let them guide low to your thighs... as they near full extension, start to lean forward using your core and the MOMENT you pass over your knees, gently bend them and start compressing your legs as you finish your lean forward!

THIS is how you make the rowing stroke feel smooth, sustainable, and efficient while staying true to the “arms core legs” mantra.

I challenge you to see how smooth you can truly make your rowing stroke feel. Be a bit more relaxed with your sequencing and you’re going to find that it works wonders to keep you strong throughout any row!

Hope this helps! Let me know if you have any rowing questions down below!

Love always,

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#rowing #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #rowingform #rowingmachineworkout #orangetheoryfitness #tallfitness #tallguys #veganfitness

Your best 1-mile strategy:

✨ One pace wins the race! ✨ That’s the magic!

Going up and down with your speed is wasted energy and is great for TRAINING, but not great for race day!

Stick as close to one speed as you can to be as efficient as possible! Your best friend is the arrow keys at your waist level on the treadmill to give you small 0.1 adjustments up or down to keep you in the fight!

Hold strong, but check in at 0.25, 0.5, and 0.75 in! When you get to 0.9 with only a bit to go, don’t think about it too hard, just bump your speed and GO! This is a max effort test!!

You’ve trained for this. You’re ready! Do your best and forget the rest! Now, do it!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #runner #running #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #mile #orangetheoryfitness #otf #tallguys

coaching_cues:
YOUR BEST 500M FORMULA:

You must row “LONG AND STRONG” the entire way
That means you must get your shoulders in front of you at the front of the stroke
You need to be aware of when it happens and be ready to FIGHT TO THE END

videos.description:
YOUR BEST 500M FORMULA:

You must row “LONG AND STRONG” the entire way! This is a long sprint, so short choppy strokes are going to burn you out while you still have more to go!

To row LONG, you have to LEAN and SWING! That means you must get your shoulders in front of you at the front of the stroke. Then, push back and SWING into a “shoulders behind hips” position!

The hip swing is what keeps the watts high and keeps you going strong... DO NOT LET YOUR TORSO GO VERTICAL WHEN YOU ARE TIRED!!

At 200m to go, you will hit “The Wall”. No matter how fit you are, everyone hits it! You need to be aware of when it happens and be ready to FIGHT TO THE END!

In that last 150 meters, increase the stroke rate, keep the lean and swing, and don’t let your handle flow around... feed it STRAIGHT IN and OUT of the rower!

With all of this in mind, you’re going to dominate and crush the 500m! Good luck and let me know how your row goes!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rowingmachine #rower #erg #row #rowingrelated #500m #orangetheoryfitness #tallfitness #veganfitness

exercise_name: Your FOOT HEIGHT on the ROWER matters more than you think
coaching_cues: Most people don’t care or think about this at all, but you NEED TO CARE
Where SHOULD you set your feet on the rower? As low as comfortably possible

videos.description:
Your FOOT HEIGHT on the ROWER matters more than you think!

Most people don’t care or think about this at all, but you NEED TO CARE!

Why? Because when your feet are set too high up, it makes it harder to lean forward at the front of the rowing stroke without rounding your lower back. This means less power and a greater risk of injury!!

Where SHOULD you set your feet on the rower? As low as comfortably possible. That’s the magic phrase! The lower, the better - but obviously you don’t want your feet flying out of the rower! So, set them as low as you can while still feeling confident and comfortable!

This will make it so much easier to lean forward safely when you row! That means LESS injury and MORE power in every stroke!

Give this advice a try and let me know if it worked for you!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rowingmachine #rower #rowingrelated #erg #rowingform #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #veganfitness

exercise_name: Your rowing form is BAD because you’re trying to “pull” the stroke
coaching_cues: That’s how you want to initiate every stroke

videos.description:
Your rowing form is BAD because you’re trying to “pull” the stroke! You only PULL when you row when you’re doing bodybuilding training on weighted machines.

Rowing machines simulate the SPORT of ROWING, which is totally different than “pull rowing”!

The rowing stroke is a PUSH! That’s how you want to initiate every stroke.

Think of the full stroke as a “push, swing, pull”! PUSH your legs first, then SWING the hips/body back, then PULL the arms in!

But that initial starting PUSH is sooo important! Without it, you have no power in the SWING!

So do your best to be extra mindful to hold your torso forward as you PUSH instead of pull, then swing, then pull! Do this and your rowing performance is gonna go through the roof!!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rower #rowingmachine #erg #rowingrelated #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit #tallfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: You’re not sneaky… I see those hands
coaching_cues:
You just need to build your abs strength with a slight modification…

GOOD THING YOUR LEGS CAN BEND AND PHYSICS EXISTS

videos.description:
You’re not sneaky… I see those hands!!

Placing your hands under you is a COMMON THING when doing Leg Raises.

But why do we do this? To reduce the challenge our abs have to face to stabilize our spine when lifting our legs!

If your lower back hurts if you don’t put your hands under you, it means that your LEGS ARE TOO HEAVY FOR YOUR ABS when your legs are straight!

That’s not a diss to your legs! You just need to build your abs strength with a slight modification…

GOOD THING YOUR LEGS CAN BEND AND PHYSICS EXISTS!! Because the more you bend your legs, the less of a challenge the Leg Raise becomes! That means you can bend your legs as much as you need to, still do the leg raise, and know that your body is building strength!

And over time, you can practice getting your legs straighter and straighter! Remember, you have your ENTIRE LIFE to get “good” or “strong” at anything - but you have to give yourself a chance to regress and build strength properly without relying on crutches like “hands under you”!

Bend your legs, do your reps, and trust you’ll build strength over time to straighten your legs if you stay consistent and practice!

Hope that helps! You got this!!

Love always,

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#abs #absworkout #abworkout #core #coreworkout #homeworkout #legraise #lowerabs #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: You’re NOT working your glutes AT ALL if you’re doing Hip Hinge Swings…
coaching_cues: This is a MENTAL HURDLE you have to get over if you actually want to see results from your dumbbell or kettlebell swings
You NEED to pick a dumbbell that would feel IMPOSSIBLE to lift up with your arms

videos.description:
You’re NOT working your glutes AT ALL if you’re doing Hip Hinge Swings…

With a weight that “feels heavy in your arms”!!

This is a MENTAL HURDLE you have to get over if you actually want to see results from your dumbbell or kettlebell swings!

You NEED to pick a dumbbell that would feel IMPOSSIBLE to lift up with your arms!

But here’s the GOOD NEWS: you don’t have to lift with your arms at all! And you SHOULDN’T BE either! All you have to do is HOLD the weight… and you’re capable of holding A LOT of weight!

This is your reminder to get out of your own head… to stop telling yourself “you can’t”… and to get after it with this one!

A 25 pound dumbbell is the BARE MINIMUM if you’re healthy for this exercise and instructed properly!

Hope this can motivate you today! NOW, DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#kettlebell #glute #glutes #glutesworkout #gluteworkout #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #fitnesstips #homeworkouttips #tallguys

exercise_name: You’re PROBABLY not as bad at sit-ups as you think you are…
coaching_cues: You’re stronger than you think, you just need to realize there are OPTIONS for you out there

videos.description:
You’re PROBABLY not as bad at sit-ups as you think you are…

You may just not have realized that the MORE you bend your legs, the HARDER sit-ups are.

Which also means that the LESS you bend them and the more you straighten them out, the EASIER sit-ups are!

The amount that you bend your knees is a great way to scale sit-ups to work for you! Keep in mind, if you REALLY want to build some serious abs strength from sit-ups it’s really important that you contract HARD and lower down as slowly as you can each rep. That’s what we call “eccentric” strength - and it is one of the best things you can do to develop your core strength if you’re finding it to be particularly weak!

You’re stronger than you think, you just need to realize there are OPTIONS for you out there! Hope this helps. Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#abs #absworkout #abworkout #core #coreworkout #coreexercise #sixpack #sixpackabs #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: You’re probably not going low enough with lunges…
coaching_cues: If that’s too much, you need to go full body weight
We NEVER want to work through pain… but your joints will ONLY get stronger when you train their full range at your current regression level

videos.description:
You’re probably not going low enough with lunges…

The gold standard is dropping your back knee just a little bit above touching the ground! If you’re going any shallower than that, you’re missing out and cheating the reps!

However, going that low can cause PAIN for a lot of folks.

The solution? Use a lighter weight. If that’s too much, you need to go full body weight! And if THAT is still causing pain, it’s time to grab some TRX suspension straps!!

The TRX allow you to offset up to 99% of your body weight into your arms to make it easier on your legs - as much as you need to for pain-free movement!

We NEVER want to work through pain… but your joints will ONLY get stronger when you train their full range at your current regression level. You have your ENTIRE LIFE to build up and get strong, but you have to give yourself a chance by dropping your ego, lightening the load, and doing it RIGHT!

Hope this can resonate!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#mobility #legs #legday #legsworkout #lunges #orangetheoryfitness #tipoftheday #tallguys #veganfitness

exercise_name: “Choose your character” OTF Single Arm Snatch Edition

videos.description: “Choose your character” OTF Single Arm Snatch Edition  #otf #coach #otfcoach #chooseyourcharacter #otfedition #orangetheory #orangetheorycoach #singlearmsnatch #snatch #exercise #funny

exercise_name: “I don’t get tired when I row
coaching_cues: This takes body CONTROL and practice

videos.description:
“I don’t get tired when I row!”… “I can’t get my heart rate up!”

ROWING IS EASY IF YOU DON’T ROW HARD:

If you want to get your heart rate up when you row and “feel it”, you have to put more EFFORT into each stroke!

Think of it like weight lifting!! You don’t do bicep curls up and down fast with 2 lbs in each hand right?? So why would you bounce up and down on the rower with light strokes?

See the comparison?

If you treat each stroke like you’re trying to LIFT A HEAVY WEIGHT each stroke, you’re going to feel it work! 💪 The trick is to row HARD on the at back, but then come UP SLOW!!

This takes body CONTROL and practice!

Just realize that “figuring out rowing” takes EFFORT! So don’t just mindlessly row and expect things to work.. you gotta bring the focus and the HARD STROKES!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowingrelated #erg #concept2 @concept2inc #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit

exercise_name: “Plank Pop” is simply an inaccessible exercise for a majority of grown adults…
coaching_cues: Power and athleticism is something we want to maintain throughout our lives to keep us young

videos.description:
“Plank Pop” is simply an inaccessible exercise for a majority of grown adults…

That is, at least without knowing how to properly regress the movement!

This common Orangetheory exercise can cause most to jump up and land on the toes with feet close - basically losing the effect of the deep squat position you want to aim for during this exercise.

Your best friend in this case is A BENCH or CHAIR! Something where you can elevate your hands and your plank position. Do this, and the demands on the squat depth are reduced - allowing you to ACTUALLY work on your squat depth from a level that works for you!

But how do you get better and increase your range? Because it’s not just gonna “happen”…

You add a PAUSE in each rep. An active, contracted pause position for 1-2 seconds holding the squat as low and as hard as you can! Over time, your mobility will increase!

This allows you to eventually work towards lowering the surface (like a low bench) and one day be able to successfully rep it out on the ground!

Power and athleticism is something we want to maintain throughout our lives to keep us young. But that doesn’t mean we need to fall victim to exercises we just aren’t ready for - so, regress to an elevated surface and work on those active pauses! You’ll get there! 🤙

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#power #agility #stayyoung #powertraining #calisthenics #calisthenicsworkout #mayhem #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: “Punch your arms out” 🥊 is a common cue you’ll incorrectly hear from coaches

videos.description:
“Punch your arms out” 🥊 is a common cue you’ll incorrectly hear from coaches.

❌ This isn’t a great cue because it makes the rowing stroke feel more robotic and overuses the arm and shoulder muscles.

✅ Instead, “relax your arms down and out”. This will keep the tension away from your shoulders, smooth out your “arms-body-legs” sequencing, and will make passing your knees before bending them much easier!

😉 Just make sure you steadily lift your hands up AFTER passing the knees to not end up at the catch with your hands low!

👍 The path of the handle is DYNAMIC. It’s not “straight in and straight out” of the rower.

Hope this helps! I have hundreds of follow along rowing workouts when I coach all of this! 💪

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingispassion #rowingmachine #erg #rowingworkout #rowingform #crossfit #hyrox #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: “Step Ups” are one of the most CHEATED exercises out there

videos.description:
“Step Ups” are one of the most CHEATED exercises out there!

Almost everyone (myself included) tends to give a subconscious push off from the back foot as we do step ups.

This happens even MORE when we start adding weights to the matter! Sometimes we feel like our arms are the limiting factor, but in reality, we aren’t putting ENOUGH emphasis in our front working leg!

✅ Next time you do step ups, try to see how much weight you can possibly put onto the bench leg while lifting the back leg as GENTLY as possible off of the ground. You will find right away that the step up is wayyyyyy harder than you originally thought it was!

Intention, my friends. Bring intention to the step up an watch just how hard light weights can be for this exercise! 💪

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#fitnesstips #workouttips #coach #fitnesscoach #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: “Strap across the widest part of your shoe” IS BAD & OUTDATED ADVICE
coaching_cues: If you want to actually be a good rower (I’m looking at you especially, short folks), these kind of adjustments MATTER

videos.description:
“Strap across the widest part of your shoe” IS BAD & OUTDATED ADVICE!!

Why? Because your feet are STILL TOO HIGH on the rower! This is BAD because the higher your feet are, the more your knees and thighs get in the way from letting you get a proper lean forward without risking injury to your lower back!

A BETTER cue to use is to strap in “as low as comfortably possible”. The lower, the better. Because this brings your knees down, making it easier to row long and row safely.

If you feel like your feet are gonna come out because you’re so low, YOU’RE NOT AS LOW AS COMFORTABLY POSSIBLE. YOU ARE TOO LOW, SO GO UP A BIT!

If you want to actually be a good rower (I’m looking at you especially, short folks), these kind of adjustments MATTER! So don’t just strap in with outdated nonsense advice… take matters into your own hands and strap in as low as comfortably possible when you row!

Now do it!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rower #rowingmachine #erg #rowingrelated #rowinglife #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit #tallfitness #tallguys

“Strap across the widest part of your shoe” is BAD ADVICE!!

It’s not the worst piece of rowing advice I’ve ever heard, but it’s not even close to optimal advice.

And that’s because the higher up your feet sit in the footplates on the rower, the higher your legs are, and the more difficult it is to lean forward safely and get maximum length each stroke.

That’s why I ALWAYS suggest to instead to strap in your feet “as low as comfortably possible”. THIS encourages you to set the footplates as low as you can without feeling like your feet are going to come out!

Because the lower your feet sit in the rower, the EASIER it is to keep better posture at the front of the stroke and the MORE length you’ll get each stroke!

Seems minor, I know, but damn… It makes a MASSIVE difference in your rowing! So, before you give up on rowing because you hate it so much - just TRY THIS and see if it helps make your rowing a little bit more enjoyable!

Now, do it!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowingworkout #rowingrelated #rowingclub #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: “TIP OF THE DAY” IS BACK BABY
coaching_cues: Advice you NEED if you want to run well
It’s time to stop putting your energy and focus on “how you should land” when you run
Your focus should be on: pulling your foot up under you, switching your pose leg, and doing so as quickly as you can

videos.description:
“TIP OF THE DAY” IS BACK BABY!!

Thanks for being patient with me over these last few weeks as I took some time off of making content! 🙏

We’re coming in HOT with some treadmill/running advice today! Advice you NEED if you want to run well!

It’s time to stop putting your energy and focus on “how you should land” when you run! If you’re THINKING about landing on the toes or the midfoot or the heel, you’re putting energy into the wrong thing!

Instead, think about your pose & fall form! The position that you do EVERY SINGLE STEP when you run!

Your focus should be on: pulling your foot up under you, switching your pose leg, and doing so as quickly as you can! The goal is to spend as little time on the ground as possible!

Then, you just FALL as you do this! That’s running! Don’t believe me? Watch the slow-mo clip at 12 MPH towards the end of the video! We ALL pose, switch, and fall! When this is your focus, your running gets a heck of a lot better! 💪

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #runner #running #treadmill #treadmillworkout #marathonmonth #otf #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness #tallguys

“Zipping an Oversized Coat!”

That’s one of my best cues for a dumbbell snatch! This cue helps you to keep the weight close to your body as it goes overhead - rather than swinging out in front of you!

What really makes this work though is thinking about using a “jump” to start the “zip”!

We want to avoid using our arm to try to muscle the weight overhead… instead, use your leg power to “jump” the legs to generate momentum for the weight to zip up!

Hope this cue can help you feel more confident with this exercise! 💪 NOW, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#power #snatch #powerlifting #speed #crossfit #fitnesstips #workouttips #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues:
⚠️

You MUST check how you’re sitting on the rower before you take your first stroke

videos.description:
⚠️ BEFORE YOU ROW, CHECK THIS!! ⚠️

You MUST check how you’re sitting on the rower before you take your first stroke!

Because if you’re sitting without thinking, you’re gonna sit where it’s comfy… aka your squishy buttcheeks!!

When you do this, it causes your lower back and hips to tuck under you, making it extremely difficult to lean and reach forward at the front of the rowing stroke AND increases the odds of a lower back strain!

If your back hurts on the rower, a lot of the time THIS IS WHY!!

So, check how you sit! And make sure you “untuck” your lower back and sit more on your hamstrings than your glutes! It’ll make it so much easier to get a good lean forward while keeping your back safe! ✅ NOW DO IT!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#rowing #rowingmachine #rower #row #rowingworkout #orangetheory #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: ⚠️ BURPEE DEBATE: Push-up or no push-up

videos.description:
⚠️ BURPEE DEBATE: Push-up or no push-up?

You might be surprised to find out that the push-up is OPTIONAL! Google it!! Just because your CrossFit coach told you it didn’t count without the push-up doesn’t mean they were correct!

Regardless if the pushup WAS required or not, the burpee without it is a great cardio alternative to while training a fairly functional movement! Just because something doesn’t “build muscle” doesn’t mean it’s a bad exercise!

✅ When you do your burpees, pushup or not, don’t forget to keep your feet WIDE! This allows you to athletically squat down each rep, rather than round and flop down if your feet were close! Gamechanger for sure!

What are your thoughts on this burpee debate?? Either way… now, do it!!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#burpee #burpeechallenge #burpees #cardio #homeworkout #orangetheoryfitness #plantpower

coaching_cues:
How SHOULD you run then??

STEP 1: find your pose
NOT a knee drive
Keep this in mind to TRULY elevate your running form and keep you efficient and injury free

videos.description:
⚠️ DOES IT HURT TO RUN??

If it does, you’re probably OVER-STRIDING! And this happens when we try to push off of our back foot when we run!

If you push off of your back foot each stride, you have NO CHOICE but to slam out in front of you… which slows you down and puts a ton of negative impact on your joints! It’s simple BAD FORM!!

How SHOULD you run then??

STEP 1: find your pose. This means “pull” your foot up under you. With as little effort as possible!! NOT a knee drive. NOT a butt kick. And NOTHING NUTTY!!! Just a light “pull” of your foot.

STEP 2: Switch your feet. Pull, and switch… back and forth! Again, with as little effort as possible!!

STEP 3: FALL forward with gravity as you switch your feet! Don’t hinge at your hips! Let your WHOLE BODY fall forward as you switch your pull!

THAT IS LITERALLY RUNNING.

This works at 1 mph… 4 mph… 10 mph… 15 mph! It’s all the same!!

Keep this in mind to TRULY elevate your running form and keep you efficient and injury free!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #runner #running #treadmill #treadmillworkout #runningmotivation #runningform #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: ⚠️ How to RUN Without Knee/Hip/Ankle Pain
coaching_cues: But we also don’t want to try to “land on the toes”

videos.description:
⚠️ How to Run Without Knee/Hip/Ankle Pain!

First off, did anyone ever teach you how to run?? For 99% of people, the answer is no! And YES, there is TECHNIQUE to running just like with rowing, cycling, lifting weights, and… EVERYTHING regarding exercise!!

The source of most pain from running comes from crappy form that results from super supportive and squishy shoes! These shoes allow us to run like crap for a while and develop bad habits!

The main bad habit being: striding “out in front of you” and striking down with your heels every step! Ever try standing and jumping and landing on your heels? YOOOOOUUUUCHH!!

But we also don’t want to try to “land on the toes”! This is JUST AS BAD!!

The solution? Learning to run. Here’s the steps:

1️⃣ Find your “pose”. That happens when you simply pull your foot under you with as little effort as possible.

2️⃣ Switch to your other foot and find your pose there. Practice switching back and forth with as LITTLE EFFORT as possible.

3️⃣ Fall with gravity while doing the pose switch.

Those are literally the 3 steps. And with enough practice, it’ll feel more natural. You can literally start doing this at 1 mph and build up to ANY speed! When you go faster, you’ll NATURALLY pull your feet higher and won’t have to think about it!

Were you good at weights the first time you lifted? Were you good at rowing the first time? Were you good AT ANYTHING the first time you tried?? Of course you weren’t. And this is no different. Be patient and practice!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#running #runningform #runner #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #treadmill #orangetheoryfitness
