coaching_cues: ✅ You need to find a damper setting that works for you, which for most is in the 3-6 range on the dial
So, if rowing feels too easy, drive back HARDER each stroke - and don’t just crank the damper setting up to 10

videos.description:
⚠️ MYTH BUSTED: Rowing “Resistance”

As you can see in this video, whether the resistance setting (actually called ‘the damper’) is set to 1 or 10, you can STILL achieve the same split/500m.

Granted, it definitely does FEEL LIGHTER at a lower damper setting! It was a bit more difficult to find a rhythm with it, but that’s because I’m 6ft8 and 225 pounds! If you’re shorter and smaller, you’ll have a bit of an easier time with a lower damper as it will allow you rely more on stroke rate than pure power per stroke!

The point is, just because the “resistance” is lower, doesn’t mean your workout is objectively easier or you can’t go as hard!

✅ You need to find a damper setting that works for you, which for most is in the 3-6 range on the dial! But remember that your workout on the rower is mainly determined by how HARD you take your strokes!

So, if rowing feels too easy, drive back HARDER each stroke - and don’t just crank the damper setting up to 10! 😉

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #hyrox #orangetheoryfitness #rowingrelated

⚠️ Split Squats are the HARDEST!! But here’s how to make them easier…

✅ It all starts with a confident set up! No more flailing my around and losing balance!! Try this: sit on the bench, extending your leg out, and stand up. Put your back foot on the bench, and BOOM, you’re ready to rock! 🤘

✅ Second, DITCH THE DUMBBELLS!! That’s right, the Split Squat doesn’t need weights for most people, especially if you really struggle with this! By going bodyweight only, you can work a greater range of motion, which I PROMISE is going to get you more results than the half-range you’ll get from holding weights from the start!

✅ To get low, think about “dropping the back knee”. If you try to bend the front leg to get down, you’re screwed!! Drop the back knee ALL THE WAY, then push through the front leg! That’s the secret to depth, balance, and QUALITY reps!

Turns out that with all of this in mind, it still sucks!! It will always be a tough one.. but hopefully these tips help! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#splitsquat #splitsquats #bulgarians #legday #legworkout #legsworkout #glutes #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: ⚠️ You CAN’t Do a Single Sit-Up
coaching_cues: Wow you must be ashamed

videos.description:
⚠️ You CAN’t Do a Single Sit-Up?!

Wow you must be ashamed!

… that’s what your inner monologue might be telling you. But it’s making you feel bad for no reason!

👍 First off, don’t forget that we all start somewhere! And nobody is instantly a master at everything they try… if you’re not “good” at sit-ups, you’re not alone!

👊 But that also means it’s time to get to work, the smart way! Did you know that your LEGS influence how hard sit-ups are?? The more you bend your legs, the HARDER sit-ups become!

That means if you’re struggling, try STRAIGHT LEG sit-ups! These are much easier to do than with your legs bent. If you still can’t get up, it’s time to use the arms to help you up! But then, on the way down, brace your abs, round your spine, and SLOWLY lower down to build “eccentric strength”, which will translate to you getting better at sit-ups week to week!

✅ EVERYTHING can be scaled down to meet your fitness level, and there is always a systematic, step-by-step approach to getting “good” at anything in the fitness world! So stop being so hard on yourself and let’s get to work!! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#situp #situps #crunches #abs #absworkout #abstraining #core #coreworkout #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: ✅ 2000m Row Strategy for your next PR
coaching_cues: You must remember that the 2000m row is like “the mile” 🏃‍♂️ of rowing

videos.description:
✅ 2000m Row Strategy for your next PR!

Most people FAIL the 2k because they burn out the first 500 meters! You must remember that the 2000m row is like “the mile” 🏃‍♂️ of rowing! It’s actually LONGER than a mile!

And you don’t just SPRINT the start of a mile!! You pace yourself - and that’s what you’ve gotta do on this row!

Pacing yourself means finding a balance of stroke rate and power per stroke! You don’t want the rate too low - so aim for 26 SPM+ (32 SPM+ for the pros) while maintaining a MODERATE power output (NOT a max effort power per stroke)!

You do this, and the first 1000m might feel suspiciously easy! But that’s not a bad thing.. because you can always BRING MORE at the end, but you can’t get back burning the start! 🔥

If you want a true Personal Best / Personal Record (PR/PB), then use the Erg Pace Calculator (google it) to get you an accurate pace/500m to aim for based on your goal time!

Happy rowing! 🚣

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rowing #rower #rowingmachine #erg #crossfit #2k #2krow #rowingrelated #rowingclub #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: Keep your heels down with a shorter leg compression to allow you to get a really high stroke rate

videos.description:
✅ 3 Tips to Row Fast (Sprints)

1️⃣ Heels planted. Keep your heels down with a shorter leg compression to allow you to get a really high stroke rate!

2️⃣ Push FIRST… then swing! Don’t fall into the trap of leaning back and pulling first! This is more common than you think!

3️⃣ Keep your hands high. When your stroke is shorter, if you don’t lift your hands up a bit higher than usual, there will be a ton of slack which will destroy your ability to generate power!

💪 Hope these help!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #rowingispassion #concept2 #rowingworkout #rowingtips #rowingform #orangetheoryfitness #crossfit #hyrox

exercise_name: ✅ Bring your elbows forward a bit…

videos.description:
✅ Bring your elbows forward a bit…

That’s the easiest and most simple cue to help you get more comfort, range, and confidence with the Shoulder Press.

Some will say elbows out to the sides is fine… you do you I guess. But if you’re looking for simplicity and the lowest risk of injury, this is the way! 💪 NOW, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#shoulder #shoulders #shoulderworkout #pushday #chestday #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: ✅ Hold the weight on your upper chest during Torso Rotations / Russian Twists
coaching_cues: 👍 Lock the weight on your torso and twist as slow and as far as you can - and you’ll feel a HUGE difference in your abs / core engagement

videos.description:
✅ Hold the weight on your upper chest during Torso Rotations / Russian Twists!

This takes the action out of your arms and into your core!

When the weight isn’t on your body, your arms take over, and you end up not getting as much rotation as you think you’re getting!

👍 Lock the weight on your torso and twist as slow and as far as you can - and you’ll feel a HUGE difference in your abs / core engagement!

Build a stronger set of abs and core by making this simple switch! 💪 Now, do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#abs #abworkout #absworkout #core #coreworkout #sixpack #fatloss #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: ✅ The SWEET SPOT for Chest Press
coaching_cues: Keep this little tip in mind when you’re crushing your reps

videos.description:
✅ The SWEET SPOT for Chest Press!

It’s not “elbows tucked in” and it’s not “elbows out to the sides”!

The SWEET SPOT is simply “slightly forward” from the “elbows out position”!

Sure, you can do a 45 degree angle - that works! But even if it not even that drastic, just elbows SLIGHTLY forward is all you need to protect your shoulders, get full range of motion, and feel confident!

Keep this little tip in mind when you’re crushing your reps! 💪 🔥

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#chestpress #chestday #chestworkout #benchpress #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: Not a reach, not a squat — just back that thing up until you feel the stretch, then squeeze it forward

videos.description:
❌ Back pain during Deadlifts? Confused on form?

💫 STELLAR DEADLIFTS IN 30 SECONDS!

The only cue you need? Butt. Back!

Not a reach, not a squat — just back that thing up until you feel the stretch, then squeeze it forward.

Slight bend in the knees, core braced, and “butt back”, baby! This is how I like to think of “the hinge” which is BASICALLY what this is!!

Hinge is too fancy of a word... that’s why I like “butt back”! But they are both the same thing!

Now, go BACK THAT THANG UP!! 💪 💃 🍑

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#deadliftform #hiphinge #glutetraining #formfix #fitnessreels #deadliftday #orangetheoryfitness #deadlifts #deadlift

coaching_cues: 💨 It controls how much air goes into the system as you row

videos.description:
❌ IT’S NOT A “RESISTANCE” SETTING!

That dial on the side of the @concept2inc RowErg? It’s called the “damper setting”!

💨 It controls how much air goes into the system as you row!

🔟 When the dial is up at 10, it’s allowing MORE air to flow, which slows down the fan faster! This can make the start of each stroke “feel heavier”.

1️⃣ When the dial is at 1, it’s allowing LESS air to flow, which keeps the fan spinning for longer. This can make the start of each stroke feel more “light and airy”!

❌ However, neither setting determines HOW HARD THE WORKOUT WILL BE!!

✅ The challenge of a rowing workout is based on how much EFFORT you put into each stroke. You can row LIGHTLY at damper 10 and feel nothing.. or you can row as HARD AS POSSIBLE at damper 1 and be cooked in a couple minutes!! 🔥

👍 That’s why setting 3-5 works for most people, for most workouts! Gives you a good balance with how fast the fan slows down and allows you to row hard without making each stroke super heavy feeling!

Who woulda thought!! Now ya know!! 😃 🤘 Questions? Let me know!

Coach Austin
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #rowingworkout #rowingrelated #hyrox #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #rowingispassion

❌ Rowing is NOT “LEGS - CORE - ARMS”.
This common misconception is what leads to one of the biggest problems for those trying to use the rower correctly…

And that problem is rowing like A ROBOT 🤖

All of the tension in the shoulders… the awkward hip hinge… it all sucks.

Rowing is FLUID! 🌊 And when we think of it instead as a “push, swing, pull”, we can better find this fluid flow and make our rowing feel strong, confident, and sustainable - rather than just pain awkward and uncomfortable!

Without getting too technical, this small mindset shift makes a BIG difference in your rowing! Give it a shot! 🤙

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowingispassion #rowingmachine #rowingmachineworkout #erg #hyrox #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #rowingform #rowingrelated

coaching_cues: ⭐️ To SPRINT on the rower, you need to row differently than you would “normally”
The lean forward should have your torso on your thighs and arms stretched out
As you take strokes, drive your legs, and SWING back with power each stroke

videos.description:
⭐️ 200m TECHNIQUE! ⭐️ To SPRINT on the rower, you need to row differently than you would “normally”!

Short rows like 200m sprints take such a short amount of time, it is BETTER to take MORE strokes faster, rather than take long efficient strokes!

This means that to optimize your form:

1️⃣ Keep your heels planted. This allows you to take strokes quickly and with power!

2️⃣ Focus on your LEAN forward! The lean forward should have your torso on your thighs and arms stretched out. As you take strokes, drive your legs, and SWING back with power each stroke. Don’t forget to have some urgency to rush out and lean to the front again!

3️⃣ Lift your hands SLIGHTLY higher than normal. Handles are pulled out of rowing machines at a slight angle upwards, and because your stroke is shorter, you need to lift UP just a bit more than you think in order to feel strong and connected!

Do these things and you’ll be FAST! Don’t do them and you will be slow 🤷‍♂️ no matter how fit you think you are!

If you don’t believe me on any of this, go ahead and search “200m world record row” and watch all the attempts that use this EXACT formula!
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #rowingrelated #erg #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #hyrox

coaching_cues: That means your speed should be MINIMUM 2+ mph over your MILE SPEED

videos.description:
🍊 The NEW Quarter Mile Benchmark!

This is gonna be a fun one! What’s the best strategy for running a quarter mile (0.25) as fast as possible?

Well, think of it like a “1.5-2 minute All Out”! That means your speed should be MINIMUM 2+ mph over your MILE SPEED! Use a mile time to pace calculator to find the speed and then add +2mph to it! That’s a good place to start, but you might need more, so be ready to adjust!

This one is gonna be over before you know it, so be ready to pump those arms to help you keep your cadence high and pick up those feet!!

Thankfully, everyone gets a PR the first time ;) give it your best shot and let me know how it goes!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #running #runner #runningform #treadmill #treadmillworkout #sprint #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: 💀 You’re dying on the rower because you’re treating it like a treadmill
coaching_cues: 🤷‍♂️ Control your body
Drive back hard, but have the body awareness to not RUSH back up… just take your time before the next stroke

videos.description:
💀 You’re dying on the rower because you’re treating it like a treadmill.

It’s like you’re pressing 15 mph, realizing you can’t, and then stop. You’d never do that while running - so you press a button that gives you a sustainable speed instead.

But on the rower there is NO SPEED BUTTON. You make the speed. And you’re simply gripping and ripping max effort and that’s why it feels impossible to row for more than a few minutes.

✅ Try “1 second back, 3 seconds up”. This is equivalent to “pressing a button that’s lets you run at a sustainable speed”.

🤷‍♂️ Control your body. Drive back hard, but have the body awareness to not RUSH back up… just take your time before the next stroke! Makes a world of difference and makes rowing fun and sustainable! 💪 Now, do it!
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg

💥 The Force Curve!

This handy graph can help you better understand where your force is being distributed each stroke!

There are plenty of examples to share for this, but here are the 3 I chose, all revolving around rowing at a steady pace between 22-25 SPM. These graphs and what’s ideal would look different for sprints, just FYI!

Example 1 shows what the curve looks like when you overuse your arms in the stroke. You see a big peak/spike happen towards the end of the line during the arm pull. A tell-all sign that you’re putting too much effort into your arms compared to your legs and hips!

Example 2 shows an “early layback”, which is when you negate the entire hip swing portion of the rowing stroke and only rely on the leg drive and arm pull. This is evidenced by a steady climbing-upwards with it peaking towards the back half of the stroke!

Example 3 shows us what a good proper stroke graph should be at this steady rate, a smooth hill with a peak that’s more towards the middle of the stroke - the point right after the leg drive and your torso swings through vertical!

When you’re rowing steady, aim for this “smooth hill” graph to show that you’re getting proper power distribution from your body efficiently each stroke! 💪

Wanna see more examples? 🤔

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingmachine #erg #rowingispassion #rowingworkout #forcecurve #power #hyrox #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness #concept2

💥Shoulder PAIN during Upright Rows…

This exercise is really uncomfortable for many.

If you struggle with pain (the bad kind) during this exercise, try to pull a little bit wider than you think you should! For some, this can make the lift feel better!

If that’s not enough, try to hinge forward JUST A LITTLE BIT!!! Like a couple inches!!!

There is no hard RULE that you need to be perfectly vertical with your body as you pull up… and this slight hinge forward might be just what you need to lift pain-free.

When it comes to building muscle, being able to load HEAVIER and push HARDER is more important than pure movement optimization based on an arbitrary rule book.

If it feels better, you can push harder! And that’s the big takeaway!

Hope this helps! Tip of the Day videos are BACK BABY!!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#shoulder #shoulders #row #rows #uprightrow #weights #fitnesstips #workouttips #otf #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: 🔥 Comment “challenge” for more info on my Run Challenge starting Monday
coaching_cues: Big squishy heels also prevent you from getting the ground feedback you SHOULD get when you run
Avoid shoes that push your toes together (also known as a “narrow toe box”)
Avoid shoes with an elevated heel

videos.description:
🔥 Comment “challenge” for more info on my Run Challenge starting Monday!

What running shoes should you buy?

There’s literally so many options… there MUST be some important things to look for that make shoes different and better / worse for your running!

1. Avoid shoes that push your toes together (also known as a “narrow toe box”). This is UNFORTUNATELY most shoe brands… sure it looks fashionable to make the shoe narrow, but it prevents you feet from working to support good run form.

- Opt for shoes with a “wide toe box”!

2. Avoid shoes with an elevated heel. This contorts your feet away from their natural shape and function! Big squishy heels also prevent you from getting the ground feedback you SHOULD get when you run!

- Opt for ZERO DROP shoes that have an even level from front to back to keep natural foot shape!

Minimalist shoes like @xeroshoes , @vivobarefoot , @vibramfivefingers all have wide toe box and are as zero drop as it gets!

Shoes from @altrarunning are your BEST CHOICE if you want more cushion if you’re newer to running or overweight and need extra support. Lots of zero drop options from them!!

What running shoes do you like? Let me know down below!

Love always,

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#running #runningshoes #runningshoe #runningmotivation #runningcommunity #jogging #powerwalking #tallfitness #tallguys

exercise_name: 🔥 FAST Sprints on the rower require SPECIAL technique
coaching_cues: ✅ Peak power comes from the hip swing after the leg drive

videos.description:
🔥 FAST Sprints on the rower require SPECIAL technique!

This is NOT the technique you’d use for any row longer than 2 minutes max! These tips are focused on peaking power for 10-30ish second sprints primarily!

🔥 Tip 1: don’t bend your legs up all the way!

✅ Focus on heels down, with about a 90 degree leg bend!

🔥 Tip 2: focus on MORE lean at the front!

✅ Peak power comes from the hip swing after the leg drive. Without a lot of lean forward, you’re gonna miss out on power! Think “11 to 1 o’clock” with your body!

🔥 Tip 3: don’t fall off the seat!

✅ Apply your force ONLY horizontally and try to “sit heavy” in the seat!

💪 Combine these tips with aggression and a high stroke rate, and you’re set to CRUSH any row sprint that comes your way! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rower #rowing #rowingispassion #rowingmachine #erg #rowingworkout #cardio #sprints #hyrox #crossfit #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: 🔥 HOT TAKE: Hips back first during a squat isn’t a great cue
coaching_cues: If it hurts your knees when your knees go over your toes, you need to simply reduce the weight you use

videos.description:
🔥 HOT TAKE: Hips back first during a squat isn’t a great cue!!

And that’s because for many, it reduces range of motion, leads to requiring more out of your spine/back muscles, and for some…it creates a fear around “knees over toes”!

What’s a better cue? “Bend your knees and push them forward as you sit your hips back”! This helps you keep a taller posture, putting less strain on the spine while allowing you to achieve a greater depth each squat!
🤔 But… knees over toes???

✅ Knees over toes is NOT inherently bad! If it was, wouldn’t it be bad to GO DOWN STAIRS??!!

If it hurts your knees when your knees go over your toes, you need to simply reduce the weight you use. 

Still hurts? Use less weight.

Less weight still hurts? Go bodyweight.

Still hurts? Try a chair or TRX to offset weight from your legs!

There is SOME level where you can manage a squat with your knees pushing forward! Drop your ego and find it! 💪 You’ve got your entire life to get stronger!!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#squat #squats #legday legs #kneesovertoes #orangetheoryfitness #legworkout

coaching_cues:
🚣‍♀️ How FAST should you row? What’s a good stroke rate??

The problem with that question, is that it’s the wrong question to ask

videos.description:
🚣‍♀️ How FAST should you row? What’s a good stroke rate??

The problem with that question, is that it’s the wrong question to ask.

Because stroke rate is only a small part of the equation… and the rest of the equation is your “power per stroke”!

You can row with VERY LITTLE power per stroke at 50+ strokes per minute and not feel like you’re doing anything.

You can also row with FULL EFFORT power per stroke at 12 strokes per minute and be exhausted after just a couple minutes!

Your power per stroke matters WAY MORE than your stroke rate - so don’t get so focused on that number!

Generally, 18-24 SPM is great for most medium-long distance rowing efforts! Get in that range and bro by your focus on your power per stroke to really get the most out of your workouts!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#row #rowing #rower #rowingmachine #rowingmachineworkout #crossfit #erg #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: Keep your heels planted and legs bent ~90° to max out your lean + reach

videos.description:
🚨 Want a faster 500m row? Here are 3 must-know tips to crush it:

🔥 Tip 1: Dial in your split
Your 500m split shows EXACTLY how fast you’re going. Know your goal split, lock it in, and stay there.

🔥 Tip 2: Heels down. Legs at 90°. GO!
Keep your heels planted and legs bent ~90° to max out your lean + reach. This keeps your stroke rate high and allows you to stay intense throughout! 

🔥 Tip 3: Survive the final 150m
When your body wants to quit—DON’T sit up!
💥 Lean more. Swing more.
💥 Stay aggressive when it hurts the most.

⚠️ Bonus tip: Finish STRONG. One stroke early = a ruined time. You row until that screen says 0:00. Now, DO IT!

🎯 Drop your 500m PR and or goal below! 👇

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#JustRow #RowingTips #500mSprint #erg #rowingrelated #rowingmachine #rowing #IndoorRowing #FitnessChallenge #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: This helps control the motion and keeps things pain-free

videos.description:
🚨KNEE PAIN DURING LUNGES?🚨

If forward lunges make your cringe for your knees, try these tips:

🦵 Step BIGGER – Most people don’t step far enough, which causes that dreaded knee pain. A longer step = less knee shear = happier joints.

⬇️ Drop the BACK knee – Not the front. This helps control the motion and keeps things pain-free. Don’t get it twisted, though! Knees over toes is fine if it’s on purpose. But for sensitive knees, focus on more action from the back knee rather than the front!

💥 Ditch the weights – If you’re hurting, your body is waving a red flag. Start with bodyweight and rebuild smarter!

💪 Use support if bodyweight isn’t enough – TRX handles, a chair, or a bench can help unload your legs into your arms more and help you build confidence.

🧠 Instead of saying “I can’t”, ask “How can I?”
You’ve got your whole life to get stronger, baby! Let’s reverse-engineer it and make it work FOR YOU. 👊🔥

Tag someone who needs to hear this! 💯 Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#painfree #lunges #lunge #legday #legworkout #legsworkout #kneepain #kneerehab #orangetheoryfitness

coaching_cues: Remember, there is no rule that says you need to run for x-amount of time to be considered “a runner”
This always gives you a stable, active, and in control pace to go back to when we start feeling the challenge of the run
And you don’t even need to “go fast”

videos.description:
🚶‍♀️➡️ 🏃‍♀️ - Here’s how you do it!

Remember, there is no rule that says you need to run for x-amount of time to be considered “a runner”. Don’t get caught in your head thinking if you start running that you can’t stop!

✅ Use power-walking as your Base. This always gives you a stable, active, and in control pace to go back to when we start feeling the challenge of the run!

✅ Run short Push intervals. This is your chance to practice! And you don’t even need to “go fast”. Running is a FORM, not a speed! You can run at 1 mph… 8 mph… 15 mph… it’s all the same form!

✅ Be patient and confident! Trust that you putting in the practice WILL make you better! That’s the beauty of being a human and DOING THINGS! :) NOW, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#run #runner #running #runningform #treadmill

coaching_cues: That weight should just be HANGING in your arm

videos.description:
🤔 Is this UNHINGED????? 😂

In a never-ending attempt to try to help things “click” for folks when doing complex exercises, we have for you today: reproduction 😂

The Hip Hinge Swing aka the kettle bell swing or whatever you wanna call it! Well, it’s kind of like a 🐪!

The idea is that we stick our hips back and thrust RATHER than “squat down and lift our arm”!

That weight should just be HANGING in your arm! All you have to do is hold and let your glutes and hamstrings do the rest! 💪

You were made for this… LITERALLY!! Grab AT LEAST a 25 pound weight and keep this in mind! You’re stronger than you think!! NOW DO IT!!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#glute #glutes #glutesday #hips #workouttips #fitnesstips #legday #legworkout #legs #orangetheoryfitness #otf #orangetheory

exercise_name: 🤫 The REAL Secret to good-looking arms
coaching_cues: So if you’re trying to at least LOOK like you’ve got strong arms, triceps training should be a priority
Try to aim for AT LEAST your neck, but lower is better as long as you don’t arch your back

videos.description:
🤫 The REAL Secret to good-looking arms! 👀

It’s natural to assume that it’s all about the biceps, but the TRICEPS actually make up MOST of the size of your arm! So if you’re trying to at least LOOK like you’ve got strong arms, triceps training should be a priority!

What’s one of the best go-to triceps exercises? The Overhead Triceps Extension! All it takes is one dumbbell - I’d recommend 99% of people start with AT LEAST a 15 pound dumbbell!

Make a peephole with your hands, bring it overhead, and make sure to 🔑 TURN your elbows forward slightly! 👍 This little turn makes a big difference in how effective the exercise is!

Try to aim for AT LEAST your neck, but lower is better as long as you don’t arch your back!

Reps of 6-20 are the secret to the gains with this one! 💪 train your triceps, people!

Now , do it!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#triceps #tricep #armtraining #armworkout #armsworkout #weights #orangetheoryfitness

exercise_name: 🥱 The Snatch is a waste of time…

videos.description:
🥱 The Snatch is a waste of time…

At least for most 🤷‍♂️ especially in a group fitness environment. If it’s prescribed, most people simply opt for weight that is too easy to see any benefit from!

If you can lift your weight over your head, it’s not doing anything! That means most folks need a 20 lbs weight MINIMUM to use the proper muscles - which are your LEGS!

The idea is to JUMP the weight overhead! Using leg drive power to explode up (you don’t physically have to leave the ground), but use the momentum generated from your legs to “zip” the weight overhead - like you’re wearing an oversized jacket!

You need something super heavy in your hands.. it’s gonna feel uncomfortable, but that’s okay! Your LEGS are strong! And YOU are stronger than you think! Now, DO IT!

Coach Austin 🤘
.
.
.
#power #muscle #strength #snatch #weightlifting #orangetheoryfitness #fitnesstips #workouttips #ﬁtnessmotivation
